questions.stem: Rita ______ her dogs many times a day. That's why they're too fat.
questions.explain: 這句話說『Rita一天______她的狗好幾次，難怪牠們太胖了』。狗會變胖，通常是因為吃太多，餵食的英文是feed，所以答案選 A feeds。其他選項：kisses是親吻、walks是遛、washes是洗澡，都不會讓狗變胖。

questions.stem: You can ask Sophia anything about butterflies and bees. She has great ______ of insects.
questions.explain: 這句話說『你可以問Sophia任何關於蝴蝶和蜜蜂的問題，她對昆蟲有很豐富的______』。可以回答任何問題，代表她懂很多、有很多知識，英文叫knowledge，所以答案選 B knowledge。其他選項：chances是機會、news是新聞、power是力量，都跟『很懂昆蟲』沒關係。

questions.stem: Adam slept ______ last night because he kept waking up from bad dreams.
questions.explain: 這句話說『Adam昨晚睡得很______，因為他一直被惡夢吵醒』。一直被吵醒，代表睡得很不好、很糟糕，英文用terribly，所以答案選 C terribly。其他選項：deeply是睡得很沉（跟一直被吵醒矛盾）、early是早、quickly是快，都不合句意。

questions.stem: Tara enjoys being with all kinds of animals; she even ______ a snake as a pet.
questions.explain: 這句話說『Tara很喜歡跟各種動物相處，她甚至______一條蛇當寵物』。『養一隻動物當寵物』的固定講法是keep...as a pet，所以答案選 C keeps。其他選項不能這樣搭配使用。

questions.stem: Little Eric chose the doll ______ big brown eyes because it was the cutest in the store.
questions.explain: 這句話說『小Eric選了這個______一雙棕色大眼睛的娃娃，因為它是店裡最可愛的』。要形容娃娃『有一雙大眼睛』，英文用with，就像說the girl with long hair（有長頭髮的女孩）一樣，所以答案選 D with。

questions.stem: After four hours of mountain climbing, Rosa's legs got ______, so she took a rest on the top of the mountain.
questions.explain: 這句話說『爬了四小時的山之後，Rosa的腿變得很______，所以她在山頂休息』。爬山爬很久腿會痠痛，英文用sore，所以答案選 C sore。其他選項：dirty是髒、sick是生病、strong是強壯，都沒辦法接『所以要休息』。

questions.stem: Having three cups of coffee a day can't be bad for our health, ______?
questions.explain: 這句話說『一天喝三杯咖啡對健康應該不會不好吧，______？』。這種句尾的小問句叫附加問句，規則是前面是否定、後面就要用肯定；前面說can't（不會），所以後面要用肯定的can；而前面『喝三杯咖啡』整件事當作一件事來看，用it代替，所以答案選 A can it。

questions.stem: You can't push that door open with only one hand. It's too heavy. You have to use ______!
questions.explain: 這句話說『你不能只用一隻手把那扇門推開，它太重了，你必須用______』。一隻手不夠，要用『兩隻手』，英文用both（兩者都），所以答案選 C both。其他選項：all是全部、another是另一個、one是一個，都不合句意。

questions.stem: Alan works hard in his new job, and he wants to know ______ his boss feels about him.
questions.explain: 這句話說『Alan在新工作很努力，他想知道老闆對他______感覺』。問『感覺如何』的英文用how，所以答案選 A how。其他選項：that、whether、why，接在這裡意思都不對。

questions.stem: I thought the girl ______ was Kate, but it was her sister, Candy. They sounded almost the same.
questions.explain: 這句話說『我以為______的女生是Kate，結果是她妹妹Candy，她們聲音太像了』。這句話裡『那個接電話的女生』是用來補充說明是哪個女生，中間要用一個字把這段補充說明接上去，這個字用who，代表『那個……的人』，所以答案選 C who answered the phone。其他選項少了這個連接的字，或用法不對，接不起來。

questions.stem: Ken is going abroad with a woman he met online, though he doesn't know ______ well.
questions.explain: 這句話說『Ken要跟一個網路認識的女生出國，雖然他跟______還不熟』。前面已經講清楚是『那位女生』，這裡的受詞直接用her（她）就好，所以答案選 A her。其他選項：each other是『彼此』（要兩個主詞才能用）、any、another，都不合句意。

questions.stem: Joe is happy he is not at the ______ of the class now. His grades are better than half of his classmates' this school year.
questions.explain: 這句話說『Joe很開心他現在不在班上______了，他這學年的成績比一半同學都好』。成績贏過一半的人，代表不再是班上最後一名、墊底，英文用at the bottom of the class（班上吊車尾），所以答案選 D bottom。其他選項都不是『吊車尾』的意思。

questions.stem: Look! There is not a cloud in the sky. I think the chances of getting some rain today are really ______.
questions.explain: 這句話說『你看！天空一片雲都沒有，我覺得今天下雨的機會真的很______』。晴空萬里，下雨機會當然很『小』，英文用small，所以答案選 D small。其他選項：good是好、far是遠、possible是可能，都不合『晴天代表雨機率低』的邏輯。

questions.stem: On windy days, the flowers in my garden ______ like shy children with their heads down.
questions.explain: 這句話說『颳風的日子，我花園裡的花會像害羞的孩子低著頭一樣______』。低著頭的動作英文叫bow（彎腰、低頭），所以答案選 A bow。其他選項：fall是掉落、rise是升起、smell是聞，都不符合『低頭像害羞小孩』的畫面。

questions.stem: Mr. Olson went to the doctor when he found he ______ his hair. He hoped he would be able to keep his thick hair.
questions.explain: 這句話說『Olson先生發現自己______頭髮的時候去看了醫生，他希望能保住濃密的頭髮』。這裡的『發現』（found）跟『去看醫生』（went）都是過去發生的事，而『掉頭髮』是那個時候正在持續進行的事，英文用過去進行式was losing來表示『當時正在……』，所以答案選 A was losing。

questions.stem: Do you know what Lindsey's first job was? Before becoming a movie star, she ______ the floors at the supermarket.
questions.explain: 這句話說『你知道Lindsey的第一份工作是什麼嗎？在成為電影明星之前，她曾在超市______地板』。這是在講很久以前、已經結束的事，英文要用過去式，拖地是mop，過去式是mopped，所以答案選 D mopped。

questions.stem: Our dog Lassie hides under the bed every time the moon comes out. She has been ______ for years, but we don't know why.
questions.explain: 這句話說『我們家的狗Lassie每次月亮出來就躲到床底下，她已經______好幾年了，但我們不知道為什麼』。這裡的『這樣』指的就是前面說的『躲床底下』這個行為，英文用like this（像這樣），所以答案選 B like this。

questions.stem:
Tom: Sam, could you help me in the kitchen now?
Sam: No, I ______ TV. It's the most important game of the season.
questions.explain: 這段對話是湯姆問山姆能不能來廚房幫忙，山姆說『不行，我現在______電視，這是這一季最重要的比賽』。『現在』（now）正在做的事，用現在進行式，所以答案選 D am watching。

questions.stem: According to the comics, what kind of person is Hawkins?

options.A: He sees good things in people.
options.B: He never goes to work on time.
options.D: He talks about things he can't do.

questions.passage: In 6th grade, I tried to get the students to pick me as student leader. I totally bombed it. "I'll make class hours shorter. And we'll have only three days of school a week…" "What? That's not what a student leader can decide." "He doesn't know anything." "I was the best choice, but why didn't people know that?" I became a salesperson when I was 23, and I believed I could be Number 1. I bombed it again. "You said you would sell 50 cars a month. But you sold only 1 car in two months." "Don't worry. I'll sell 149 cars next month." "Hawkins, we don't need your service anymore. Go pack your things…" "Why?" "You said that a year ago."
questions.explain: 這題問漫畫裡的Hawkins是怎樣的人。他選班級幹部時亂開空頭支票（要縮短上課、一週只上三天課），根本不是幹部能決定的事；當業務員時又誇口要賣很多車，結果做不到被開除。這些都顯示他常常說自己能做到什麼，結果卻做不到，所以答案選 D『他常說一些自己做不到的事』。其他選項文章裡都沒有這樣描述他。

questions.stem: What does it mean when we say someone bombed something?
questions.passage: In 6th grade, I tried to get the students to pick me as student leader. I totally bombed it. "I'll make class hours shorter. And we'll have only three days of school a week…" "What? That's not what a student leader can decide." "He doesn't know anything." "I was the best choice, but why didn't people know that?" I became a salesperson when I was 23, and I believed I could be Number 1. I bombed it again. "You said you would sell 50 cars a month. But you sold only 1 car in two months." "Don't worry. I'll sell 149 cars next month." "Hawkins, we don't need your service anymore. Go pack your things…" "Why?" "You said that a year ago."
questions.explain: 這題問bombed這個字是什麼意思。Hawkins選幹部沒選上、賣車業績超差被開除，可以猜到bomb在這裡是『搞砸了、徹底失敗』的意思，所以答案選 B『他們失敗了』。

questions.stem: Which question can the brochure answer?

options.C: How can I get to the Marigolds' Home?
options.D: When can I visit the Marigolds' Home?

questions.passage: This is a brochure for the Marigolds' Home. The Marigolds' Home. Opening times: March to October 10:00-17:00; November to February 10:00-16:00. Closed on Mondays. To make sure you enjoy your visit to The Marigolds' Home, we'd like to ask you to follow the rules below: 1. Pets are not allowed in any areas of the Marigolds' Home. 2. Eating and drinking are not allowed inside the buildings, except in the café. 3. Picture-taking is not allowed inside Sir Archie's House. 4. Please take off your shoes before entering the Rabbit's Temple. Become a member and save 10% on tickets and 30% on all items in the gift shops. To join, visit www.themarigoldshome.com.
questions.explain: 這題問這份簡介手冊可以回答哪個問題。手冊上寫了開放時間（幾月到幾月、幾點到幾點）跟『週一休館』，所以可以回答『什麼時候可以參觀』，答案選 D。手冊沒有寫線上訂票、票價多少、怎麼去，所以其他選項都答不出來。

questions.stem: What breaks the rules for visitors to the Marigolds' Home?

options.A: Eating burgers in the Rose Garden.
options.B: Taking pictures in the Main House.
options.C: Entering the Rabbit's Temple without shoes.
options.D: Taking pet dogs for a walk on the playground.

questions.passage: This is a brochure for the Marigolds' Home. The Marigolds' Home. Opening times: March to October 10:00-17:00; November to February 10:00-16:00. Closed on Mondays. To make sure you enjoy your visit to The Marigolds' Home, we'd like to ask you to follow the rules below: 1. Pets are not allowed in any areas of the Marigolds' Home. 2. Eating and drinking are not allowed inside the buildings, except in the café. 3. Picture-taking is not allowed inside Sir Archie's House. 4. Please take off your shoes before entering the Rabbit's Temple. Become a member and save 10% on tickets and 30% on all items in the gift shops. To join, visit www.themarigoldshome.com.
questions.explain: 這題問哪一個行為違反了參觀規則。規則第一條寫『任何區域都不能帶寵物』，所以帶寵物狗在遊樂場散步就違反規定了，答案選 D。其他選項：在玫瑰園吃東西（禁食的是室內建築物）、在主屋拍照（禁拍照的是Sir Archie's House）、脫鞋進兔廟（這本來就是規定，沒有違規），都不算違規。

questions.stem: Why was Beatrice Harrison called the Nightingale Lady?

options.B: She sang as beautifully as a nightingale did.
options.C: She taught nightingales to sing in a radio show.
options.D: She was famous for playing music with nightingales.

questions.passage: One summer evening when English cello player Beatrice Harrison was practicing in her garden, she was joined by a visitor she did not expect. The visitor was a nightingale. The small brown bird sang together with Harrison's cello music, and the birdsong sounded like the piece she was playing. For the next few evenings, her visitor kept coming back, sometimes with several of its friends. Harrison decided that more people should hear the little singers. So, on May 19, 1924, Harrison worked with the BBC, the UK's national TV and radio station, and played in her garden. At first, the artist was playing alone. Fifteen minutes before the end of her playing, the nightingales began to sing with the cello. The nightingale show was popular with radio listeners. It was repeated the next week, and for the next twelve years Harrison played on the radio with her garden friends. During this time, she got 50,000 fan letters. People called her "the Nightingale Lady."
questions.explain: 這題問Beatrice Harrison為什麼被稱為夜鶯女士。文章說她在花園拉大提琴時，夜鶯會跟著一起唱歌，後來她跟廣播電台合作，在節目裡跟夜鶯一起表演長達十二年，因為這樣的音樂表演出名，所以答案選 D『她因為和夜鶯一起演奏音樂而出名』。她不是自己寫歌、唱歌，也不是教鳥唱歌，所以其他選項都不對。

questions.stem: Which is NOT used in the reading to mean the nightingale(s)?
questions.passage: One summer evening when English cello player Beatrice Harrison was practicing in her garden, she was joined by a visitor she did not expect. The visitor was a nightingale. The small brown bird sang together with Harrison's cello music, and the birdsong sounded like the piece she was playing. For the next few evenings, her visitor kept coming back, sometimes with several of its friends. Harrison decided that more people should hear the little singers. So, on May 19, 1924, Harrison worked with the BBC, the UK's national TV and radio station, and played in her garden. At first, the artist was playing alone. Fifteen minutes before the end of her playing, the nightingales began to sing with the cello. The nightingale show was popular with radio listeners. It was repeated the next week, and for the next twelve years Harrison played on the radio with her garden friends. During this time, she got 50,000 fan letters. People called her "the Nightingale Lady."
questions.explain: 這題問文章裡哪一個詞『不是』用來指夜鶯。文章裡的her visitor（她的訪客）、the little singers（小歌手們）、her garden friends（她花園裡的朋友）都是指夜鶯；但the artist（那位藝術家）指的是Harrison自己（拉大提琴的人），不是夜鶯，所以答案選 C。

questions.stem: Why does Alex Jackson write about Rose Lacey?

options.D: Because she is leaving for another job.

questions.passage: With heavy hearts, we are here to say goodbye to Rose Lacey, a wonderful writer who will be sadly missed. Rose and I first met in 2010. I was at a party to pick up my wife when I heard Rose explaining to her and her friends why Get Across was a great movie. Her ideas were so interesting that I invited her to write some articles about movies for Action! US. Rose was very excited and agreed right away. Over the next ten years, Rose wrote more than 150 articles. Rose could always bring out something new from movies that we thought we knew so well. People often told me that Rose never failed to surprise them. Her articles often made them think whether they actually saw the same movie, but if they went to see it again, they experienced the movie in a whole new way. In her short life of 40 years, Rose has made her mark on the world of movies with her articles for Action! US. We are lucky to have had her in our lives. She was a very special person. Alex Jackson, Head of Action! US
questions.explain: 這題問Alex Jackson為什麼寫這篇文章紀念Rose Lacey。文章裡用了say goodbye（道別）、sadly missed（會被大家想念）、short life of 40 years（短短40年的一生）這些話，都是在悼念已經過世的人才會用的說法，所以可以知道Rose已經過世了，答案選 A。

questions.stem: How did Rose Lacey and Alex Jackson get to know each other?

options.C: They worked in the same office.
options.D: They took a course on movies together.

questions.passage: With heavy hearts, we are here to say goodbye to Rose Lacey, a wonderful writer who will be sadly missed. Rose and I first met in 2010. I was at a party to pick up my wife when I heard Rose explaining to her and her friends why Get Across was a great movie. Her ideas were so interesting that I invited her to write some articles about movies for Action! US. Rose was very excited and agreed right away. Over the next ten years, Rose wrote more than 150 articles. Rose could always bring out something new from movies that we thought we knew so well. People often told me that Rose never failed to surprise them. Her articles often made them think whether they actually saw the same movie, but if they went to see it again, they experienced the movie in a whole new way. In her short life of 40 years, Rose has made her mark on the world of movies with her articles for Action! US. We are lucky to have had her in our lives. She was a very special person. Alex Jackson, Head of Action! US
questions.explain: 這題問Rose和Alex是怎麼認識的。文章說Alex去參加一個派對接太太時，聽到Rose在講一部電影講得很精彩，因此認識了她，所以答案選 A『他們在派對上認識的』。

questions.stem: What is special about Rose Lacey's articles?

options.A: They are important to movie makers.
options.B: They talk about movies from a fresh angle.
options.C: They give interesting information about actors.
options.D: They are good examples of how to write stories.

questions.passage: With heavy hearts, we are here to say goodbye to Rose Lacey, a wonderful writer who will be sadly missed. Rose and I first met in 2010. I was at a party to pick up my wife when I heard Rose explaining to her and her friends why Get Across was a great movie. Her ideas were so interesting that I invited her to write some articles about movies for Action! US. Rose was very excited and agreed right away. Over the next ten years, Rose wrote more than 150 articles. Rose could always bring out something new from movies that we thought we knew so well. People often told me that Rose never failed to surprise them. Her articles often made them think whether they actually saw the same movie, but if they went to see it again, they experienced the movie in a whole new way. In her short life of 40 years, Rose has made her mark on the world of movies with her articles for Action! US. We are lucky to have had her in our lives. She was a very special person. Alex Jackson, Head of Action! US
questions.explain: 這題問Rose的文章特別在哪裡。文章說她總是能從大家早就熟悉的電影裡，找出新的、令人驚喜的東西，也就是用新角度看老電影，所以答案選 B『她的文章用新角度談電影』。

questions.stem: Why does the title say "Glass Bottles' Second Life"?

options.A: People collect sea glass and use it to make new glass bottles.
options.B: People collect sea glass at the beach and use it to make wishes.
options.C: Glass bottles that are thrown into the sea become the homes of sea animals.
options.D: Glass bottles that are thrown into the sea become sea glass which is used in art pieces.

questions.passage: SEA GLASS: GLASS BOTTLES' SECOND LIFE. Sea glass is made from the magic of the sea. It usually comes from glass bottles that are thrown into the water. Each piece of sea glass looks different, and sea glass is often seen on art pieces. The pictures below explain how sea glass is born. 1. Glass bottles are thrown as garbage into the sea. 2. When these bottles ride the waves of the sea, they hit each other or other garbage in the sea and break into small pieces of glass. 3. Pieces of glass are pushed by sea water and move along the sea floor. The sharp pieces slowly become rounder and rounder. 4. After tens or hundreds of years in the sea, the pieces of glass grow an ice-like white color on the outside and become sea glass. 5. Sea glass is finally pushed up to the beach. 6. Artists collect pieces of sea glass and put them into their works. However, people now seldom use glass to make bottles and bowls—paper bowls and cups have become more popular these days. This means there are fewer glass items in the sea, so less and less sea glass will be found in the future.
questions.explain: 這題問為什麼標題叫『玻璃瓶的第二生命』。文章說被丟進海裡的玻璃瓶，經過海水多年沖刷變成海玻璃，後來又被藝術家撿去做成藝術品，等於玻璃瓶有了重新開始的第二段生命，所以答案選 D。

questions.stem: According to the reading, how is sea glass made?

options.A: Sea glass becomes rounder after it is pushed up to the beach.
options.B: Colder sea water helps pieces of glass become sea glass faster.
options.C: Pieces of glass become white on the outside after many years in the sea.
options.D: Glass bottles break into pieces when they are dug out from the sea floor.

questions.passage: SEA GLASS: GLASS BOTTLES' SECOND LIFE. Sea glass is made from the magic of the sea. It usually comes from glass bottles that are thrown into the water. Each piece of sea glass looks different, and sea glass is often seen on art pieces. The pictures below explain how sea glass is born. 1. Glass bottles are thrown as garbage into the sea. 2. When these bottles ride the waves of the sea, they hit each other or other garbage in the sea and break into small pieces of glass. 3. Pieces of glass are pushed by sea water and move along the sea floor. The sharp pieces slowly become rounder and rounder. 4. After tens or hundreds of years in the sea, the pieces of glass grow an ice-like white color on the outside and become sea glass. 5. Sea glass is finally pushed up to the beach. 6. Artists collect pieces of sea glass and put them into their works. However, people now seldom use glass to make bottles and bowls—paper bowls and cups have become more popular these days. This means there are fewer glass items in the sea, so less and less sea glass will be found in the future.
questions.explain: 這題問海玻璃是怎麼形成的。文章步驟四說玻璃碎片在海裡待了幾十年到幾百年後，外層會長出像冰一樣的白色，所以答案選 C『玻璃碎片在海裡待很多年後，外表會變白』。變圓是在海底移動時發生的，不是上岸以後，所以選項A是錯的。

questions.stem: According to the reading, why will less sea glass be found in the future?

options.A: People do not make as many glass items as before.
options.B: People have learned not to throw garbage into the sea.
options.C: Artists are using too much sea glass in their art pieces.
options.D: Waves are not big enough to push sea glass up to the beach.

questions.passage: SEA GLASS: GLASS BOTTLES' SECOND LIFE. Sea glass is made from the magic of the sea. It usually comes from glass bottles that are thrown into the water. Each piece of sea glass looks different, and sea glass is often seen on art pieces. The pictures below explain how sea glass is born. 1. Glass bottles are thrown as garbage into the sea. 2. When these bottles ride the waves of the sea, they hit each other or other garbage in the sea and break into small pieces of glass. 3. Pieces of glass are pushed by sea water and move along the sea floor. The sharp pieces slowly become rounder and rounder. 4. After tens or hundreds of years in the sea, the pieces of glass grow an ice-like white color on the outside and become sea glass. 5. Sea glass is finally pushed up to the beach. 6. Artists collect pieces of sea glass and put them into their works. However, people now seldom use glass to make bottles and bowls—paper bowls and cups have become more popular these days. This means there are fewer glass items in the sea, so less and less sea glass will be found in the future.
questions.explain: 這題問為什麼未來能找到的海玻璃會變少。文章最後說現在的人已經比較少用玻璃做瓶子和碗，改用紙做的，所以丟進海裡的玻璃變少了，海玻璃自然也會越來越少，答案選 A。

questions.stem: What does the prevalence of English mean?

options.C: That English is easy and simple.
options.D: That English is not a national language.

questions.passage:
The Future of Icelandic (Scandinavian News, by Anna Adams). Icelandic is a language that is spoken only in Iceland. It has a long history. But many Icelanders are worried that they're losing Icelandic. The reason for their worry is the prevalence of English in Iceland. "I use English when I talk to my housework robot, use my phone, and watch movies," said Helgi Atlason, an engineer. There is a reason for that. Most of these products use English, and few companies care to change the language into Icelandic because there are only a small number of Icelandic speakers (314,000 people). Actually, you see English everywhere in Iceland. Icelanders also speak and hear more English than Icelandic these days because many foreigners who come to live and work in Iceland speak only English. "We can't use Icelandic abroad, and we're not using it much in Iceland, either. How do you expect our kids will want to learn it?" said Eirikur Wilson, a teacher. Will Iceland one day give up Icelandic for English? It may happen soon.

Our Future with Icelandic (Scandinavian News, by Gunnar Eggertsson). Many people think Icelandic is a language in its sickbed and that it needs to be saved. I understand their worries, but does the future of our language really look that bad? According to Dr. David Clingingsmith, a language needs at least 35,000 speakers to be "safe" from becoming a dying language. There are now 314,000 Icelandic speakers. Also, every year Iceland spends 51.3 million Icelandic crowns (3.7 million US dollars) teaching Icelandic to machines: phones, computers, and robots. Icelandic is appearing more often in products. Most importantly, schools still teach Icelandic to children. Clearly, we are not giving up our first language. However, I'm not saying that Iceland has done enough for Icelandic. Our country can do a better job at getting more people to speak this beautiful language. One way to do so is to give more language courses to foreigners. With much more work, I'm sure Icelandic can grow and even reach farther into the world.
questions.explain: 這題問prevalence of English是什麼意思。後面的句子舉了很多例子，說在冰島到處都看得到英文，可以猜到prevalence是指英文『非常普遍、隨處可見』，所以答案選 A『英文非常常見』。

questions.explain: 這題問Anna Adams用什麼方法讓讀者相信她的想法。她引用了一位工程師和一位老師親口說的話，來當作證據，這種借用別人親身經驗來說服人的方法，答案選 B『借用別人的經驗』。

questions.stem: Which words in the second reading are NOT used to describe Icelandic?

questions.explain: 這題問第二篇文章裡，哪個說法『不是』用來形容冰島語。作者Gunnar提到a dying language（快要消失的語言）時，其實是在講『很多人以為』的說法，是他要反駁的觀點，不是他自己真正用來形容冰島語的話；他自己反而說冰島語是our first language（我們的母語）和this beautiful language（這美麗的語言），所以答案選 B。

questions.stem: Are Anna Adams and Gunnar Eggertsson worried about the future of Icelandic?

options.C: Adams is, but Eggertsson is not.
options.D: Adams is not, but Eggertsson is.

questions.explain: 這題問Anna和Gunnar是不是都擔心冰島語的未來。Anna在文章裡表達擔心冰島語會被英文取代；但Gunnar覺得情況沒那麼糟，並不擔心，所以答案選 C『Adams擔心，但Eggertsson不擔心』。

questions.stem: Which idea do Anna Adams and Gunnar Eggertsson both talk about in the readings?

options.A: Whether Icelanders speak better English than Icelandic.
options.B: Whether the number of Icelandic speakers is big enough.
options.C: Whether school subjects should be taught only in Icelandic.
options.D: Whether Iceland should allow more foreigners to work in Iceland.

questions.explain: 這題問兩篇文章都有提到的共同話題是什麼。兩篇都提到冰島語使用人數是314,000人——Anna說人數太少讓人擔心，Gunnar說這個人數已經超過安全門檻不用太擔心，兩人討論的都是『說這個語言的人夠不夠多』，所以答案選 B。

questions.stem: "Come back on New Year's Day and show me what you have grown," he said to them, "and I will ______."

options.A: give you a piece of good land
options.B: choose a person to be the next king
options.C: put the beautiful ones in my castle
options.D: decide who will work in my garden

questions.passage: A long time ago, there was an old king who had no child of his own. The king was worried that his land would fall into the wrong hands after his death, so he decided to pick a child in his land to take the high seat. One day, he called all the children to his castle and gave each of them a seed. "Come back on New Year's Day and show me what you have grown," he said to them, "and I will 40 ." Every child brought their seeds home carefully. One of the children, Wong, planted his seed in a pot. He gave it water every day and made sure there was sun to help it grow. 41 . Then, Sung, the child of the richest family in the land, told people that a small plant was growing from his seed. He was 42 . Everyone excitedly said that he would be king. Soon, one after another, more and more children were also saying that they had something in their pots. But Wong still didn't find anything in his. On the big day, when Wong brought his pot to the castle, he saw all the other children carrying interesting plants. Some had flowers in the shape of a bird, and some had grasses of different colors. Everyone proudly showed their plants to the king, but the king looked unhappy until he saw Wong's pot. "I boiled the seeds before I gave them out, so no plants could grow from them." The king looked seriously at the children. "There is only one child who is 43 enough to be king."
questions.explain: 這句話是說國王對孩子們說『新年那天回來給我看你們種出了什麼，然後我會______』。國王原本就是想從孩子裡選一個人來繼承王位（take the high seat），所以他承諾的是『選出下一任國王』，答案選 B。

questions.stem: One of the children, Wong, planted his seed in a pot. He gave it water every day and made sure there was sun to help it grow. ______.

options.A: But he lost his pot a few weeks later
options.B: Months passed but nothing grew from it
options.C: But soon the plant that grew from it died
options.D: The seed grew into a big tree in a short time

questions.passage: A long time ago, there was an old king who had no child of his own. The king was worried that his land would fall into the wrong hands after his death, so he decided to pick a child in his land to take the high seat. One day, he called all the children to his castle and gave each of them a seed. "Come back on New Year's Day and show me what you have grown," he said to them, "and I will 40 ." Every child brought their seeds home carefully. One of the children, Wong, planted his seed in a pot. He gave it water every day and made sure there was sun to help it grow. 41 . Then, Sung, the child of the richest family in the land, told people that a small plant was growing from his seed. He was 42 . Everyone excitedly said that he would be king. Soon, one after another, more and more children were also saying that they had something in their pots. But Wong still didn't find anything in his. On the big day, when Wong brought his pot to the castle, he saw all the other children carrying interesting plants. Some had flowers in the shape of a bird, and some had grasses of different colors. Everyone proudly showed their plants to the king, but the king looked unhappy until he saw Wong's pot. "I boiled the seeds before I gave them out, so no plants could grow from them." The king looked seriously at the children. "There is only one child who is 43 enough to be king."
questions.explain: 這句話前面說Wong每天澆水、確保有陽光，後面接著要說種子的結果。因為後面文章一直提到『Wong的盆子裡還是沒有東西』，可以知道種子其實一直沒發芽，所以這裡應該是『過了好幾個月，卻什麼都沒長出來』，答案選 B。

questions.stem: Then, Sung, the child of the richest family in the land, told people that a small plant was growing from his seed. He was ______. Everyone excitedly said that he would be king.

options.A: the last one to give up
options.B: the first one to share good news
options.C: the first one to bring his pot to the king
options.D: the only one to take good care of his plant

questions.passage: A long time ago, there was an old king who had no child of his own. The king was worried that his land would fall into the wrong hands after his death, so he decided to pick a child in his land to take the high seat. One day, he called all the children to his castle and gave each of them a seed. "Come back on New Year's Day and show me what you have grown," he said to them, "and I will 40 ." Every child brought their seeds home carefully. One of the children, Wong, planted his seed in a pot. He gave it water every day and made sure there was sun to help it grow. 41 . Then, Sung, the child of the richest family in the land, told people that a small plant was growing from his seed. He was 42 . Everyone excitedly said that he would be king. Soon, one after another, more and more children were also saying that they had something in their pots. But Wong still didn't find anything in his. On the big day, when Wong brought his pot to the castle, he saw all the other children carrying interesting plants. Some had flowers in the shape of a bird, and some had grasses of different colors. Everyone proudly showed their plants to the king, but the king looked unhappy until he saw Wong's pot. "I boiled the seeds before I gave them out, so no plants could grow from them." The king looked seriously at the children. "There is only one child who is 43 enough to be king."
questions.explain: 這句話是說Sung跟大家說他的種子長出了小植物，他是______，大家聽了都興奮地說他會當國王。因為Sung是第一個說自己種子發芽的人，之後才有其他小孩跟著說自己盆子裡也有東西，所以他是『第一個分享好消息的人』，答案選 B。

questions.stem: The king looked seriously at the children. "There is only one child who is ______ enough to be king."
questions.passage: A long time ago, there was an old king who had no child of his own. The king was worried that his land would fall into the wrong hands after his death, so he decided to pick a child in his land to take the high seat. One day, he called all the children to his castle and gave each of them a seed. "Come back on New Year's Day and show me what you have grown," he said to them, "and I will 40 ." Every child brought their seeds home carefully. One of the children, Wong, planted his seed in a pot. He gave it water every day and made sure there was sun to help it grow. 41 . Then, Sung, the child of the richest family in the land, told people that a small plant was growing from his seed. He was 42 . Everyone excitedly said that he would be king. Soon, one after another, more and more children were also saying that they had something in their pots. But Wong still didn't find anything in his. On the big day, when Wong brought his pot to the castle, he saw all the other children carrying interesting plants. Some had flowers in the shape of a bird, and some had grasses of different colors. Everyone proudly showed their plants to the king, but the king looked unhappy until he saw Wong's pot. "I boiled the seeds before I gave them out, so no plants could grow from them." The king looked seriously at the children. "There is only one child who is 43 enough to be king."
questions.explain: 這句話是國王嚴肅地看著孩子們說『只有一個孩子夠______才能當國王』。因為國王事先把種子煮過，所以沒有一顆種子真的能發芽，只有誠實端出空盆子的Wong沒有說謊，所以國王看重的是『誠實』，英文是honest，答案選 C。

questions.stem: When I was a teenager, I was very ______.  But now, it's easier for me to talk to people.
questions.explain: 這句話說『我以前是青少年的時候很______，但現在比較容易跟人講話』。因為『現在比較敢講話』，代表『以前不敢講話、很緊張』，這種個性的英文叫 shy（害羞的），所以答案選 D shy。其他選項：happy是開心、lazy是懶惰、popular是受歡迎，都跟『以前不敢講話』對不上。

questions.stem: Lena doesn't want to go ______ with John because she is afraid of water.
questions.explain: 這句話說『Lena不想跟John去______，因為她怕水』。既然是怕水，這個活動一定跟水有關，sailing 是『搭船、航行』的意思，最跟水有關，所以答案選 C sailing。其他選項：dancing是跳舞、hiking是健行、shopping是逛街，都跟水沒關係。

questions.stem: Cindy enjoys ______ her dad read stories to her before bed.
questions.explain: 這句話說『Cindy很喜歡睡前______爸爸讀故事給她聽』。enjoy 是『喜歡』的意思，後面接的動詞要加 ing，像 enjoy singing（喜歡唱歌）一樣，這裡是『喜歡聽』，listen加ing、後面接to，所以答案選 B listening to。其他選項的動詞形式不對，不能接在enjoy後面。

questions.stem: Dad is busy cooking in the kitchen.  Dinner will be ______ in ten minutes.
questions.explain: 這句話說『爸爸正忙著在廚房煮飯，晚餐十分鐘後就會______』。煮好、可以上桌了，英文叫 ready（準備好了），所以答案選 D ready。其他選項：free是空閒、full是滿的、medium是中等，都不合句意。

questions.stem: There are so many new ______ in the office.  It'll take me some time to remember who is who.
questions.explain: 這句話說『辦公室有好多新的______，要花點時間才能記住誰是誰』。要記住『誰是誰』，指的當然是很多新面孔、新同事，英文用 faces（臉、面孔），所以答案選 A faces。其他選項：ideas是點子、rules是規定、tools是工具，都跟『記住是誰』無關。

questions.stem: I feel like a ______.  I was looking for my keys for hours but they have been in my pocket the whole time.
questions.explain: 這句話說『我覺得自己像個______，找鑰匙找了好幾個小時，結果鑰匙一直都在口袋裡』。找了老半天結果東西一直在自己身上，會覺得自己很傻，英文叫 fool（傻瓜），所以答案選 A fool。其他選項：ghost是鬼、king是國王、stranger是陌生人，都跟『覺得自己很傻』沒關係。

questions.stem: Mr. and Mrs. Wu have three daughters.  Two are in high school, and ______ is in elementary school.
questions.explain: 這句話說『吳先生和吳太太有三個女兒，兩個在讀高中，______在讀國小』。三個裡面先講了兩個，剩下最後那一個，英文用 the other（剩下的那一個），所以答案選 B the other。其他選項不是用來指『剩下最後一個』的講法。

questions.stem: It is hard for trees to ______ along this beach because of the strong winds from the sea.
questions.explain: 這句話說『因為海邊風很強，樹很難在這片海灘上______』。樹在土地上長大，英文叫 grow（生長），所以答案選 D grow。其他選項：blow是吹、build是建造、follow是跟隨，都不是『樹木長大』的意思。

questions.stem: Christmas ______ and I want to visit my aunt abroad.  Do you have any plans yet?
questions.explain: 這句話說『聖誕節快要到了，我想去國外拜訪阿姨，你有計畫了嗎』。講的是快要發生的事，英文可以用『現在進行式』來表示快要發生的未來事情，就像說『I'm coming（我馬上來）』一樣，所以這裡用 is coming，答案選 C。其他選項的時態不合句意。

questions.stem: Jo won't be happy if you're late for his party tonight, so ______ sure that you arrive on time.
questions.explain: 這句話說『如果你今晚參加喬的派對遲到，他會不高興，所以請______準時到』。這句話是在『叫別人做什麼』，像老師說『Stop talking!（不要講話！）』一樣，動詞要用最原始的形式、不加s也不變化，所以這裡用 make sure（務必、一定要），答案選 A make。其他選項的動詞形式不對，不能這樣用。

questions.stem: You may have a long drive because of the terrible ______.  There are usually a lot of cars and buses during this time.
questions.explain: 這句話說『因為糟糕的______，你可能要開很久的車，這個時段通常有很多車和公車』。很多車很多公車在路上，指的是『交通、車潮』，英文叫 traffic，所以答案選 D traffic。其他選項：experience是經驗、machine是機器、service是服務，都跟『很多車』無關。

questions.stem: In the future, there will ______ be greater basketball players than Stephen Curry, but now we believe he is the best!
questions.explain: 這句話說『未來______會有比Stephen Curry更厲害的籃球選手，但現在我們相信他是最棒的』。這句話語氣不確定，是『或許、也許』的意思，英文用 perhaps，所以答案選 D perhaps。其他選項：again是再一次、already是已經、never是從不，語氣都太肯定或不合句意。

questions.stem: I guess the rainwater has come in from the kitchen.  See? ______ of the windows are closed except the one in the kitchen.
questions.explain: 這句話說『我猜雨水是從廚房滲進來的，你看？除了廚房那扇窗，其他窗戶______都關著』。除了一扇窗以外，其他全部都關著，英文用 All（全部），所以答案選 A All。其他選項：Both是兩者、Most是大部分、Some是一些，都不是『除了一個以外全部』的意思。

questions.stem: ______ machines have been used to pick fruits for a long time, they were not used on strawberry farms until several years ago.
questions.explain: 這句話說『______機器已經被用來採收水果很久了，草莓園幾年前才開始用機器』。前面說機器早就被拿來採果，後面卻說草莓園最近才用，前後語意有點『轉折、不一樣』，這種轉折的句子英文用 Although（雖然），所以答案選 A Although。其他選項：Because是因為、Before是在……之前、If是如果，語意都不合。

questions.stem: There are online videos that teach you exercises you can do at home.  They'll ______ you a trip to the gym, and some money too.
questions.explain: 這句話說『網路上有教你在家運動的影片，它們可以幫你______跑健身房的時間，還有一些錢』。在家運動就不用花錢花時間去健身房，等於『省下』了這些，英文叫 save，所以答案選 D save。其他選項：cost是花費、give是給、keep是保留，都不是『省下』的意思。

questions.stem: Jane's parents are always happy to see their grandchildren, but mine ______ less so when I visit them with my kids.
questions.explain: 這句話說『珍的爸媽每次看到孫子孫女都很開心，但我的爸媽當我帶小孩去看他們時，就沒那麼開心』。mine在這裡代表『我的爸媽』（my parents），是兩個人、算複數，動詞要用 are，答案選 B are。其他選項的動詞形式跟複數的爸媽對不上。

questions.stem: Before she ______ about it, you should tell Daphne you broke her favorite cup.
questions.explain: 這句話說『在她______這件事之前，你應該先跟達芙妮說你打破了她最愛的杯子』。這種『在……之前』的句子講的是還沒發生的事，但英文習慣不用未來式，而是直接用現在式來代替，所以這裡用 asks，答案選 A asks。其他選項的時態不是這種句型該用的形式。

questions.stem: It was very windy this morning.  Some of the shirts on the balcony were blown away ______ in the pond.
questions.explain: 這句話說『今天早上風很大，陽台上有些衣服被吹走______掉進池塘』。衣服先『被吹走』，然後又『掉進』池塘，這是兩個接連發生的動作，中間要用 and 連接，而且『掉進』這個動作也要跟前面一樣是過去式 fell，所以答案選 B and fell。其他選項少了 and，或動詞形式不對，接不起來。

questions.stem: What happened to Rex?
questions.passage: Rex is a dog that lives at the bakery next to our school.  He is cute and friendly.  Every morning, he stands in front of the school to welcome everybody.  We all see Rex as one of us.  But one Monday morning, I was surprised that Rex was not there to say hello to us.  “Rex is in the hospital.  He was hit by a car last night,” my classmate told me.  We went to see Rex in the hospital that day after school.  Two weeks later, Rex was much better, and we decided to take him for a walk every evening.  Because of the exercise, Rex became healthier and stronger.  Soon he could happily run and jump again.  He is now as handsome and strong as before!
questions.explain: 這篇文章在說一隻叫Rex的狗發生了什麼事。文章裡說 Rex was hit by a car（被車撞了），所以牠是受傷了，答案選 B『牠受傷了』。其他選項：迷路、咬人、吃太多，文章裡都沒有提到。

questions.stem: How did the writer help Rex?

options.C: By taking him to see a doctor.
options.D: By looking for a new home for him.

questions.passage: Rex is a dog that lives at the bakery next to our school.  He is cute and friendly.  Every morning, he stands in front of the school to welcome everybody.  We all see Rex as one of us.  But one Monday morning, I was surprised that Rex was not there to say hello to us.  “Rex is in the hospital.  He was hit by a car last night,” my classmate told me.  We went to see Rex in the hospital that day after school.  Two weeks later, Rex was much better, and we decided to take him for a walk every evening.  Because of the exercise, Rex became healthier and stronger.  Soon he could happily run and jump again.  He is now as handsome and strong as before!
questions.explain: 這題問作者們怎麼幫助Rex。文章說他們決定每天晚上帶Rex去散步（take him for a walk），靠運動讓牠慢慢恢復健康、變強壯，所以答案選 B『帶牠運動』。其他選項：打電話報警、帶去看醫生、幫牠找新家，文章都沒有寫。

questions.stem: What can you do with a White Lake City Card?

options.B: Visit any public museum in the city for free.
options.C: Take a train to places out of the three zones.
options.D: Move around the city by metro as much as you want.

questions.passage:
Buy a White Lake City Card When You're in the City
With any of the Cards, you can
- visit the city by metro, bus, or train as many times as you want. *
- take one child under 12 with you for free.
- save 20% on tickets to public museums.
The 1-day, 3-day, and 5-day Cards are for trips from Monday to Friday, and the Weekend Card is for trips on weekends and holidays.
Check the map and buy the right Card for the zones you'll be entering.

Card        Zone 1   Zones 1-2   Zones 1-3
1-day       $20      $40         $60
3-day       $40      $60         $80
5-day       $50      $80         $110
Weekend     $30      $50         $80
* The Cards are only for trips inside the three zones.（原題附區域地圖，此處省略）
questions.explain: 這題問買了White Lake City Card可以做什麼。海報上寫著可以搭地鐵、公車、火車『想搭幾次就搭幾次』（as many times as you want），也就是可以在城市裡無限次搭地鐵到處跑，所以答案選 D。其他選項：省20%車票只是給小孩的優惠、逛博物館是打折不是全免、跨區搭火車海報上寫『只能在這三個區域內使用』，都跟海報寫的不一樣，要仔細比對數字和條件才不會選錯。

questions.stem: What is Rolling Acres?
questions.passage:
At 5 o'clock every morning, while most people are sleeping, 16-year-old Libby Larson is already up.  She mops the floor, gets the mail and the newspapers, and gets ready for visitors to Rolling Acres.  Libby's grandparents started Rolling Acres in 1970, and since then all of the Larson family have been working there.  Rolling Acres used to be just a sheep farm, but now, with its fruit trees, vegetable gardens, and many other kinds of animals, it has become a popular place for families to go on vacation.  Visitors can pick fruits, collect eggs, and feed the animals.  It is one of Libby's jobs to show kids how to feed the baby sheep.

For her work, Libby is paid $10 an hour.  This summer, she expects to make about $1,200 and plans to buy a cell phone.  Her friends think it's great to make that much money in a summer.  However, for Libby, working at a family business also means making sacrifices.  “I'd love to sleep until noon or go to summer camp like other kids.  My friends stopped inviting me on trips because I'm always busy in the summer.”  However, Libby also said, “But it's better to see both sides.  Working with your family is great.  You feel closer to each other.”
questions.explain: 這題問Rolling Acres是什麼地方。文章說它現在有果樹、菜園和很多動物，已經變成一個很多家庭喜歡去度假的地方（a popular place for families to go on vacation），所以答案選 C『一個度假農場』。其他選項：動物園、露營地、家庭餐廳，文章都沒有這樣說。

questions.stem: What do we learn from the first paragraph?

options.A: What Libby does at Rolling Acres.
options.B: What visitors think of Rolling Acres.
options.C: Why Libby's grandparents started Rolling Acres.
options.D: What the Larson family's plans are for Rolling Acres.

questions.explain: 這題問第一段主要在講什麼。第一段描述Libby每天早上拖地、拿信、拿報紙，準備讓客人上門，也負責教小朋友怎麼餵小羊，這些都是在講『Libby在Rolling Acres做什麼事』，所以答案選 A。其他選項是文章後面才提到的內容，不是第一段的重點。

questions.stem: What does making sacrifices mean?

options.A: Learning to make money and use it wisely.
options.B: Getting to know different sides of your family.
options.C: Making excuses for failing to do something difficult.
options.D: Giving up something important to do something else.

questions.passage:

questions.explain: 這題問making sacrifices是什麼意思。文章說Libby很想像其他小孩一樣睡到中午、去夏令營，但因為要工作沒辦法，等於放棄了自己想做的事去做該做的事，這種『放棄重要的事、去做別的事』英文就叫 making sacrifices，所以答案選 D。其他選項都不是這個意思。

questions.stem: What do the comics tell us?

options.B: Follow the old ways of life.
options.C: Save our planet before it's too late.
options.D: Treat others the way we want to be treated.

questions.passage: （本題組為漫畫，以下為各格文字敘述）Around the year 400, people came to Easter Island.  “So many trees here!”  They used trees to make fire and build houses.  Because of the trees, people had a great life on the island.  In their free time, they loved making statues with big rocks.  With the trees they cut down, they could move the statues around easily.  Trees were needed and used in every part of their life.  Years passed, and there were fewer and fewer trees on Easter Island.  Without enough trees to keep water under the ground, the land became dry, and plants didn't grow.  “I'm thirsty.  And I'm hungry.”  People began to fight for water and food.  The statues were moved to fighting grounds to show their power.  The last trees were probably cut down at this time.  Those who cut down the last trees surely understood how important trees were to them—but still, they did it.  When the last trees fell to the ground, people on the island fell, too.  “Let's not make Earth, our only home, another Easter Island.”
questions.explain: 這題問這篇漫畫想告訴我們什麼。漫畫講復活節島的人把樹全部砍光，最後沒水沒食物、大家互相爭鬥，文明也跟著崩壞，最後一句說『別讓地球變成另一個復活節島』，這是在提醒大家趁還來得及的時候保護地球，所以答案選 C『在來得及之前保護我們的星球』。其他選項都不是漫畫真正想講的道理。

questions.stem: What can we learn about the people in the comics?

options.A: They made fire before starting a fight.
options.B: They fought for land and plants all the time.
options.C: They prayed to the statues beside a large fire.
options.D: They used statues to show how strong they were.

questions.passage: （本題組為漫畫，以下為各格文字敘述）Around the year 400, people came to Easter Island.  “So many trees here!”  They used trees to make fire and build houses. Those who cut down the last trees surely understood how important trees were to them—but still, they did it.  When the last trees fell to the ground, people on the island fell, too.  “Let's not make Earth, our only home, another Easter Island.”
questions.explain: 這題問我們可以從漫畫裡知道島上的人什麼事。漫畫裡說雕像被搬到打鬥的地方，是為了展現他們的力量（to show their power），所以答案選 D『他們用雕像來展現自己有多強』。其他選項文章裡沒有這樣寫。

questions.stem: What does did it in Picture 7 mean?

options.C: Move the statues to fighting grounds.

questions.passage: （本題組為漫畫，以下為各格文字敘述）Around the year 400, people came to Easter Island.  “So many trees here!”  They used trees to make fire and build houses. Those who cut down the last trees surely understood how important trees were to them—but still, they did it.  When the last trees fell to the ground, people on the island fell, too.  “Let's not make Earth, our only home, another Easter Island.”
questions.explain: 這題問第7格裡的did it是指什麼。前一句才剛說『砍下最後的樹的人』，接著說『但他們還是做了』，這個『做了』（did it）指的就是前面講的『砍下最後的樹』，所以答案選 B。其他選項都不是did it指的動作。

questions.stem: What did Ariely try to find out in the origami study?

options.A: If he could stop the IKEA effect.
options.C: Why IKEA furniture is so famous.
options.D: If anyone shared his IKEA experience.

questions.passage:
Many years ago, Dan Ariely bought a cabinet from IKEA.  IKEA is a famous furniture store that sells boxes of furniture parts.  People who buy them have to put all the parts together themselves.  It took Ariely hours to build his cabinet.  Ariely didn't really enjoy himself when he was trying to put all the parts together.  However, after he finished, he felt quite good about himself and his cabinet.  Later, he found that he loved his cabinet more than any of his other furniture.

Ariely wanted to know if anyone shared his feelings, so he did a fun origami study.  He invited two groups of people to join his study.  He called one group “builders” and the other group “buyers.”  Builders were told to make origami, like paper frogs or paper birds.  When they finished, they were asked how much they would spend on their works.  Ariely then showed the origami to buyers and asked them how much they would pay for them.  The two groups gave very different answers.  Though buyers would pay only five cents for builders' origami, builders would spend 25 cents!  Interestingly, builders believed others would think their works were good enough to cost that much money!

Ariely's study showed that he was not alone in his IKEA experience — we just love things more, sometimes blindly, when we make them ourselves.  Ariely called it “the IKEA effect.”
questions.explain: 這題問Ariely在摺紙研究裡想知道什麼。第二段說Ariely想知道『是不是也有別人跟他有一樣的感覺』（if anyone shared his feelings），也就是想知道別人是不是也有過跟他一樣組裝IKEA家具的經驗，所以答案選 D。其他選項都不是他做這個研究的目的。

questions.stem: Which is true about the origami study?

options.A: Buyers would spend less on builders' origami than builders would.
options.B: Builders and buyers needed to decide a price on the origami they made.
options.C: Builders knew others would not pay as much for the origami as they would.
options.D: Buyers would spend more on builders' origami after knowing how they were made.

questions.explain: 這題問關於摺紙研究，哪一個是對的。文章說買家（buyers）只願意出五分錢買別人摺的紙，但做的人自己（builders）卻願意花二十五分錢，也就是說『買的人出的錢比做的人少』，所以答案選 A。其他選項跟文章寫的不一樣。

questions.stem: Jerry just can't get his daughter Mia to eat more vegetables at dinner.  Every time he tries to do so, there is always a lot of shouting and crying.  If Jerry wants to have Mia eat more vegetables by using the IKEA effect, what should he do?

options.A: Tell Mia that he cooks the vegetables just for her.
options.B: Ask Mia to help him cook vegetables for her meal.
options.C: Give Mia her favorite candy after she eats vegetables.
options.D: Ask Mia what vegetables she likes and cook them for her.

questions.passage:

questions.explain: 這題是情境題：Jerry想用IKEA效應讓女兒Mia多吃蔬菜。IKEA效應的意思是『自己動手做的東西會讓人更喜歡它』，就像Ariely自己組裝家具後更愛那個家具一樣。所以如果讓Mia自己動手幫忙煮蔬菜，她做完之後會更想吃自己煮的菜，答案選 B『請Mia幫忙一起煮蔬菜』。其他選項都不是『自己動手做』的作法，用不到IKEA效應。

questions.stem: What is the reading mainly about?

options.A: The tips on using a picture to tell a story.
options.B: The change that electricity brought to people's lives.
options.C: The history behind the picture of a UK electricity worker.
options.D: The story of a famous UK electricity worker from the 1970s.

questions.passage:
In the picture is a UK electricity worker in the 1970s.  Pictures like this appeared in many newspapers at the time.  In 1972, electricity workers in the UK asked to be paid more, and they decided to stop working until their wish was answered.  The government worried that there wouldn't be enough electricity for the country if these workers didn't go to work, so it agreed to give them the pay rise.  However, the story didn't end there.
A year later, the electricity workers asked for another pay rise.  To get what they wanted, they worked shorter hours and acted like they would walk away from their job anytime.  But this time, the government decided to fight.  To save electricity, it made new rules for people to follow.  For example, each family could turn on the heat in only one room; TV stations had to stop their shows at 10:30 p.m.; businesses could open only three days a week.
People's lives were greatly changed.  Without enough heat to keep the house warm, people covered themselves in blankets all the time.  Hospitals had to use candles because they couldn't keep all the lights on.  Factories couldn't keep their machines working.  Many people lost their jobs.  For months, people were paying the price while the electricity workers were fighting for their wish.  In the end, the government agreed to the second pay rise.  This picture shows what many people thought of the electricity workers during the “dark” time.
questions.explain: 這題問這篇文章主要在講什麼。整篇文章都在講這張1970年代英國電力工人照片背後發生的故事，所以答案選 C『這張英國電力工人照片背後的歷史』。其他選項太籠統或不是文章重點。

questions.stem: In the UK in the 1970s, what did people most likely think of the UK electricity workers?

options.A: They were not brave enough to fix their problems.
options.B: They worked like robots and never learned to change.
options.C: They were asking too much and did not know when to stop.
options.D: They did not care whether their job might hurt people's health.

The government worried that there wouldn't be enough electricity for the country if these workers didn't go to work, so it agreed to give them the pay rise.  However, the story didn't end there.
A year later, the electricity workers asked for another pay rise.  To get what they wanted, they worked shorter hours and acted like they would walk away from their job anytime.  But this time, the government decided to fight.  To save electricity, it made new rules for people to follow.  For example, each family could turn on the heat in only one room; TV stations had to stop their shows at 10:30 p.m.; businesses could open only three days a week.
People's lives were greatly changed.  Without enough heat to keep the house warm, people covered themselves in blankets all the time.  Hospitals had to use candles because they couldn't keep all the lights on.  Factories couldn't keep their machines working.  Many people lost their jobs.  For months, people were paying the price while the electricity workers were fighting for their wish.  In the end, the government agreed to the second pay rise.  This picture shows what many people thought of the electricity workers during the “dark” time.
questions.explain: 這題問1970年代英國人可能怎麼看待電力工人。文章說工人才過一年就又要求加薪，還害大家生活變得很不方便，可以猜到當時人們可能覺得他們要求太多、不知道適可而止，所以答案選 C。其他選項文章沒有這樣描述。

questions.stem: Why does the writer put the “ ” mark around the word dark in the last sentence?

options.A: To say that the “dark” time was actually not dark.
options.B: To tell people that the word was said by the government.
options.C: To mean both the days without lights and the difficult lives people lived.
options.D: To show that the government and the electricity workers both lost their fights.

questions.passage:
In the picture is a UK electricity worker in the 1970s.  Pictures like this appeared in many newspapers at the time.  In 1972, electricity workers in the UK asked to be paid more, and they decided to stop working until their wish was answered.  The government worried that there wouldn't be enough electricity for the country if these workers didn't go to work, so it agreed to give them the pay rise.  However, the story didn't end there.
A year later, the electricity workers asked for another pay rise.  To get what they wanted, they worked shorter hours and acted like they would walk away from their job anytime.  But this time, the government decided to fight.  To save electricity, it made new rules for people to follow.  For example, each family could turn on the heat in only one room; TV stations had to stop their shows at 10:30 p.m.; businesses could open only three days a week.
People's lives were greatly changed.  Without enough heat to keep the house warm, people covered themselves in blankets all the time.  Hospitals had to use candles because they couldn't keep all the lights on.  Factories couldn't keep their machines working.  Many people lost their jobs.  For months, people were paying the price while the electricity workers were fighting for their wish.  In the end, the government agreed to the second pay rise.  This picture shows what many people thought of the electricity workers during the “dark” time.
questions.explain: 這題問作者為什麼把dark這個字加上引號。這個dark有兩層意思：一是字面上『沒有電、沒有燈的黑暗』，二是比喻『人們過得很辛苦的日子』，一語雙關，所以答案選 C。其他選項只講到其中一個意思，不完整。

questions.stem: He ______ (38) doing this in his first year of junior high school.
questions.passage:
Most kids want gifts from their parents on their birthdays, but Cameron doesn't.  On his birthday, he gives his mom a gift.  He __38__ doing this in his first year of junior high school.  That year, during one class, he watched a video about how a baby is pushed from its mother's body.  After class, __39__.  How brave a woman is to have a baby!  Thank God I'll never have to do that!

When Cameron came home that day, he held his mom in his arms for a long time.  He decided that his birthday __40__.  During those nine months inside his mom, he just ate and slept while his mom was doing all the hard work.  So why should he get a birthday gift for doing nothing?  If anyone should get a gift on his birthday, it should be his mom.

The __41__ “birthday gift” Cameron prepared was a bag of cookies he baked.  The cookies tasted so bad, but his mom said they were the best thing she ever got.  Hearing that __42__.  It was so much better than getting a gift.  Now, on his birthday every year, Cameron makes a gift for his mother to thank her for giving him life.

This year, Cameron __43__ his mom a nice dress.  A few months ago, he learned to make dresses at school and decided to make one for his mom.  Now the dress is finished and hanging behind Cameron's bedroom door.  He believes the gift will tell his mother how much he loves her.
questions.explain: 這句話是說『他在國一那年______做這件事』。國一那年是過去發生的事，動詞要用過去式，開始的英文是start，過去式是started，所以答案選 B started。

questions.stem: After class, ______ (39).  How brave a woman is to have a baby!  Thank God I'll never have to do that!

options.A: he got quite scared of two things
options.B: he still couldn't understand two things
options.C: he couldn't stop thinking about two things
options.D: he did two things that would change his life

When Cameron came home that day, he held his mom in his arms for a long time.  He decided that his birthday __40__. If anyone should get a gift on his birthday, it should be his mom.

The __41__ “birthday gift” Cameron prepared was a bag of cookies he baked.  The cookies tasted so bad, but his mom said they were the best thing she ever got.  Hearing that __42__.  It was so much better than getting a gift.  Now, on his birthday every year, Cameron makes a gift for his mother to thank her for giving him life.

This year, Cameron __43__ his mom a nice dress.  A few months ago, he learned to make dresses at school and decided to make one for his mom.  Now the dress is finished and hanging behind Cameron's bedroom door.  He believes the gift will tell his mother how much he loves her.
questions.explain: 這句話前面說Cameron看了一段媽媽生小孩的影片，下課後______，接著說『女人生小孩好勇敢！還好我不用生小孩！』，這些話聽起來就是他腦中一直在想這兩件事、停不下來，所以答案選 C『他一直忍不住在想兩件事』。其他選項意思不合，例如選項A是『害怕』、選項B是『不懂』，跟後面的內容對不上。

questions.stem: He decided that his birthday ______ (40).  During those nine months inside his mom, he just ate and slept while his mom was doing all the hard work.

options.A: should be spent helping his mom
options.B: should be a time of welcoming a new start
options.C: should not be about him, but about his mom
options.D: should not be celebrated for just one day, but for nine months

This year, Cameron __43__ his mom a nice dress.  A few months ago, he learned to make dresses at school and decided to make one for his mom.  Now the dress is finished and hanging behind Cameron's bedroom door.  He believes the gift will tell his mother how much he loves her.
questions.explain: 這句話前面說Cameron決定生日______，後面接著解釋：在媽媽肚子裡的九個月，他只是吃跟睡，辛苦的都是媽媽，所以生日不該只想到自己，應該想到媽媽，答案選 C『生日不該是關於他，而是關於媽媽』。

questions.explain: 這句話是說Cameron準備的第______份『生日禮物』是一包他自己烤的餅乾。後面文章又說『現在每年生日都會做禮物給媽媽』，可以知道這是他做的『第一份』禮物，之後年年持續，所以答案選 A first（第一個）。

questions.stem: The cookies tasted so bad, but his mom said they were the best thing she ever got.  Hearing that ______ (42).  It was so much better than getting a gift.

questions.explain: 這句話前面說餅乾其實烤得不太好吃，但媽媽卻說這是她收過最棒的東西，聽到這句話讓Cameron______，比收到禮物還開心。心裡覺得溫暖、開心到飛起來，英文用 made his heart sing（讓他心花怒放），所以答案選 B。其他選項：頭痛、改變主意、放棄烘焙，都不合開心的語氣。

This year, Cameron __43__ his mom a nice dress.  A few months ago, he learned to make dresses at school and decided to make one for his mom.  Now the dress is finished and hanging behind Cameron's bedroom door.  He believes the gift will tell his mother how much he loves her.
questions.explain: 這句話說『今年Cameron______媽媽一件漂亮的洋裝』，後面接著說『幾個月前他學會做洋裝、決定幫媽媽做一件』、『現在洋裝已經做好掛在門後』，可以知道送洋裝這件事還沒發生、是快要發生的計畫，這種『打算要做的事』英文用 is going to + 動詞，所以答案選 A is going to give。

questions.stem: My ______ hurts so much that I cannot even turn my head.
questions.explain: 這句話說「痛到連頭都不能轉」，會痛到不能轉頭的部位是「脖子」，英文是 neck，所以答案選 C。（arm 手臂、knee 膝蓋、stomach 肚子都跟轉頭沒關係）

questions.stem: Our school basketball team won the national game last night. We are so ______ them.
questions.explain: 這句話說籃球隊贏了全國比賽，大家都很「以他們為榮」，英文片語是 be proud of，所以答案選 B。

questions.stem: Tomorrow is Sam's last day in the office. Nobody knows why he decided to ______.
questions.explain: 這句話說明天是 Sam 在辦公室的最後一天，沒有人知道他為什麼決定要「離開（工作）」，英文是 leave，所以答案選 B。

questions.stem: It's not a good idea to go mountain climbing in this bad ______. We should wait until the typhoon goes away.
questions.explain: 後面提到「颱風」，可以知道前面在說很差的「天氣」，英文是 weather，所以答案選 D。

questions.stem: Chris loves walking with Anna on snowy days, but Anna hates ______ very much.
questions.explain: 這句話說 Anna 很討厭「在下雪天散步」這件事。要用一個字代替前面整件事，用 it，所以答案選 D。

questions.stem: Lora likes to eat bananas that are already a little brown on the outside, and so ______ I.
questions.explain: 這句話的意思是「我也是」，前面用的是 likes（一般現在式），所以「我也是」要用 do，and so do I，所以答案選 B。

questions.stem: Your refrigerator shouldn't be making loud noises now, but if it ______ does, just give me a call and I'll come check it again.
questions.explain: 這句話是說「冰箱現在應該不會吵了，但如果它『仍然』還在吵，就打給我」，「仍然」的英文是 still，所以答案選 D。

questions.stem: After winning money in the card game, Jay decided to try again. He felt that he might also be ______ a second time.
questions.explain: 這句話說 Jay 玩牌贏了錢，決定再玩一次，覺得第二次可能也會「幸運」，英文是 lucky，所以答案選 C。

questions.stem: The knife doesn't cut very well. It's not as ______ as before.
questions.explain: 這句話說刀子切得不好，因為它不像以前那麼「鋒利」，英文是 sharp，所以答案選 D。

questions.stem: John will stay with his sister until he ______ an apartment.
questions.explain: until（直到）後面接的句子，就算是在講未來的事，也要用現在式來寫，he 是他，動詞要加 s，所以是 finds，答案選 C。

questions.stem: Students ______ to go on the school trip should ask their parents first.
questions.explain: 這句話在說「想去校外教學的學生」，要用 who 代替「學生」當主詞，再接 want（想要），who want 就是「想要…的」，所以答案選 A。

questions.stem: The temple sits alone in the mountains at a height of 3,000m ______ sea level.
questions.explain: 講海拔高度，「高於海平面」的英文說法是 above sea level，所以答案選 A。

questions.stem: Patty spent several days planning to invite Charlie to dinner, ______ she couldn't say a word when they met.
questions.explain: 這句話說 Patty 花了好幾天計畫要約 Charlie 吃飯，見面時卻一句話都說不出來，前後意思是相反的、有轉折，要用 but，所以答案選 A。

questions.stem: I can't tell you what I think of the movie because I ______ it. I'll probably watch it this Saturday.
questions.explain: 這句話說「到現在為止都還沒看過」這部電影，所以還沒辦法說感想，「到現在都還沒做過」用 haven't seen，所以答案選 C。

questions.stem: The new guy at the help desk answers calls like a ______. There are no ups and downs in his voice and you can't tell if he is happy or sad.
questions.explain: 這句話說他講話都沒有高低起伏，聽不出來是開心還是難過，這種講話方式很像沒有感情的「機器人」，英文是 robot，所以答案選 D。

questions.stem: Jasmine planned to spend her summer in the country, but right after she got there, she started to ______ the noise in the city.
questions.explain: 這句話說 Jasmine 本來計畫去鄉下過暑假，結果一到那裡卻開始「想念」城市的吵鬧聲，英文是 miss，所以答案選 C。

questions.stem: "Bad traffic" is perhaps the ______ excuse for being late when your boss knows it only takes you five minutes to walk to work.
questions.explain: 老闆明明知道走路上班只要五分鐘，還拿「塞車」當遲到的藉口，這是「最爛的」藉口，英文是 worst，所以答案選 D。

questions.stem: The housework in Mr. and Mrs. Wang's family ______ between them and their kids. Everyone's got their own job to do.
questions.explain: 這句話說家事是「被」爸媽和小孩一起分擔的，家事（housework）當主詞、動詞要用單數的被動形式，is shared 就是「被分擔」，所以答案選 A。

questions.stem: I want to find another dentist because ______ pulled out a good tooth last time I went to him.
questions.explain: 這句話是說「因為我的牙醫上次把一顆好牙拔掉了」，「我的牙醫」可以用 mine 這個字代替（意思是 my dentist），所以答案選 C。

questions.stem: Mom: Linda, you've been playing computer games all evening! Have you finished your report? Linda: Well, ______ most of it this afternoon, and I'll finish it by Friday.
questions.explain: Linda 說的是「今天下午」已經發生過的事，這是明確講出來的過去時間，要用過去式 did，I did（我做了）大部分，所以答案選 B。

questions.stem: Why did Philip go out?

options.C: To ask the police for help.
options.D: To buy food for his brother.

questions.passage:
It was around eight o'clock on Saturday night. Philip was at home with his little brother, Jason. Jason was hungry and kept crying. But Philip couldn't cook and their father was not home yet because he was working late in the office. So Philip decided to take Jason out for some food.
Half an hour later, when they came back home, Philip was surprised to find that their father was talking to the police and a woman outside their house. He didn't know who the woman was. She looked scared and stood behind the police. Philip's father was very angry and kept shouting, "I didn't do anything wrong! I forgot my keys and was just trying to get into MY OWN HOUSE!" But the police didn't believe him until Philip ran to them and explained everything.
The police told Philip that the woman called 110 when she saw Philip's father trying to get into a house through a window. That was how it all happened.
questions.explain: 文章說弟弟 Jason 肚子餓一直哭，Philip 又不會煮飯，所以決定帶弟弟出門買東西吃，所以答案選 D。

questions.stem: Why was Philip's father angry?

options.B: The woman was hiding from him.
options.C: The police didn't believe what he said.
options.D: Philip and his brother went out at night.

questions.passage:
It was around eight o'clock on Saturday night. Philip was at home with his little brother, Jason. Jason was hungry and kept crying. But Philip couldn't cook and their father was not home yet because he was working late in the office. So Philip decided to take Jason out for some food.
Half an hour later, when they came back home, Philip was surprised to find that their father was talking to the police and a woman outside their house. He didn't know who the woman was. She looked scared and stood behind the police. Philip's father was very angry and kept shouting, "I didn't do anything wrong! I forgot my keys and was just trying to get into MY OWN HOUSE!" But the police didn't believe him until Philip ran to them and explained everything.
The police told Philip that the woman called 110 when she saw Philip's father trying to get into a house through a window. That was how it all happened.
questions.explain: 文章說爸爸很生氣，是因為他明明沒做錯事，只是想進自己家門，警察卻不相信他說的話，所以答案選 C。

questions.stem: What kind of people do Yan's and Chang's friends most likely think Yan and Chang are?
questions.passage:
Yan lived a good life in a big house. One day he invited a friend to dinner at his house. However, on the dinner table there was only one small dish of one small fish. The friend looked quietly at the fish. It was no bigger than a finger. Then he asked Yan if he could borrow a lamp. "What for?" Yan asked. "Well, it's so dark in here," the friend said with a dry smile, "I can't see the other delicious dishes you've prepared for me."
Chang kept a lot of ducks and chickens on his farm. One day, his best friend came to visit him. At noon, Chang told his friend that he couldn't let him stay for lunch because there wasn't much food to eat. The friend looked out at Chang's farm animals for a moment. Then he asked Chang if he had a big knife. "Yes, but what for?" Chang asked. "I'm thinking about killing the horse I rode here so we'll have something for lunch," the friend said. "But how are you going to go home without it?" "Well, you wouldn't mind lending me one of your many ducks or chickens so I can ride it home, would you?"
questions.explain: 兩個朋友都用反諷的方式，說主人明明很有錢、家裡養很多雞鴨，卻只準備一點點食物招待客人，暗示 Yan 和 Chang 都是「不喜歡分享」的人，所以答案選 B。

questions.stem: What is it in the second story?

options.D: One of Chang's ducks or chickens.

questions.passage:
Yan lived a good life in a big house. One day he invited a friend to dinner at his house. However, on the dinner table there was only one small dish of one small fish. The friend looked quietly at the fish. It was no bigger than a finger. Then he asked Yan if he could borrow a lamp. "What for?" Yan asked. "Well, it's so dark in here," the friend said with a dry smile, "I can't see the other delicious dishes you've prepared for me."
Chang kept a lot of ducks and chickens on his farm. One day, his best friend came to visit him. At noon, Chang told his friend that he couldn't let him stay for lunch because there wasn't much food to eat. The friend looked out at Chang's farm animals for a moment. Then he asked Chang if he had a big knife. "Yes, but what for?" Chang asked. "I'm thinking about killing the horse I rode here so we'll have something for lunch," the friend said. "But how are you going to go home without it?" "Well, you wouldn't mind lending me one of your many ducks or chickens so I can ride it home, would you?"
questions.explain: 「But how are you going to go home without it?」這句話裡的 it，指的是前面朋友說要殺來當午餐吃的那匹「馬」，所以答案選 B。

questions.stem: Why does the writer talk about doctors and a health center in the reading?

options.B: To talk about Chen's future plans.
options.C: To explain Chen's love for books.
options.D: To show why bookstores are important.

questions.passage:
Usually, people visit bookstores to buy books. But those who walk into Cotoha come to see its owner, Chen Bing-Hong. They bring him books with different problems. Some have lost their covers, some are missing a few pages, and some have pages that are falling out. Chen can always fix the books and make them whole again.
Chen sees himself as a doctor. He says that books, like people, get sick and need help. In Taiwan, there are not many "doctors" like him, and most of them work for big libraries and museums. If big libraries and museums are hospitals, Chen's bookstore is a health center. Big libraries and museums fix important old books with serious problems, and Chen helps people with books that have smaller problems. However, the smaller size of the problems doesn't mean Chen's job is less important.
Chen gave an example of a science book he once worked on. It was very old and half of its cover was lost. Its owner still wanted to keep it because it was a gift from a teacher who helped him follow his dream of studying science. When he got his book back, he was very surprised at the new-old book. He felt he was brought back to the days with his teacher. Seeing the owner's smile made Chen happy, because smiles like that are what his magic is for.
questions.explain: 作者用「醫生」和「健康中心」來比喻陳先生修書的工作，是為了說明他到底在做什麼樣的服務，所以答案選 A。

questions.stem: Why is Chen Bing-Hong's job important in the example he gave?

options.A: It allows people to get books as gifts.
options.B: It saves people money on new books.
options.C: It gives people hope to follow their dreams.
options.D: It helps people think of special moments in the past.

questions.passage:
Usually, people visit bookstores to buy books. But those who walk into Cotoha come to see its owner, Chen Bing-Hong. They bring him books with different problems. Some have lost their covers, some are missing a few pages, and some have pages that are falling out. Chen can always fix the books and make them whole again.
Chen sees himself as a doctor. He says that books, like people, get sick and need help. In Taiwan, there are not many "doctors" like him, and most of them work for big libraries and museums. If big libraries and museums are hospitals, Chen's bookstore is a health center. Big libraries and museums fix important old books with serious problems, and Chen helps people with books that have smaller problems. However, the smaller size of the problems doesn't mean Chen's job is less important.
Chen gave an example of a science book he once worked on. It was very old and half of its cover was lost. Its owner still wanted to keep it because it was a gift from a teacher who helped him follow his dream of studying science. When he got his book back, he was very surprised at the new-old book. He felt he was brought back to the days with his teacher. Seeing the owner's smile made Chen happy, because smiles like that are what his magic is for.
questions.explain: 那本書修好後，主人看到修好的舊書，感覺自己好像又回到了跟老師相處的那段時光，所以修書可以幫人「想起以前珍貴的時刻」，答案選 D。

questions.stem: What is his magic?

options.C: Finding books that are long lost.
options.D: Changing his bookstore into a library.

questions.explain: 整篇文章用「魔法」來形容陳先生能把破損的書修得完整如新，所以「他的魔法」指的就是「修書」，答案選 A。

questions.stem: According to the reading, why did Rohla and Kreytenberg open Habibi & Hawara?

options.A: To help refugees live better in Austria.
options.B: To collect money to help Syria fight the war.
options.C: To help Austrians learn about the war in Syria.
options.D: To help refugees go back to their home countries.

questions.passage:
A Delicious Surprise and Welcome Change. By Karl Falk, June 1, 2016.
In Austria, there are not many restaurants like Habibi & Hawara. Here you can enjoy Austrian and Syrian food together. Two Austrians, Martin Rohla and David Kreytenberg, started this yummy plan from their experience of working with refugees.
Every year, thousands of people have to leave their homes and families because of war in their countries. So many of them go to Austria that some people in the country think they have become a problem. They are afraid refugees may take away their jobs and they don't feel safe around them, either. But Rohla and Kreytenberg would beg to differ: The refugees don't have to be a problem. They want to help them start a new life. They invited refugees to meetings and found that some of them were cooks back in Syria. So the idea of Habibi & Hawara was born—a restaurant that brings together Austrian and Syrian food. "The two worlds far away from each other meet together," Kreytenberg said.
But Rohla and Kreytenberg want to do more than just give the refugees work. They want their best workers to buy the restaurant in the end. They hope that Habibi & Hawara is not just a one-time plan and that other Austrians will join them and help more refugees.
questions.explain: 文章說兩人想幫助難民在奧地利「展開新生活」，所以開了一家餐廳讓難民有工作做，答案選 A。

questions.stem: What can we learn about Habibi & Hawara?

options.A: It was moved from Syria to Austria.
options.B: It may finally be sold to its workers.
options.C: It has cooking classes in Syrian food.
options.D: It is an important meeting place for Syrians.

questions.passage:

questions.explain: 文章最後說，他們希望表現最好的員工最後可以把餐廳「買下來」，所以答案選 B。

questions.stem: What does it mean when people beg to differ?
questions.passage:
A Delicious Surprise and Welcome Change. By Karl Falk, June 1, 2016.
In Austria, there are not many restaurants like Habibi & Hawara. Here you can enjoy Austrian and Syrian food together. Two Austrians, Martin Rohla and David Kreytenberg, started this yummy plan from their experience of working with refugees.
Every year, thousands of people have to leave their homes and families because of war in their countries. So many of them go to Austria that some people in the country think they have become a problem. They are afraid refugees may take away their jobs and they don't feel safe around them, either. But Rohla and Kreytenberg would beg to differ: The refugees don't have to be a problem. They want to help them start a new life. They invited refugees to meetings and found that some of them were cooks back in Syria. So the idea of Habibi & Hawara was born—a restaurant that brings together Austrian and Syrian food. "The two worlds far away from each other meet together," Kreytenberg said.
But Rohla and Kreytenberg want to do more than just give the refugees work. They want their best workers to buy the restaurant in the end. They hope that Habibi & Hawara is not just a one-time plan and that other Austrians will join them and help more refugees.
questions.explain: 前面說有人覺得難民是個問題，接下來說這兩位卻「不這麼認為」，beg to differ 就是「不同意、有不同看法」的意思，所以答案選 A。

questions.stem: What does it mean when someone is an advocate of something?

options.A: They talk a lot but do little about it.
options.B: They believe it is good and should be done.
options.C: They have had some bad experiences with it.
options.D: They are one of the first people who have done it.

questions.passage:
VOICES OF PEOPLE. (Ellen Zimmer) If you want to learn about bringing back extinct animals, reading John Smith's Back from the Dead is a good start. But I have to say I can't agree with everything he says.
From his book, I can see Smith is an advocate of bringing extinct animals back. He says we have lost many animals because we made their living spaces too dirty. He also thinks we must do everything we can to bring them back. However, I don't know how much time and money we would need to make the world a better place for them, and honestly no one knows if they could actually come back.
Smith also says that bringing back extinct animals may be good for us. He talks about an extinct frog that grew tadpoles in its stomach. Some women's babies die before they are born, and Smith believes bringing back the frog may be an answer to the problem. According to him, by studying how the tadpoles grow inside the frog's stomach, we might be able to find new ways to help these women successfully keep their babies. But I don't think things are as easy as Smith thinks. We have spent ten years and millions of dollars on this kind of frog, but all we have got are only a few dead frog eggs. If we can't see a real frog, how can we be sure women will get the help they need?
Dr. Solomon Wang from the Animal Saving Office says the study of bringing back extinct animals is "throwing good money after bad." And he's right. A lot of hard work has been put into this expensive dream, but we haven't seen anything come out of it. So should we still keep going down this road?
questions.explain: 文章說 Smith 很支持把絕種動物救回來，覺得一定要想辦法做到，這種「相信一件事是對的、覺得應該去做」的人就叫做 advocate，所以答案選 B。

questions.stem: What is special about the extinct frog in Smith's book?

options.A: It can live in a dirty living space.
options.B: It might help fix a health problem.
options.C: It eats its babies when it cannot find food.
options.D: It is the first extinct animal that people studied.

questions.explain: 文章說這種青蛙的蝌蚪是在媽媽的肚子裡長大的，研究牠可能可以幫忙解決「孕婦保住寶寶」的健康問題，所以答案選 B。

questions.stem: Why does Ellen Zimmer use the words from Dr. Solomon Wang?

options.D: To invite people to take action.

questions.explain: 作者引用王博士說「把錢丟到不會回來的地方」這句話，是為了讓「自己反對復育絕種動物」的想法講得更清楚，所以答案選 C。

questions.stem: What does Ellen Zimmer most likely think about bringing back extinct animals?
questions.passage:

questions.explain: 作者一直強調，花了十年、好幾百萬，卻只得到幾顆死掉的蛙蛋，看不到活的青蛙，這代表她認為復育絕種動物「是不可能做到的」，所以答案選 C。

questions.stem: Though it has been a very popular topic this year, social distancing ______. In fact, it was widely used in the U.S. in the flu pandemic in 1918.
questions.passage:
A HISTORY LESSON ON THE PANDEMIC (2020.10.29)
Since the Covid-19 pandemic started, people have begun "social distancing"—keeping a safe space between you and anyone you don't live with. Though it has been a very popular topic this year, social distancing ___(40)___. In fact, it was widely used in the U.S. in the flu pandemic in 1918. But how well did it work? The Americans' experience can tell us whether it really saved lives.
From the picture, we can see that ___(41)___ started social distancing earlier than the other three cities. This helped the city keep the number of deaths down. Besides taking early action, keeping the action long enough was also important in fighting the pandemic. The picture shows that Portland and Denver did well in the first few weeks. However, both cities ___(42)___, and the numbers of deaths climbed again.
But there was one thing Portland did right. The city used several ways of social distancing at the same time to deal with the pandemic. ___(43)___, sick people were kept away from healthy ones; schools were closed; public activities were not allowed. All these were useful in keeping people safe.
The lessons of the 1918 pandemic bring us hope. While we are still learning to fight Covid-19, social distancing may be the best way to get us through the dark days, at least for now.
questions.explain: 後面用 In fact 舉例說，1918年流感大流行時美國就已經廣泛使用這個方法了，可以知道社交距離「不是新點子」，所以答案選 A。

questions.stem: The picture shows that Portland and Denver did well in the first few weeks. However, both cities ______, and the numbers of deaths climbed again.

options.C: began social distancing a second time
options.D: had the most days of social distancing

questions.passage:
A HISTORY LESSON ON THE PANDEMIC (2020.10.29)
