The lessons of the 1918 pandemic bring us hope. While we are still learning to fight Covid-19, social distancing may be the best way to get us through the dark days, at least for now.
questions.explain: 前面說前幾週做得不錯，後面卻說死亡人數又上升了，可以推測是因為這兩個城市「太早停止」社交距離，所以答案選 B。

questions.stem: The city used several ways of social distancing at the same time to deal with the pandemic. ______, sick people were kept away from healthy ones; schools were closed; public activities were not allowed.
questions.passage:
A HISTORY LESSON ON THE PANDEMIC (2020.10.29)
Since the Covid-19 pandemic started, people have begun "social distancing"—keeping a safe space between you and anyone you don't live with. Though it has been a very popular topic this year, social distancing ___(40)___. In fact, it was widely used in the U.S. in the flu pandemic in 1918. But how well did it work? The Americans' experience can tell us whether it really saved lives.
From the picture, we can see that ___(41)___ started social distancing earlier than the other three cities. This helped the city keep the number of deaths down. Besides taking early action, keeping the action long enough was also important in fighting the pandemic. The picture shows that Portland and Denver did well in the first few weeks. However, both cities ___(42)___, and the numbers of deaths climbed again.
But there was one thing Portland did right. The city used several ways of social distancing at the same time to deal with the pandemic. ___(43)___, sick people were kept away from healthy ones; schools were closed; public activities were not allowed. All these were useful in keeping people safe.
The lessons of the 1918 pandemic bring us hope. While we are still learning to fight Covid-19, social distancing may be the best way to get us through the dark days, at least for now.
questions.explain: 空格後面列出好幾個具體做法（隔離病人、關閉學校、禁止公共活動），這些都是在「舉例說明」前面說的好幾種做法，所以答案選 D。

questions.stem: Dennis enjoys ______ in public. He is proud of his beautiful voice.
questions.explain: 這句話是說 Dennis 很喜歡在很多人面前做一件事，後面說他很得意自己聲音好聽。跟聲音有關的動作是「唱歌」，英文是 singing，所以答案選 D。（dancing 跳舞、drawing 畫畫、shopping 買東西都跟聲音沒關係）

questions.stem: Mrs. Johnson can't hear very well. If you need to talk to her, you must ______.
questions.explain: 這句話說 Johnson 太太耳朵不太好，如果要跟她講話，就要「大聲喊」，英文是 shout，所以答案選 D。（explain 是解釋、hurry 是趕快、listen 是聽，都不是「用力發出聲音」的意思）

questions.stem: People got very excited when they watched Ms. Smith ______ at the party.
questions.explain: 這句話是說大家看到 Smith 老師在派對上做什麼，大家都很興奮。watch（看）後面接的動作要用「動詞+ing」，像 watch her dancing（看她跳舞），所以答案選 B dancing。

questions.stem: I tried on these shoes in several different ______, and I thought the white pair looked best on me.
questions.explain: 這句話說試穿了好幾種不一樣的鞋子，後面說「白色那雙」最好看，可以猜到前面在講鞋子有不同的「顏色」，英文是 colors，所以答案選 A。

questions.stem: Rex did not feel the earthquake this morning. He ______ in the park at the time.
questions.explain: 這句話說 Rex 沒感覺到早上的地震，因為「當時」他正在公園做一件事。要說「當時正在做什麼」，動詞要用「was + ing」，像 was jogging（正在慢跑），所以答案選 B。

questions.stem: Mr. Lee has worked in the same store for ten years; he's never thought about ______ his job.
questions.explain: 這句話說 Lee 先生在同一家店工作十年了，從來沒想過要做什麼。think about 後面接的動詞要加 ing，像 think about changing（想要換），changing 是「換」的意思，所以答案選 A。

questions.stem: I didn't take the bus today because it was ______. All the seats were taken and a lot of students were standing.
questions.explain: 後面說座位都被坐光了，還有很多學生站著，可以知道公車「客滿」了，英文是 full，所以答案選 C。

questions.stem: Don't go away when you're cooking, ______ the food might burn.
questions.explain: 這句話是說「煮東西的時候不要走開，＿＿食物可能會燒焦」。要表示「不然、否則」用 or，所以答案選 C。

questions.stem: Jerry wanted to know ______ he was kicked off the soccer team, but no one gave him a good reason.
questions.explain: 後面說「沒有人給他一個好理由（reason）」，可以知道 Jerry 想知道的是「為什麼」被踢出球隊，英文是 why，所以答案選 D。

questions.stem: Jenny is already forty, doesn't have a job and often makes trouble for her parents. To them, she is really a(n) ______.
questions.explain: 這句話說 Jenny 已經四十歲了，沒有工作，還常常給爸媽惹麻煩，對爸媽來說她真的是一個很讓人「頭痛」的人，英文是 headache，所以答案選 D。

questions.stem: Ed and Jill ______ camping this weekend, so they have to finish their homework by Friday.
questions.explain: 這句話說「這個週末」要做什麼事，所以他們要在星期五前把作業寫完。講「已經計畫好、快要發生的事」可以用「be + V-ing」，像 are going camping（要去露營），所以答案選 C。

questions.stem: Doraemon, a blue Japanese robot cat, has hated mice since his ears ______ by a mouse.
questions.explain: 這句話說多啦A夢的耳朵是「被」老鼠咬的，而且是很久以前發生的事，要用「被＋過去式」的說法，像 were bitten（被咬了），所以答案選 C。

questions.stem: If we play some interesting games in class, there ______ more fun in learning English.
questions.explain: 這句話的意思是「如果我們在課堂上玩一些好玩的遊戲，學英文就會變得更好玩」，講「未來會有」用 there will be，所以答案選 C。

questions.stem: The ______ of this shop was so bad; I never got any answer after I emailed them my questions.
questions.explain: 這句話說寄信問問題卻都沒人回覆，可以知道是在說這家店的「服務」很差，英文是 service，所以答案選 D。

questions.stem: It's not easy to see those islands clearly from here on sunny days, and it's even less ______ to see them on cloudy days.
questions.explain: 這句話說晴天都不容易看清楚那些島了，陰天當然更「不可能」看到，英文是 possible，所以答案選 C。

questions.stem: Do you remember the CD I was looking for for months? I ______ found it in a small shop. Look, here it is!
questions.explain: 這句話說找了好幾個月的CD，「終於」在一家小店找到了，英文是 finally，所以答案選 C。

questions.stem: Business at Jane's shop has not been good these days. And the new supermarket across the street only makes things ______.
questions.explain: 這句話說 Jane 的店生意本來就不好，對面新開的超市讓情況變得「更糟」，英文是 worse，所以答案選 B。

questions.stem: Scott wasn't sure if the young woman before him was ______ pulled him out of a car on fire.
questions.explain: 這句話在說「那個女生是不是把他從著火的車裡拉出來的『那個人』」。要指一個人再補充說明是誰做了什麼事，可以說 the one who（就是那個…的人），所以答案選 D。

questions.stem: I ______ swimming for several years before I went to this high school. I gave it up because of heavy schoolwork.
questions.explain: 這句話是說「上這所高中之前」做了什麼事，指過去已經發生完的事，用過去式 practiced（練習過），所以答案選 C。

questions.stem: Frank Kane is so good in the movie that many people ______ he will win the best actor prize.
questions.explain: 這句話說 Frank 演得太好了，很多人「猜想、覺得」他會得最佳男主角獎，英文是 expect，所以答案選 A。

questions.stem: The new medicine that just came out on the market ______ thousands of lives.
questions.explain: 這句話的主角是「新藥」，要說它「已經救了」好幾千條人命，用「has + 過去分詞」表示到現在為止已經做完的事，has saved 就是「已經救了」，所以答案選 B。

questions.stem: Now I often think of those days with Pip, my pet dog. When I read in my room, he ______ quietly beside me.
questions.explain: 這句話在回想「以前常常發生的事」，要說「以前常常」用 used to，used to come and sit 就是「以前常常走過來坐下」，所以答案選 D。

questions.stem: What is the trick that the mosquito uses in rain?

options.A: It shakes its body fast enough to get water off.
options.B: It drops with the raindrop and then rolls off it.
options.C: It flies behind the raindrop and pushes it away.
options.D: It rides on the raindrop and lands on the ground.

questions.passage:
Sometimes when the rain falls hard and fast on you, it might hurt a little. But what happens when it hits a mosquito? A 2012 study says when a raindrop falls on a mosquito, it's like when a bus hits a person. Besides, the little insect is hit by a raindrop about once every 20 seconds. So why don't we see many dead mosquitoes after it rains?

A mosquito is as big as a raindrop, but it is much lighter — 0.002 g only. This saves its life in raindrop attacks. Because the mosquito is so light, when it is hit by a raindrop, it won't experience a force that is strong enough to kill it.

The study also found that when a mosquito is hit by a raindrop, the insect is pushed by the raindrop and falls together with it. But the mosquito doesn't get wet easily because it is covered with hairs which keep off water. After dropping about 6 cm, it will roll off the raindrop and fly away.

However, this trick isn't always successful. If the mosquito flies too low when it is hit by the raindrop, it won't have time to fly off. Then it will hit the ground and meet its death. (force 力量)
questions.explain: 文章第三段說，蚊子被雨滴打到的時候，會跟著雨滴一起往下掉，掉了大概6公分之後，再從雨滴上滾下來飛走。所以答案選 B。

questions.stem: What keeps a mosquito safe in the rain?

options.C: It is as big as a raindrop.
options.D: It is strong enough to fight the force of a raindrop.

questions.explain: 文章第二段說，蚊子因為很輕（只有0.002克），被雨滴打到時不會受到能要牠命的力量，所以答案選 A：「牠很輕」。

questions.stem: When would it be dangerous for a mosquito in the rain?

options.A: When it flies too close to the ground.
options.B: When the rain falls too hard and too fast.
options.C: When it is hit by raindrops too many times.
options.D: When it drops for more than 6 cm in the rain.

questions.explain: 文章最後一段說，如果蚊子飛得太低（太靠近地面），被雨滴打到時就來不及飛走，會直接摔到地上死掉，所以答案選 A：「飛得太靠近地面的時候」。

questions.stem: What do the three stories in the reading all talk about?
questions.passage:
You probably have heard Beethoven's famous piano piece "For Elise," but do you know who "Elise" was?

One story is that Elise was Elisabeth Röckel. She once sang in Beethoven's opera Fidelio, and the two became very good friends. Beethoven called her "Elise." When Röckel was leaving the city of Vienna, Beethoven wrote "For Elise" to say goodbye to her. Another story is that Elise was Therese Malfatti. Beethoven was in love with her. He wrote a piece for her and put "For Therese" on the manuscript. Malfatti kept the manuscript and left it to her family when she died. Later, a man copied the manuscript and wrote "For Elise" by mistake. There is another possible Elise: Elise Barensfeld. She learned the piano from Malfatti, the same Malfatti in the second story. To help Malfatti, Beethoven wrote the piece for her to use to teach Barensfeld.

These are just some guesses about who Elise was. We may never know the right answer. No one knew about the piece until 1867, when the manuscript was found. This was 40 years after Beethoven's death, so he wasn't around to tell us who Elise was. Only one thing is for sure: "For Elise" will always be enjoyed. (opera 歌劇 manuscript 手稿)
questions.explain: 文章講了三個關於貝多芬〈給愛麗絲〉的故事，每個故事都在猜「他到底是為了誰、為什麼寫這首曲子」，所以答案選 D。

questions.stem: What do we know about "For Elise" from the reading?

options.A: It first appeared in the opera Fidelio.
options.C: The manuscript was found after Beethoven died.
options.D: The manuscript is now kept by the city of Vienna.

questions.explain: 文章最後說，這份手稿是在1867年被找到的，而那時候貝多芬已經過世40年了，所以手稿是在他死後才被發現的，答案選 C。

questions.stem: What can we learn about the three women from the stories?

options.A: They were called "Elise" by their friends.
options.B: Elisabeth Röckel was a close friend of Beethoven's.
options.C: Therese Malfatti changed "For Elise" to "For Therese."

questions.stem: What idea does Jesse Cohen talk about in the reading?

options.A: Not all kinds of toys are helpful for children's learning.
options.B: Parents should give their children the right toys for their age.
options.C: Children should learn about their gender from playing with toys.
options.D: We should not let gender decide which toys children can play with.

questions.passage:
Which would you give a boy for his birthday, a toy truck or a doll? If you choose the truck, well, you're not alone. Most people think that some toys are for boys and some are for girls. We might pick building toys for boys and kitchen toys for girls. But why do we do this? Are boys and girls really born with different interests in toys?

No, they are not. Children start to show different interests in toys at age three, but only because at this time, they begin to learn about gender and to follow what others of their gender do. If you give a girl kitchen toys, she will think they are girls' toys because she sees other girls playing with them. But if you give a girl a toy car and show her a group of girls who are playing with cars, she'll play with the toy car and think it is a girls' toy. This "watch and follow" habit often becomes a rule for both boys and girls in choosing the "right" toys.

However, this rule can be bad for children. Different toys teach children different things. Dolls help with their early language use, and building toys are good for learning math and science. By playing with different toys, children can find out what they are interested in or what they are good at. If they only play one kind of toy, they may lose this important chance. They may also get a wrong idea about what boys or girls are able to do. Some doctors worry that children may use the same kind of thinking when they look for jobs in the future. If they do, they may miss something more important than just getting a chance to play with different toys. They may miss many, perhaps better, chances in life. (gender 性別)
questions.explain: 整篇文章一直在說，不應該用「男生女生」來決定小孩可以玩什麼玩具，所以答案選 D。

questions.stem: In the third paragraph, why does Jesse Cohen think "this rule can be bad for children"?

options.A: Children may not want to share their toys with others.
options.B: Children may have the wrong idea about following rules.
options.C: Children may not be able to learn from playing with toys.
options.D: Children may miss the chance to find out what they really like.

questions.stem: In the reading, after Jesse Cohen talks about an idea, he often gives an example to make it clear. Below are four of the sentences in the third paragraph. Which is used as an example?

options.A: "However, this rule can be bad for children."
options.B: "Dolls help with their early language use, and building toys are good for learning math and science."
options.C: "Some doctors worry that children may use the same kind of thinking when they look for jobs in the future."
options.D: "If they do, they may miss something more important than just getting a chance to play with different toys."

questions.explain: 前面說「不同的玩具會教小孩不同的東西」是一個想法，接下來用「娃娃可以幫助學說話、積木可以幫助學數學科學」這句話具體舉例說明，所以答案選 B。

questions.stem: What is the reading mostly about?
questions.passage:
Marie Colvin was a world-famous war reporter. She was born in New York, USA, in 1956 and died in Homs, Syria, in 2012.

When she was a student at Yale University, Colvin decided that she wanted to be a reporter in the future. She joined the Sunday Times, a UK newspaper, in 1985 and soon became its Middle East reporter. Besides the Middle East, she also reported from other parts of the world: Chechnya, Kosovo and Sri Lanka, for example. When she was reporting on the war in Sri Lanka, she lost her left eye. But that never stopped her. She still went to dangerous places that few reporters wanted to visit and stayed when others left.

Colvin showed great empathy in her stories. For her, war reporting was not just some quick interviews and a few photos. She wrote about real people and their real lives. Her writing made you feel so strongly about these people's stories, just like they were your own. She hoped that through her work the world would understand what really happened in war, and would take action to stop the fighting.

On February 22, 2012, the day after she gave her report on the war in Homs, Colvin was killed when the house she was staying in was attacked. She always knew what price a war reporter might have to pay. She already lost an eye before. This time, she gave her life to the work she could not give up — telling the true stories to the world. (war 戰爭)
questions.explain: 整篇文章從她大學決定當記者開始，一路寫到她最後因為採訪戰爭而過世，講的是她「一生的工作經歷」，所以答案選 B。

questions.stem: What does it mean when someone has empathy?

options.A: They are good at taking war photos.
options.B: They like to read other people's life stories.
options.C: They are able to understand how other people feel.
options.D: They are good at helping people change their own lives.

questions.explain: empathy 是「同理心」的意思，就是能懂別人的感受。文章說她寫的東西會讓讀者感覺這些人的故事就像自己的一樣，所以答案選 C：「能理解別人的感受」。

questions.stem: Which is true about Marie Colvin?

options.A: The chance to study at Yale made her leave her job in the Middle East.
options.B: The experience in Sri Lanka did not change her way of reporting news.
options.C: She was killed in Homs when she was interviewing soldiers there for her report.
options.D: She was not sure what to do in the future until she worked for the Sunday Times.

questions.explain: 文章說她在斯里蘭卡失去左眼之後，還是繼續去危險的地方採訪，沒有改變她報導的方式，所以答案選 B。

questions.stem: I just love Josh Katz's new book! It's about ______ in different parts of the US.

options.D: what words are often spelled differently

questions.passage:
Speaking American is a great book! Lucia Leisure ★★★★★
I just love Josh Katz's new book! It's about ______(42) in different parts of the US. The information in the book was collected from the quiz that Katz built on the website in 2013. The quiz asked what words people use for something, and their answers showed interesting facts. For example, there are different names for sports shoes. In most parts of the US, people say "tennis shoes." In the northeast of the country, people wear "sneakers." In some towns, "tennis shoes" and "sneakers" become "gym shoes."

The use of words tells us where people are from, like Katz's book says. But in my experience, it also shows ______(43). My family has lived in New York since my grandparents. It's not surprising that I say "sneakers." But my grandfather says "plimsolls," and my daughter says "kicks" or "vans." Maybe Katz can study this difference for his next book. (website 網站)
questions.explain: 後面舉例說，運動鞋在美國不同地方有不同的講法（tennis shoes、sneakers、gym shoes），可以知道這本書在講「東西在不同地方被叫做什麼名字」，所以答案選 A。

questions.stem: The use of words tells us where people are from, like Katz's book says. But in my experience, it also shows ______.

options.D: whether they are men or women

questions.passage:
Speaking American is a great book! Lucia Leisure ★★★★★
I just love Josh Katz's new book! It's about ______(42) in different parts of the US. The information in the book was collected from the quiz that Katz built on the website in 2013. The quiz asked what words people use for something, and their answers showed interesting facts. For example, there are different names for sports shoes. In most parts of the US, people say "tennis shoes." In the northeast of the country, people wear "sneakers." In some towns, "tennis shoes" and "sneakers" become "gym shoes."

The use of words tells us where people are from, like Katz's book says. But in my experience, it also shows ______(43). My family has lived in New York since my grandparents. It's not surprising that I say "sneakers." But my grandfather says "plimsolls," and my daughter says "kicks" or "vans." Maybe Katz can study this difference for his next book. (website 網站)
questions.explain: 作者說，用詞不只能看出一個人「來自哪裡」，還能看出別的東西。她舉例說，阿公、她自己、女兒都住紐約，卻用不同的字叫運動鞋（阿公說 plimsolls、她說 sneakers、女兒說 kicks），這是因為「年紀不同、世代不同」，所以答案選 A：「年紀多大」。

questions.stem: The movie starts at two o'clock, ______ let's meet at the theater at one forty-five.
questions.explain: 電影兩點開始，所以「我們一點四十五在戲院見」——前面是原因，後面是結果，這種「所以」的意思用 so，選 A。

questions.stem: Peter is afraid of the dark. He even leaves the ______ on when sleeping.
questions.explain: Peter 怕黑，所以睡覺時會開著「燈」，leave the lights on 就是「讓燈開著」，所以選 C。

questions.stem: Pam is a ______ baseball player; she has more fans than any other player on her team.
questions.explain: Pam 的球迷比隊上任何人都多，代表她很「受歡迎」，這個字是 popular，所以選 C。

questions.stem: I did not do my homework, so my teacher said I ______ stay after school to finish it.
questions.explain: 因為沒寫作業，老師說他「不得不」留下來把作業寫完，這種「不得不」的意思是 had to，所以選 B。

questions.stem: Kevin has only enough money for the bag or the shoes. That is a hard ______ to make because he likes them both.
questions.explain: Kevin 兩個都喜歡，但錢只夠買一個，這是很難做的「選擇」，make a choice 就是做選擇，所以選 A。

questions.stem: It was ______ for us to answer the math question because we've done the same kind of questions many times.
questions.explain: 因為已經做過很多同樣類型的題目了，所以回答數學題對他們來說很「容易」，這個字是 easy，所以選 B。

questions.stem: Although it took me lots of time ______ a big meal for ten people, I was happy that everyone enjoyed it.
questions.explain: it took me lots of time to prepare a big meal 是說「花了我很多時間去準備一頓大餐」，這種「花時間去做某件事」的句型，後面要接 to + 動詞，to prepare，所以選 B。

questions.stem: Don't let the children swim in the river. We don't know how ______ it is. It could be dangerous.
questions.explain: 不知道河水有多深，才會覺得游泳危險，這個字是 deep（深），所以選 A。（far 遠、long 長、thick 厚，都跟「水的危險程度」沒有直接關係）

questions.stem: Bob is ______ of the boys in the family. He never does any housework. His brothers at least take out the garbage sometimes.
questions.explain: of the boys in the family 是說「家裡的男孩子當中」，這是三個人以上一起比較，要用「最...的」這種說法，the laziest 就是最懶的，所以選 D。

questions.stem: Aunt Gina has lived in this town for more than sixty years, so she ______ it very well.
questions.explain: Aunt Gina 已經住在這個小鎮六十幾年了，所以她現在「很了解」這裡，這種講「現在的事實」的句子用現在式 knows，所以選 C。

questions.stem: We won't see the sun even after the typhoon leaves, because the news said that heavier rain will soon ______.
questions.explain: 颱風走了以後，新聞說更大的雨「接著」會來，這個字是 follow（跟著來、接續發生），所以選 B。

questions.stem: Yesterday when I got home from work, my brother ______ for dinner, so he invited me to join him.
questions.explain: 我下班回到家的那個時候，剛好看到弟弟「正要」出門吃晚餐，這種「過去某個時刻正在做的事」，用 was going out，所以選 D。

questions.stem: You were ______ not to lend Amy money. She never gives back what she borrows.
questions.explain: Amy 借錢都不還，所以不借給她是很「聰明、明智」的做法，這個字是 wise，所以選 C。

questions.stem: Have you found a summer job yet? Mr. Firth ______ someone to take care of his kids during the vacation. Maybe you can talk to him.
questions.explain: 現在正在找人幫忙照顧小孩，這種「現在正在做」的事情用 is looking for，所以選 B。

questions.stem: David looked out of the balcony window and saw a woman get in his car ______ away.
questions.explain: 看到一個女人上了他的車，然後「開走」了——這是用眼睛看到的兩個連續動作（上車、開走），像這種看到的一連串動作，兩個動作用 and 連起來，就是 and drive，所以選 C。

questions.stem: The police haven't found the little girl who ______ at a supermarket. They'll keep doing all they can to find her.
questions.explain: 小女孩是「被」帶走的，不是自己走的，這種「被…做了什麼」的說法，加上這是過去發生的事，所以用 was taken away，選 D。

questions.stem: Buses to the airport only come once every hour, and we just missed ______. Why don't we take a taxi?
questions.explain: 公車一小時才一班，我們剛好錯過「其中一班」，這裡的 one 是指某一班公車（不特定的哪一班都可以），所以選 C。

questions.stem: Ariel ______ every night for a week before her Chinese test and got a very good grade.
questions.explain: 考試前一個星期，Ariel 每天晚上都讀書，這是過去已經做完的事，用過去式 studied，所以選 A。

questions.stem:
While reading this story, Brad saw the word "trolling" and didn't know what it meant.
  Josh turned off the screen and sat back. "Why are they trolling me like this?" He didn't understand. They wanted him to share what he thought about the show, and he did. And now look what he got. In the end, all they wanted was nice words.
He found several meanings of the word in a dictionary. Which one should Brad choose?

options.B: To make someone or something move around.
options.C: To pull a fishing line through the water, often from a boat.
options.D: To write something on the Internet to hurt someone or make them angry.

questions.explain: 故事裡 Josh 分享了自己對節目的看法，結果卻在網路上被人攻擊、講難聽的話，這種在網路上寫東西傷害人、讓人生氣的行為，就是 trolling，所以選 D。

questions.stem: Why did Darrell tell Marina to go to Pinterest?

options.A: To find some examples for her homework.
options.B: To shop for things that are needed for art classes.
options.C: To meet new friends who have the same interests.
options.D: To share her works and tell people how they are made.

questions.passage:
Darrell : Marina…Marina…MARINA!
Marina : Oh, sorry. I didn't hear you. I was thinking about my homework.
Darrell : What's it about?
Marina : Well, I need to draw a future house for my art class, but I haven't got any ideas. Maybe I should go to the library and look for something useful.
Darrell : Or you can try Pinterest.
Marina : Isn't it a shopping app?
Darrell : Not really. Many people share their works on Pinterest and tell you how they made them. I'm sure you can get some ideas there.
Marina : Sounds like you use it often.
Darrell : Yeah. Just last week I went there and found the A to Z of making chocolate cake—from choosing good chocolate to baking the cake to making sugar flowers on top.
Marina : Really? I'll check it out later. Thanks a lot.
questions.explain: Darrell 建議 Marina 去 Pinterest 找靈感，因為上面有很多人分享自己的作品、還會教你怎麼做，Marina 可以參考這些例子來完成她的美術作業，所以選 A。

questions.stem: What does it mean when you learn something from A to Z?

options.A: You can learn it at any time.
options.B: You learn it in a baking class.
options.D: You spend all your life learning it.

questions.passage:

Marina : Oh, sorry. I didn't hear you. I was thinking about my homework.
Darrell : What's it about?
Marina : Well, I need to draw a future house for my art class, but I haven't got any ideas. Maybe I should go to the library and look for something useful.
Darrell : Or you can try Pinterest.
Marina : Isn't it a shopping app?
Darrell : Not really. Many people share their works on Pinterest and tell you how they made them. I'm sure you can get some ideas there.
Marina : Sounds like you use it often.
Darrell : Yeah. Just last week I went there and found the A to Z of making chocolate cake—from choosing good chocolate to baking the cake to making sugar flowers on top.
Marina : Really? I'll check it out later. Thanks a lot.
questions.explain: from A to Z 意思是「從頭到尾、完完整整地」學會一件事的每個步驟，所以選 C。

questions.stem: Which idea is talked about in the first paragraph of the reading?

options.A: How you should do Tabata training.
options.B: What is the best time for Tabata training.
options.C: Who first had the idea of Tabata training.
options.D: How often you should do Tabata training.

questions.passage:
Tabata training is a very popular way of exercising these days. It doesn't take much time or space, and it burns calories faster than other ways of exercising. The idea of Tabata training is simple: exercise for 20 seconds, rest for 10 seconds, and then repeat (at least eight times). The moves for the 20-second exercise are not difficult to learn. Jumping jacks, high knees, squats, and planks are some of the most common moves. You can decide yourself what moves to do in your Tabata training. For example, you can do more leg exercises if you want strong legs.

One great thing about Tabata training is that your body will keep burning calories for at least an hour after 4 minutes of Tabata training. But to have this wonderful "afterburn," you need to exercise really hard during each 20 seconds. If you seldom exercise or have heart problems, this exercise may not be good for you. But for people who enjoy exercising but are too busy to go to the gym, Tabata training might just be the answer.
questions.explain: 第一段在說明 Tabata 訓練「該怎麼做」——運動 20 秒、休息 10 秒、重複做、可以做哪些動作，所以選 A。

questions.stem: Who might find that Tabata training is right for them?

options.B: People who want to start exercising.
options.C: People who want to fix their heart problems.
options.D: People who already have a habit of exercising.

questions.explain: 文章說，很少運動或有心臟問題的人不適合這種訓練，比較適合「已經有運動習慣、但太忙沒空上健身房」的人，所以選 D。

questions.stem: Which is true about Tabata training?

options.A: It is difficult to learn the moves.
options.B: You are free to choose your own moves.
options.C: You need a large space to do the exercises.
options.D: You cannot rest between moves if you want the afterburn.

questions.stem:
There are four important points in the report:
a. What "No Overtime Day" is
b. Why "No Overtime Day" fails
c. Why there is "No Overtime Day" in the country
d. How workers deal with "No Overtime Day"
How are they ordered in the report?
questions.passage:
The problem of long working hours is a real headache in our country. It kills many people every year (see Figure 1), and many companies are trying to change that. They have at least one "No Overtime Day" in a week, and it is often Wednesday. On "No Overtime Day," workers are asked to leave their offices before 8 p.m. At 8 p.m., companies play the song "There's Always Tomorrow" and turn off all the lights.
     However, after the workers walk out of their offices, they don't go home. Some go back later, turn on their table lamps, and keep working, and others find somewhere else to work until very late at night. Restaurants and coffee shops are busier on Wednesday evenings, and they have started selling drinks and meals for these workers.
     But why do these people keep working? A study shows that workers who work more hours often have a bigger chance to get paid more and rise higher in their companies. This is true for both men and women (see Figure 2). Working long hours has become a way to show that people are hard-working. Clearly, this must be changed before the problem gets any worse.
questions.explain: 文章先說為什麼會有「不加班日」(c)，接著說明不加班日是什麼、怎麼做(a)，然後說員工下班後怎麼應對、還是繼續工作(d)，最後說明為什麼這個政策沒有用、失敗了(b)。照順序排就是 c、a、d、b，所以選 D。

questions.stem: What does this mean in the report?

options.A: The way workers show they are hard-working.
options.B: Restaurants and coffee shops open until very late at night.
options.C: Workers do not go home when they walk out of the office.
options.D: The number of workers who get paid more and rise higher in the company.

questions.passage:

questions.explain: 最後一句「這件事必須被改變」裡的 this，指的是前一句講的「長時間工作變成大家用來證明自己很認真、很拚」這件事，所以選 A。

questions.stem: What does resentful mean in the reading?
questions.passage:
Language and Power: Cameroon's Story
By Elisa Grant, 2 Oct 2017

In the modern world, being able to speak English is often a way to get power. However, this is not true for English speakers in Cameroon. To know why things are different in this African country, we need to go back one hundred years.

In 1919, Kamerun (the name of Cameroon at the time) was cut into two parts: one belonged to France, and the other belonged to the UK. In 1960, the French Cameroon became the Republic of Cameroun, and the UK's part joined it in 1961. The new country changed its name to the Federal Republic of Cameroon. Although both French and English are Cameroon's official languages, only around 20% of its people speak English. Also, the government has been in French speakers' hands since the two parts became one country.

For a long time, Cameroon's English speakers have felt they are not welcome in their own country. For example, it is very difficult for them to find jobs in the government, and they are often asked to speak French in business and at official events. They have become more resentful at the government and decided to fight for themselves. Since last year, the English speakers have tried to build their own country. Yesterday, they said in a public meeting that they were not part of Cameroon anymore. They are now a new country, "Ambazonia." When the police tried to stop the meeting, at least eight people were killed.
questions.explain: 文章說喀麥隆的英語族群長期覺得不被歡迎，最後決定挺身反抗政府，resentful 這個字指的是對政府感到「生氣、不滿」，最接近 Angry（生氣的），所以選 B。

questions.stem: What does Cameroon's government most likely think of Ambazonia?

options.A: It plans to do business with Ambazonia.
options.B: It needs Ambazonia for money and help.
options.C: It does not want to be part of Ambazonia.
options.D: It does not agree that Ambazonia is a country.

questions.explain: 文章最後說，警察想阻止英語族群開會宣布獨立，結果造成人員死傷，這代表政府「不承認」Ambazonia 是一個國家，所以選 D。

questions.stem: What does Elisa Grant try to tell readers by talking about the history of Cameroon?

options.A: Why speaking English is a way to get power.
options.B: Why English speakers in Cameroon have less power.
options.C: Why the two parts of Cameroon became one country.
options.D: Why only 20% of the people in Cameroon speak English.

questions.explain: 作者花時間講喀麥隆的歷史，是要告訴讀者：為什麼在自己國家裡，說英語的人反而權力比較小，所以選 B。

questions.stem: What is recommended to people who are visiting the Southend Trail?

options.C: Hiking one part of the trail a day.
options.D: Visiting the museum in the morning.

questions.passage:
THE SOUTHEND TRAIL has been popular with nature lovers for years. The 120-km trail passes beautiful lakes, crosses rivers, and goes through mountains and hills. This gives bird lovers some of the best places for birdwatching. The trail also takes you to the Southend Museum and to two famous castles, Edward Castle and Sloan Castle.
It's best to plan one day for one part of the trail and start your hike early in the morning, because each part takes at least seven hours. If you don't want to walk, you can bike, but make sure you stay on the main trail, because the side trails are not wide enough for biking.
There are two types of lodging for visitors. Camping is popular in summer, but it is only allowed on a few campgrounds (see the map). A more comfortable one is to stay at a hotel in one of the towns. You can find some very nice ones that also have breakfast.
questions.explain: 文章建議大家一天只走步道的「一段」，而且要一大早出發，因為每一段至少要走七個小時，所以選 C。

questions.stem: What does lodging mean in the reading?
questions.passage:
THE SOUTHEND TRAIL has been popular with nature lovers for years. The 120-km trail passes beautiful lakes, crosses rivers, and goes through mountains and hills. This gives bird lovers some of the best places for birdwatching. The trail also takes you to the Southend Museum and to two famous castles, Edward Castle and Sloan Castle.
It's best to plan one day for one part of the trail and start your hike early in the morning, because each part takes at least seven hours. If you don't want to walk, you can bike, but make sure you stay on the main trail, because the side trails are not wide enough for biking.
There are two types of lodging for visitors. Camping is popular in summer, but it is only allowed on a few campgrounds (see the map). A more comfortable one is to stay at a hotel in one of the towns. You can find some very nice ones that also have breakfast.
questions.explain: lodging 後面接的是露營和住旅館這兩種選擇，可以知道這個字指的是「住宿的地方」，所以選 B。

questions.stem: A palindrome is a word or a sentence that reads the same from left to right or from right to left, ______, "eye," "Bob," "my gym," and "Was it a car or a cat I saw?"
questions.passage:
English words are made of 26 letters, and palindromes and anagrams are two kinds of word games about spelling. A palindrome is a word or a sentence that reads the same from left to right or from right to left, __40__, "eye," "Bob," "my gym," and "Was it a car or a cat I saw?" An anagram of a word or words is made by putting the letters of the word or words in a different way. Look at the words and their anagrams below. Can you think of other possible anagrams of these words?

Anagrams are often longer words that don't really mean anything but are fun to say. Sometimes they can even mean something __42__, like when a common word, "restaurant," becomes "Eat rats, run!" Actually, palindromes and anagrams are __43__. Palindromes can be used to learn mathematics and make music. Anagrams are also a good way to hide something. In history, people often hid their important studies in anagrams. Can you think of any other way to use them?
questions.explain: 空格後面舉了 eye、Bob 這些例子，用來「舉例說明」前面說的東西，for example 就是「舉例來說」，所以選 D。

questions.stem: Sometimes they can even mean something ______, like when a common word, "restaurant," becomes "Eat rats, run!"

questions.explain: restaurant（餐廳）這個字重新排列字母後變成 Eat rats, run!（快逃，吃老鼠！），意思變得很「奇怪」，所以選 A。

questions.stem: Actually, palindromes and anagrams are ______. Palindromes can be used to learn mathematics and make music. Anagrams are also a good way to hide something.

options.D: not first used to learn words

questions.explain: 後面接著說，回文可以用來學數學、做音樂，重組字還可以用來藏祕密訊息，這些都說明回文和重組字「不只是遊戲而已」，所以選 A。

questions.stem: Listen! The baby ______ in the bedroom. Why don't you go in and take a look?
questions.explain: Listen（聽）這個字告訴我們，現在正在發生的事。寶寶現在正在房間裡哭，這種「現在正在做」的事情，英文要說 is crying（正在哭），所以答案選 C。

questions.stem: Jill is ______ that the city park is closed for the music festival because now she can't jog there.
questions.explain: Jill 因為公園關閉不能去慢跑，心情不好、不開心，不開心的英文是 unhappy，所以選 D。（excited 興奮、proud 驕傲、scared 害怕，都不符合她的心情）

questions.stem: Steven wants to be a ______, because he loves to watch people enjoy the food he prepares.
questions.explain: Steven 喜歡看別人把他煮的東西吃得很開心，這種職業就是廚師 cook，所以選 A。（doctor 醫生、driver 司機、farmer 農夫都跟做菜沒關係）

questions.stem: Paul misses his parents a lot. He ______ them since he came to work in Taiwan a year ago.
questions.explain: since he came to work in Taiwan a year ago 是說「自從一年前他來台灣工作」，這種「從以前一直到現在」的句子，英文要用 have/has + 過去分詞，像 hasn't seen（到現在都還沒見到），所以答案選 C。

questions.stem: Our teacher Ms. Wu seldom laughs, but when she ______, everyone in the same building can hear her.
questions.explain: 這句話的意思是「我們老師很少笑，但她一笑的時候，整棟樓的人都聽得到」。when she does 這裡的 does 是用來代替前面的 laughs（笑），不用重複講一次 laughs，直接用 does 代替就好，所以答案選 B。

questions.stem: My sister is coming to my home today. She ______ with me for a week.
questions.explain: She ______ with me for a week 是說「她要來我家住一個星期」，這件事還沒發生、是以後才會發生的事，所以用未來式 will stay（將會住），選 D。

questions.stem: Edward had worked as a computer engineer for ten years. This ______ helped him a lot when he started his own computer shop.
questions.explain: Edward 當了十年電腦工程師，這些累積下來的本事幫他後來開店，這叫做「經驗」experience，所以選 B。（chance 機會、hobby 興趣、knowledge 知識都不是十年工作累積下來最直接的東西）

questions.stem: If you're interested in our business plan, ______ this number and ask for Ms. Lee. She'll answer your questions.
questions.explain: If you're interested..., ______ this number 是在「請你做一件事」（打這個電話號碼），像這種「請你做」的句子，前面直接用動詞開頭就好，call this number（打這個電話），所以選 B。

questions.stem: Jimmy would not get up for breakfast, ______ his dad had already tried to pull him from his bed several times.
questions.explain: Jimmy 的爸爸已經拉他下床好幾次了，可是 Jimmy 還是不肯起床吃早餐——前後兩件事剛好相反（爸爸努力叫他 vs 他還是不起床），這種「雖然…但是…」的意思，英文用 although，所以選 A。

questions.stem: Duncan spent all his money trying to ______ the bookstore his mom left him. Sadly, the business never got better, and he had to close it in the end.
questions.explain: 書店是媽媽留給 Duncan 的，他花光所有的錢想讓書店繼續開下去，這是在「挽救」save 這家店，所以選 C。（build 蓋、buy 買、start 開始，都跟「挽救一家已經存在的店」意思不合）

questions.stem: Fiona loves listening to her children sing songs ______ at school.
questions.explain: 這句話是說「Fiona 喜歡聽小孩唱在學校學的歌」，songs they learned at school 意思就是「他們在學校學的歌」，直接把 they learned 放在 songs 後面說明是什麼歌，中間可以省略掉 that，所以選 C。

questions.stem: Beverly eats lots of snacks ______ meals. That's why she is often too full to eat anything at mealtimes.
questions.explain: Beverly 常常在正餐「中間」吃零食，所以正式吃飯時反而吃不下，between meals 就是「兩餐之間」，所以選 B。

questions.stem: Nora: Can I check your drawer for some tools we can use? Matt: Sure. Take a look. See if you can find ______ in there.
questions.explain: Nora 要找工具，但不確定抽屜裡有什麼、有多少，這種「不特定、隨便什麼都可以」的說法用 any，所以選 A。

questions.stem: What is this ad for?

options.A: Teaching people how to use their BeautiMed points.
options.B: Telling people they can shop at BeautiMed on the Internet now.
options.C: Inviting people to share their shopping experience at e-BeautiMed.
options.D: Letting people know what is on sale at e-BeautiMed from 7/1 to 8/31.

questions.passage:
We've prepared many wonderful surprises for you at e-BeautiMed, BeautiMed's online store!

Surprise 1: Join "BeautiMed Friend" online for free and get 2000 BeautiMed points!

Surprise 2: Already a "BeautiMed Friend"? Come collect your free gift by entering your "BeautiMed Friend" card number at e-BeautiMed before 8/31.

Surprise 3: Get 500 BeautiMed points by shopping at e-BeautiMed and you'll get 500 more points if you spend more than $1,000.

Surprise 4: Shop at e-BeautiMed during 7/1-8/31 and you may win $5,000 or free movie tickets!

Surprise 5: Invite a friend to join "BeautiMed Friend" and you both can get 1000 points each!
questions.explain: 這則廣告在說 e-BeautiMed 這個網路商店開幕了，裡面提到好幾個「驚喜」，都是講加入會員、線上購物就能拿點數，整篇重點就是告訴大家「現在可以上網買 BeautiMed 的東西了」，所以選 B。

questions.stem: What will NOT get you BeautiMed points?

options.C: Inviting a friend to join "BeautiMed Friend."

questions.passage:
We've prepared many wonderful surprises for you at e-BeautiMed, BeautiMed's online store!

Surprise 1: Join "BeautiMed Friend" online for free and get 2000 BeautiMed points!

Surprise 2: Already a "BeautiMed Friend"? Come collect your free gift by entering your "BeautiMed Friend" card number at e-BeautiMed before 8/31.

Surprise 3: Get 500 BeautiMed points by shopping at e-BeautiMed and you'll get 500 more points if you spend more than $1,000.

Surprise 4: Shop at e-BeautiMed during 7/1-8/31 and you may win $5,000 or free movie tickets!

Surprise 5: Invite a friend to join "BeautiMed Friend" and you both can get 1000 points each!
questions.explain: 廣告裡 Surprise 2 說輸入會員卡號是去領「免費禮物」，不是拿點數；Surprise 1、3、5 分別講加入會員、線上購物、邀朋友，這三種都可以拿到點數，只有「輸入卡號」沒有點數，所以選 D。

questions.stem: What can we learn about Lynn from the dialogue?

options.B: She likes to try new food.
options.C: She did not enjoy the food at Caldron.
options.D: She changed Joey's idea about Caldron.

questions.passage:
Joey: Lynn, have you tried Caldron? I've heard that restaurant is good.
Lynn: I have. I went there once for Tom's party.
Joey: And?
Lynn: Well, I never thought I would have to cook for myself when eating out.
Joey: But it's fun to cook your own food.
Lynn: I don't see any fun in that. Besides, I want nice food.
Joey: So you didn't like the food there?
Lynn: Everything tasted the same. I mean, they were all cooked in the same boiling pot. How could they taste different? I just kept throwing strange things into the pot. I didn't even know what half the things were made of.
Joey: Don't worry. Everything should be safe to eat after a hot bath in the pot. And you didn't get sick after that, right? Besides, you tried something different there. Still a good experience, I think?
Lynn: I don't think we share the same idea of good experiences.
questions.explain: Lynn 說每一樣東西吃起來都一樣，因為全部都丟進同一鍋滾水裡煮，她還說自己不覺得煮東西好玩。這些話都表示她不喜歡 Caldron 餐廳的食物，所以選 C。

questions.stem: What do we know about Woollie from Katie's diary?

options.B: Woollie was taken to visit sick kids.
options.C: Woollie was sick when he ran away.
options.D: Woollie looked strange without his fleece.

questions.passage:
Dear Diary,                    June 7, 2011
    I was very sad to hear what happened to Woollie yesterday. Woollie was my favorite sheep. I have a storybook about Woollie. It says when Woollie was four, he ran away from Mr. Armstrong's farm, and it took Mr. Armstrong six years to find Woollie. But Woollie had grown so much fleece that he didn't even look like a sheep. Mr. Armstrong decided to cut his fleece on TV so everyone could see. Daddy said this happened on the day I was born. Woollie's fleece weighed 27 kg, and could make clothes for 20 large men. I asked Daddy if his favorite vest was made from Woollie's fleece. Daddy said no, because Woollie's fleece was sold to collect money for sick kids. In the storybook, Woollie was taken to see Ms. Stella Clark, the leader of our city then. They even had tea together. Daddy said Woollie did meet Ms. Clark, but he wasn't sure if they had tea. Daddy said Woollie was put down because he was too sick and there was no way to help him. I cried. I hope Woollie will be happy up there in the sky.
questions.explain: 故事說 Woollie 四歲的時候跑走，過了六年才被找到、剪毛上電視，而剪毛那天剛好是 Katie 出生的那天。這樣算一算，Woollie 剪毛的時候已經十歲了，比剛出生的 Katie 大很多，所以選 A。

questions.stem: What happened when Woollie was put down?
questions.passage:
Dear Diary,                    June 7, 2011
    I was very sad to hear what happened to Woollie yesterday. Woollie was my favorite sheep. I have a storybook about Woollie. It says when Woollie was four, he ran away from Mr. Armstrong's farm, and it took Mr. Armstrong six years to find Woollie. But Woollie had grown so much fleece that he didn't even look like a sheep. Mr. Armstrong decided to cut his fleece on TV so everyone could see. Daddy said this happened on the day I was born. Woollie's fleece weighed 27 kg, and could make clothes for 20 large men. I asked Daddy if his favorite vest was made from Woollie's fleece. Daddy said no, because Woollie's fleece was sold to collect money for sick kids. In the storybook, Woollie was taken to see Ms. Stella Clark, the leader of our city then. They even had tea together. Daddy said Woollie did meet Ms. Clark, but he wasn't sure if they had tea. Daddy said Woollie was put down because he was too sick and there was no way to help him. I cried. I hope Woollie will be happy up there in the sky.
questions.explain: put down 這個說法，是指一隻動物病得太重、沒辦法醫治，只好讓牠安詳地離開。Katie 因為這件事哭了，還說希望 Woollie 在天上快樂，這些都在說 Woollie 死掉了，所以選 C。

questions.stem: What can we learn about The Piano Lesson from the reading?

options.A: Matisse painted it when his son was away from home.
options.B: The woman who sits on a seat behind the boy is his mother.
options.C: Matisse uses yellow and red to show that his son was happy.
options.D: The candle on the piano shows that the piano lesson has just begun.

questions.passage:
Here's The Piano Lesson, one of Henri Matisse's most famous paintings. The boy in the picture is the painter's son, Pierre. In the picture, Pierre looks quite serious. Why? Well, look at the yellow candle on the red piano. It's almost burned down. So maybe Pierre has already practiced for hours. And Matisse uses a lot of gray, not a very fun color, right? It shows how the poor kid feels, don't you think?
    On the top right corner, you can see a woman sitting straight on a seat. Is she Pierre's piano teacher? Or his mother? Well, if you're a Matisse fan, you'll know this is in fact another painting by him, Woman on a High Stool. Interesting, right? Matisse likes to do that a lot. In fact, there's another work by Matisse in the picture. Did you find it? Right! The sculpture on the bottom left corner!
    Matisse painted this picture in 1916. Pierre was already 16 then, but the artist painted his son much younger than he really was. Pierre had gone away to be a soldier, and Matisse didn't know whether he would come back. Maybe this is a worried father's way to remember the good old days.
questions.explain: 文章最後說 Pierre 已經離家去當兵了，畫家 Matisse 是在兒子不在身邊的時候畫下這幅畫的，所以選 A。

questions.stem: What does do that mean in the reading?

options.A: Put his family in his paintings.
options.B: Paint to remember the good old days.
options.C: Use a lot of fun colors in his art works.
options.D: Put his earlier art work in a new painting.

questions.passage:

questions.explain: 文章說畫家很喜歡「把自己以前畫過的東西，放進新畫裡」，這裡指的就是他把之前畫的《Woman on a High Stool》，畫進了《鋼琴課》這幅新畫裡，所以選 D。

questions.stem: What is NOT used in the reading to refer to Henri Matisse?
questions.passage:

questions.explain: 文章中「可憐的孩子」（the poor kid）指的是畫裡的兒子 Pierre，不是畫家 Matisse 本人；the artist（藝術家）、the painter（畫家）、a worried father（擔心的爸爸）指的才是 Matisse，所以選 C。

questions.stem: What idea is talked about in the reading?

options.A: How Teachers' Day is celebrated now.
options.B: Why Teachers' Day was celebrated at first.
options.C: Why Confucius was seen as a great teacher.
options.D: How teachers today feel about Teachers' Day.

questions.passage:
On Teachers' Day, we thank our teachers for their hard work. We also celebrate this day to remember Confucius, the great Chinese teacher from 2,500 years ago. Well, that's everything we're taught about Teachers' Day. But, the first Teachers' Day was not on Confucius's birthday, and it was not about thanking our teachers.
    In 1930, Mr. Tai Shuang-qiu and other teachers celebrated the first Teachers' Day in Nanjing. But there was nothing to celebrate. Teachers were paid very little and not respected. These teachers took this chance to shout out their problems. They had Teachers' Day on June 6 because the date was easy to remember and near the end of the school year. This action by Mr. Tai and the other teachers was welcomed and followed by teachers from other cities. In 1939, the government made Teachers' Day a national holiday. However, it was on August 27, the day when Confucius's birthday was celebrated. Not everyone hailed this decision: Teachers' Day was never about Confucius, and Confucius couldn't speak for all the teachers either.
    One funny thing was that the government was wrong about the date of Confucius's birthday. In 1952, people found out he was in fact born on September 28. That was when we started to celebrate Teachers' Day on Confucius's real birthday.
questions.explain: 整篇文章在講教師節「一開始」為什麼會有——是老師們想爭取自己的權益，不是為了紀念孔子，所以選 B。

questions.stem: Which is true about Teachers' Day from the reading?

options.A: It used to be celebrated on different dates.
options.B: It was started by students for their teachers.
options.C: It has been celebrated in Nanjing for over 2,500 years.
options.D: Teachers decided to celebrate it on Confucius's birthday.

questions.passage:

questions.explain: 文章提到教師節本來訂在 6 月 6 日，後來政府改到 8 月 27 日（原本以為是孔子生日，其實算錯了），最後又改成 9 月 28 日，日期改了好幾次，所以選 A。

questions.stem: What does "not everyone hailed this decision" mean in the reading?

options.A: Not everyone cared about the decision.
options.C: Not everyone heard about the decision.

questions.passage:

questions.stem: What does the writer try to do with this reading?

options.A: To talk about a problem that no one notices.
options.B: To share a fun experience that was not common.
options.C: To tell a piece of history that few people know about.
options.D: To give an example of an idea that is hard to understand.

questions.passage:

questions.stem: Why does National Formosa Railway write this letter?

options.A: There is a train ticket sale.
options.B: A train line will be closed in July.
options.C: It will open a new train line in August.
options.D: Some of its trains will run on different lines.

questions.passage:
Dear Traveler,
    You have bought an NFR ticket online for a train that runs between White Water City and Cloud City.
    We are sorry to tell you that, because of the typhoon last month, the Sand Town – Spring Town line will be closed between August 14 and 28 2016. During this time, some trains will change lines and will not stop at a few stations. There will be free buses to take travelers to these stations. Please check below for the changes. Visit http://www.nfr.gov/ for more information.
    We wish you a nice summer.

Lena Muller
National Formosa Railway
questions.explain: 信裡說因為颱風，有一段鐵路要封閉，這段時間有些火車會改開別的路線、不停靠某些站，所以選 D。

questions.stem: Below are the ideas that are talked about in the reading. a. The problems Darayya has. b. How Darayya's library was started. c. What makes Darayya's library special. d. How Darayya's library helps people there. In what order does the writer put his ideas in the reading?
questions.passage:
In Darayya, a city in Syria, there's a library, and it has 15,000 books on almost every subject you can think of. However, it is different from any libraries you know: It is a secret underground library, and only people in Darayya know where it is.
    Over the years, war has shaken Darayya badly. Every day, houses are bombed and people are killed. Stores are closed one after another, and so are schools. To help the kids in Darayya with their learning, Anas Ahmad, a 19-year-old student, and his friends decided to build a library. They built the library under the ground to keep it safe from bombing. But it is dangerous to collect books for the library. Often, Ahmad and his friends look for books in houses that were bombed. They need to be careful because they may be killed in another bombing.
    You may ask, "In a place like Darayya, would people be interested in books?" "Just like the body needs food, the mind needs books," says one library user. In the library, people enjoy their time of reading and forget about the terrible world above, so their life doesn't seem so hard. Through reading, they are able to dream of a better life after war.
questions.explain: 第一段先說這個圖書館很特別（c：跟別的圖書館不一樣），第二段講戰爭帶來的問題（a）和圖書館怎麼蓋起來的（b），第三段講圖書館怎麼幫助大家（d）。照文章順序排下來就是 c、a、b、d，所以選 D。

questions.stem: Why do people in Darayya go to the library even during the war?

options.B: They find joy and hope in reading.
options.C: They want to learn how to win the war.
options.D: Their school teachers give lessons there.

questions.explain: 文章最後說，大家在圖書館裡享受閱讀的時光，暫時忘掉外面可怕的戰爭，還能夢想戰後更好的生活，所以選 B。

questions.stem: What do we know about Darayya's library?

options.A: It was built during the war.
options.B: It has been bombed many times.
options.C: It was built to remember people who died in the war.
options.D: Most of its books are collected from outside of Darayya.

questions.stem: We know we told you that the party would be in ______. However, we just moved to Carlton City last month, so the party won't be in Pattersons Town.
questions.passage:
Hello Dave,

We're happy to invite you and your family to Daniel's fifth birthday party this Saturday.

We know we told you that the party would be in   35  . However, we just moved to Carlton City last month, so the party won't be in Pattersons Town. It'll be at Mosman Garden right next to Victor Zoo, just across from our new apartment. Please don't go to Northbank Park next to our old house.

The weather report said that it may be rainy this weekend, but   36  . If it rains, we'll move the party inside our home. However, if we're   37   and it is fine, we'll still have the party at the garden.

Again, we're all very happy and excited to welcome you to join us for some fun this weekend!

Best,
Mia, Sam & Daniel
questions.explain: 後面說「請不要去舊家旁邊的 Northbank Park」，這句話代表大家原本以為派對是辦在 Northbank Park，所以空格要填 Northbank Park，選 D。

questions.stem: The weather report said that it may be rainy this weekend, but ______. If it rains, we'll move the party inside our home.

options.A: we have a plan if this happens
options.B: we could just wait for the rain to stop
options.C: we should be fine if the rain isn't too heavy
options.D: we will let you know if we'll still have the party

questions.stem: However, if we're ______ and it is fine, we'll still have the party at the garden.
questions.passage:

Best,

questions.explain: 要天氣放晴才能在花園辦派對，這種事要靠運氣，運氣好的英文是 lucky，所以選 C。

questions.stem: The stories I'd heard about the park ______ into my head at this moment. Anyone with a clear mind would just turn back.
questions.passage:
Usually I wouldn't cross the park at this time of night. But walking around the park would take more time, and it was raining so hard that I couldn't even see clearly what was right before me. So I entered the park. And that was the first stupid thing I did tonight.
    Soon after I walked into the park, I saw a man under a tree up ahead. My heart fell. The stories I'd heard about the park   38   into my head at this moment. Anyone with a clear mind would just turn back. But me? No. I did   39   stupid thing: I decided to hurry past him.
    Just when I was passing the man, he raised his head and gave me the strangest smile ever. I got scared and started running. "Hey!" the man shouted from behind. "Wait!" He was running after me!
    I ran like crazy. I had hoped the trees would keep some rain off. And they   40  . But they also made the park look even darker. I couldn't see what was ahead of me. Then, I ran into something. It was the man!
    "Don't kill me!" I cried.
    "What? I   41   to give back your bag! You dropped it," the man gave me my bag.
    Now, I felt saying that to the man was the stupidest thing I did tonight.
questions.explain: 整篇故事都是用「過去」的方式在講故事。這句話是說「我以前聽過的公園故事，這時候一個個湧上我的腦海」，這種「過去某個時刻正在發生」的事，要用 were all coming，選 C。

questions.stem: But me? No. I did ______ stupid thing: I decided to hurry past him.
questions.passage:

    "Don't kill me!" I cried.
    "What? I   41   to give back your bag! You dropped it," the man gave me my bag.
    Now, I felt saying that to the man was the stupidest thing I did tonight.
questions.explain: 前面已經做了「第一件」蠢事（走進公園），這裡又做了「另一件」蠢事，另一件的英文是 another，所以選 A。

questions.stem: I had hoped the trees would keep some rain off. And they ______. But they also made the park look even darker.

questions.explain: 前面說「我希望樹能幫忙擋一點雨」，這裡的 did 是用來代替前面的 kept some rain off（真的擋住了一些雨），不用重複講一次，所以選 B。

questions.stem: "What? I ______ to give back your bag! You dropped it," the man gave me my bag.
questions.passage:

questions.explain: 男子解釋自己剛才追上來，是因為「正想要」把包包還給主角，這是「過去某個時候正在做的事」，用 was trying，所以選 A。

questions.stem: My mom puts the ______ on to keep her hands away from water when she washes the dishes.
questions.explain: 洗碗的時候，為了不讓手碰到水，媽媽會戴上「手套」，gloves 就是手套的意思，戴在手上剛好符合句意，所以答案選 gloves。

questions.stem: Can you ______ your feet a little off the floor? I want to see if my keys are under the sofa.
questions.explain: 想看看沙發底下有沒有鑰匙，會請對方把腳「抬高」一點離開地板，raise 就是「抬高」的意思，所以答案選 raise。check 是「檢查」、kick 是「踢」、show 是「給人看」，都不是「抬起來」的意思。

questions.stem: ______ of my sisters are older than I am. I'm the youngest of the three children in my family.
questions.explain: 家裡三個小孩裡說話的人最小，代表另外兩個姊姊都比自己大，Both 是「兩者都」的意思，剛好符合兩個姊姊都比較大，所以答案選 Both。

questions.stem: The beach near our town is a very ______ place. Every summer at least one kid dies there in the water.
questions.explain: 每年夏天都至少有一個小孩在這片海邊的水裡喪命，可以知道這片海灘很「危險」，dangerous 就是危險的意思，所以答案選 dangerous。

questions.stem: My wife enjoys going to the movies with her friends, but I like to watch TV at home by ______.
questions.explain: by oneself 是「自己一個人」的意思，前面的主詞是 I，所以要用「我自己」，英文是 myself，例如 by myself（我自己一個人），所以答案選 myself。

questions.stem: For the past twenty years, my father ______ in a school library. But he'll leave the job next month.
questions.explain: For the past twenty years（過去二十年來）表示這件事從過去一直持續到現在，這種情況英文要用「現在完成式」has worked，所以答案選 has worked。

questions.stem: It's great that we can buy train tickets over the phone without going out. It ______ us a lot of time.
questions.explain: 不用出門就能買到火車票，替我們「省下」很多時間，save time 就是「省時間」的意思，所以答案選 saves。

questions.stem: Mozart ______ his first music when he was only six years old.
questions.explain: when he was only six years old（他只有六歲的時候）是過去一個很清楚的時間點，這種情況要用「過去式」wrote，所以答案選 wrote。

questions.stem:
Jill: Have you decided ______ you will celebrate your 30th birthday?
Sue: Yeah, I'm going to have a big barbecue party.
questions.explain: Sue 回答說要辦一個烤肉派對，這是在說「要用什麼方式」慶生，所以要問 how（怎麼樣、用什麼方式），答案選 how。

questions.stem: Alison doesn't like ______ what to do. She only does things she wants to do.
questions.explain: Alison 不喜歡「被告知」要做什麼，因為她只做自己想做的事。「被告知」用英文表示是 be told，前面加 to 就是 to be told，所以答案選 to be told。

questions.stem: Josh has planned to make a trip to New York and ______ some of his friends there.
questions.explain: 這句話是說 Josh 計畫「去紐約旅行」和「拜訪一些朋友」，make a trip 跟 visit 是兩個並列的動作，前面已經用了原形的 make，所以後面也要用原形的 visit，所以答案選 visit。

questions.stem: Ms. Johnson has been taking phone calls since she entered the office this morning. Just when she thought she could finally leave work, ______ call came in.
questions.explain: 她一直在接電話，正想說終於可以下班了，結果「又」進來一通電話。another 就是「再一通、又一通」的意思，所以答案選 another。

questions.stem: We were so sure that Jerry ______ well on the difficult job. His past experience in other work showed he was the right guy for it. So when he failed, no one believed it.
questions.explain: 這句話是站在過去的時間點，去猜「以後」Jerry 會不會做得好，這種「過去對未來的猜測」英文要用 would do，所以答案選 would do。

questions.stem: For Mike, the price is ______ important thing when he shops for jeans. He cares even more about the shape and the size of the pockets.
questions.explain: 後面說他更在意版型和口袋的大小，代表「價格」對他來說是「最不」重要的事，the least 是「最少、最不」的意思，所以答案選 the least。

questions.stem: What is it in the reading?
questions.passage:
When it arrives,
Everyone cheers.
The shirts and shorts dance
On the clothesline,
The trees and flowers wave
Like they're saying hi,
And the clouds are so excited
To have a running race.

When it comes,
Dad's face falls.
His coat and jeans jump
Off the clothesline
Into the pond.
The tree leaves hop off
And have a party on his new car.

When it gets here,
A lot of fun things I notice:
The pond's face,
Sad and old with lines;
Mrs. Smith's bread,
Delicious and freshly baked.
But what smell is it?
Oh, no, it's from Mr. Brown's feet!

(leaf (leaves) 葉子)
questions.explain: 衣服在曬衣繩上飄動、雲朵在賽跑、樹葉飛起來、還把味道吹過來，這些都是「風」造成的畫面，所以答案選 The wind。

(leaf (leaves) 葉子)
questions.explain: Dad's face falls（爸爸的臉垮下來）表示他不開心，加上他的外套牛仔褲被吹進池塘、樹葉掉在新車上，都讓爸爸不高興，所以答案選 He is not happy。

questions.stem: What can we learn about Buffy's friends?

options.A: Matt has experience of yard sales.
options.B: Jamie had a successful yard sale last week.
options.C: Albert thinks weather is important for yard sales.
options.D: Debby thinks things are cheaper online than at yard sales.

questions.passage:
Buffy: Hey, guys! I have a lot of things I don't need, so I'm thinking about having a yard sale. What do you think?

Debby: Having a yard sale is not easy. You have to collect things, put prices on them, put them out on tables and then wait for people to come. And usually you have to sell things at VERY low prices. For all that time and work, you make very little money. Why not just sell your things online for higher prices?

Jamie: Debby's right. But I think it'd be even better to just give your things to people who need them. I just sent out a lot of things last week.

Albert: Hey, I love yard sales! It's a great chance to make big money if you pick the right day. In my experience, Saturday is the best.

Matt: I agree with Albert. You can make money and meet interesting people. I made some good friends at my last yard sale.

（原題為對話框圖示，發話者姓名依對話內容與官方答案還原標註）
questions.explain: Matt 說他上次辦跳蚤市場的時候交到了一些好朋友，可以知道他有辦過跳蚤市場的經驗，所以答案選 Matt has experience of yard sales。

questions.stem: What do Buffy's friends think about having a yard sale?

options.A: Albert and Matt think it's a nice way to make friends.
options.B: Debby and Albert think it's difficult to make money from it.
options.C: Matt agrees with Jamie that it's a good chance to help people.
options.D: Jamie agrees with Debby that it's a lot of trouble to prepare for it.

questions.passage:

questions.explain: Jamie 說「Debby's right」（黛比說的對），表示他同意黛比說的「辦跳蚤市場很花時間、很麻煩」，所以答案選 Jamie agrees with Debby that it's a lot of trouble to prepare for it。

questions.stem: From the ad, which is true about Sunny Sun pictures?

options.A: The first day to use Sunny Sun pictures is Feb. 4.
options.B: The last day to collect Sunny Sun pictures is Feb. 10.
options.C: You cannot use Sunny Sun pictures to get free desserts.
options.D: You cannot get Sunny Sun pictures when you buy books.

questions.passage:
Here is an ad for Sunny Market.
From Jan. 4 to Feb. 4, for each $50 you spend (on anything EXCEPT books and CDs) at Sunny Market, you'll get a Sunny Sun picture. (2 pictures for every $100, 3 pictures for every $150, …)
With Sunny Sun pictures, you can get one of the prizes below for free or at low prices!!
Prizes: Sunny Cup, Chocolate, Fruitcake, Ice cream
★ Collect 12 pictures, and you can get a Sunny Cup for free!
★ Collect 10 pictures, and you can get any of the desserts on the right for free!
★ Collect 6 pictures, and you can buy a Sunny Cup for $200, or any of the desserts on the right for $100!
★ From Jan. 4 to Feb. 10, you can use Sunny Sun pictures to get Sunny Cups or desserts.
questions.explain: 廣告寫說買東西可以拿貼紙，但「書和 CD 除外」，所以買書是拿不到貼紙的，答案選 You cannot get Sunny Sun pictures when you buy books。

questions.stem: Sammy has 7 Sunny Sun pictures. She wants to get a Sunny Cup. How can she get one?

options.A: Use 6 Sunny Sun pictures and pay $100.
options.B: Use 6 Sunny Sun pictures and pay $200.
options.C: Spend $150 to get 3 more Sunny Sun pictures.
options.D: Spend $200 to get 4 more Sunny Sun pictures.

questions.passage:

questions.stem: What does them mean in the reading?
questions.passage:
Now I'm going to show you how to work with chocolate. I'll do it in a “bain-marie,” or, well, some people call it “water bath.” I myself love the name “bain-marie.” To make a “bain-marie,” you need two pots, one bigger than the other.
First, break the chocolate into small pieces and put them in the smaller pot. Usually when making chocolate desserts, you'll need to mix chocolate with butter and sugar. So if you do, put them in the pot too.
Now, half fill the bigger pot with warm water. Put the smaller pot over the bigger one, and start to slowly mix the chocolate, the butter, and the sugar together. Keep the water under 50ºC, or the chocolate may lose its shine. And make sure that no water goes in the smaller pot, or the chocolate will become hard and cannot be used.
Keep mixing for about five to ten minutes, and the job is done. Easy, right?

(mix 混合)
questions.explain: 前一句才剛說做巧克力甜點通常要把「奶油和糖」加進去一起混合，put them in the pot too 裡的 them 指的就是這句話裡的奶油和糖，所以答案選 Butter and sugar。

questions.stem: From the reading, which is true about working with chocolate?

options.A: It is better to use a “bain-marie” than a “water bath.”
options.B: The water in the pot should not be warmer than 50ºC.
options.C: It is better to finish mixing it in less than five minutes.
options.D: We should not mix butter and sugar together at the same time.

questions.passage:
Now I'm going to show you how to work with chocolate. I'll do it in a “bain-marie,” or, well, some people call it “water bath.” I myself love the name “bain-marie.” To make a “bain-marie,” you need two pots, one bigger than the other.
First, break the chocolate into small pieces and put them in the smaller pot. Usually when making chocolate desserts, you'll need to mix chocolate with butter and sugar. So if you do, put them in the pot too.
Now, half fill the bigger pot with warm water. Put the smaller pot over the bigger one, and start to slowly mix the chocolate, the butter, and the sugar together. Keep the water under 50ºC, or the chocolate may lose its shine. And make sure that no water goes in the smaller pot, or the chocolate will become hard and cannot be used.
Keep mixing for about five to ten minutes, and the job is done. Easy, right?

(mix 混合)
questions.explain: 文章寫「Keep the water under 50ºC」，意思是水溫不能超過攝氏 50 度，不然巧克力會失去光澤，所以答案選 The water in the pot should not be warmer than 50ºC。

questions.stem: What does the news story say about the Yangs' food truck?

options.A: What people love about their food.
options.B: What made them start their business.
options.C: How they made delicious fried chicken.
options.D: How they fixed their business problems.

questions.passage:
Taiwanese Bian-dang in Manhattan, New York City
5/30/2014    by Eva Schmidt

During lunch time, on 53rd Street, you'll see a long line of people in front of a Taiwanese food truck for their “bian-dang,” a Taiwanese word for “lunch box.” On the menu you can find fried chicken with rice, pork dumplings, tea eggs, and other popular Taiwanese dishes.
The owners of the food truck are Thomas and Diana Yang, two Taiwanese-Americans. This brother-sister team started their business in 2009. Back then, there were a few Taiwanese restaurants in the city and several thousand food trucks, but the Yangs were the first to sell Taiwanese food on a food truck.
At first, business was slack. People thought they were just another Chinese food truck, and the food truck's name, “Cravings,” often made people think of desserts, not lunch. Few people would stop by and try their food. “I couldn't even give away free food,” said Thomas. To catch people's eye, they painted their truck blue, and drew Taiwan's national flower, the plum blossom, on it. They also changed the food truck's name to “bian-dang.” Soon people started to notice them, and business finally got better. Now on a good day, they can sell about 150 bian-dangs in about two hours.
So what's next for Thomas and Diana? Well, they hope one day there will be a block with Taiwanese food in the city, just like there is a Chinatown, a Korea Town, and a Little Italy. And their bian-dang food truck might just help to make that happen.

(national 國家的)
questions.explain: 這篇報導主要在講餐車生意一開始很冷清、常被誤會，後來老闆怎麼把餐車漆成藍色、改名字，終於把問題解決，重點是「他們怎麼解決生意上的困難」，所以答案選 How they fixed their business problems。

questions.stem: What does it mean when business is slack?
questions.passage:
Taiwanese Bian-dang in Manhattan, New York City
5/30/2014    by Eva Schmidt

During lunch time, on 53rd Street, you'll see a long line of people in front of a Taiwanese food truck for their “bian-dang,” a Taiwanese word for “lunch box.” On the menu you can find fried chicken with rice, pork dumplings, tea eggs, and other popular Taiwanese dishes.
The owners of the food truck are Thomas and Diana Yang, two Taiwanese-Americans. This brother-sister team started their business in 2009. Back then, there were a few Taiwanese restaurants in the city and several thousand food trucks, but the Yangs were the first to sell Taiwanese food on a food truck.
At first, business was slack. People thought they were just another Chinese food truck, and the food truck's name, “Cravings,” often made people think of desserts, not lunch. Few people would stop by and try their food. “I couldn't even give away free food,” said Thomas. To catch people's eye, they painted their truck blue, and drew Taiwan's national flower, the plum blossom, on it. They also changed the food truck's name to “bian-dang.” Soon people started to notice them, and business finally got better. Now on a good day, they can sell about 150 bian-dangs in about two hours.
So what's next for Thomas and Diana? Well, they hope one day there will be a block with Taiwanese food in the city, just like there is a Chinatown, a Korea Town, and a Little Italy. And their bian-dang food truck might just help to make that happen.

(national 國家的)
questions.explain: 後面說幾乎沒人願意停下來，連免費的食物都送不出去，可以猜出 slack 是指生意「不好、冷清」的意思，所以答案選 It is bad。

questions.stem: What does that mean in the news story?

options.C: A city block that sells Taiwanese food.
options.D: A business that is popular across the country.

questions.passage:
Taiwanese Bian-dang in Manhattan, New York City
5/30/2014    by Eva Schmidt

During lunch time, on 53rd Street, you'll see a long line of people in front of a Taiwanese food truck for their “bian-dang,” a Taiwanese word for “lunch box.” On the menu you can find fried chicken with rice, pork dumplings, tea eggs, and other popular Taiwanese dishes.
The owners of the food truck are Thomas and Diana Yang, two Taiwanese-Americans. This brother-sister team started their business in 2009. Back then, there were a few Taiwanese restaurants in the city and several thousand food trucks, but the Yangs were the first to sell Taiwanese food on a food truck.
At first, business was slack. People thought they were just another Chinese food truck, and the food truck's name, “Cravings,” often made people think of desserts, not lunch. Few people would stop by and try their food. “I couldn't even give away free food,” said Thomas. To catch people's eye, they painted their truck blue, and drew Taiwan's national flower, the plum blossom, on it. They also changed the food truck's name to “bian-dang.” Soon people started to notice them, and business finally got better. Now on a good day, they can sell about 150 bian-dangs in about two hours.
So what's next for Thomas and Diana? Well, they hope one day there will be a block with Taiwanese food in the city, just like there is a Chinatown, a Korea Town, and a Little Italy. And their bian-dang food truck might just help to make that happen.

(national 國家的)
questions.explain: make that happen（讓這件事成真）裡的 that，指的是前一句提到希望城市裡出現一個「賣台灣食物的街區」，就像中國城一樣，所以答案選 A city block that sells Taiwanese food。

questions.stem: What problem is Selena Bieber trying to fix?

options.A: High school lessons are too difficult.
options.B: Classes start too early in the morning.
options.C: Too many students are late for school.
options.D: Lunch break is too short for taking a rest.

questions.passage:
This is a letter by Selena Bieber to the students in her school.
http://www.nvhsstudsup.org/openletter

Later Is Better!
Selena Bieber
Northville High School

Do you feel it's hard to get up early for school and even harder to listen well in the first class at 8 a.m.? Well, this happens to most of us and is not helpful for our learning!

Some studies show that teenagers' brains make melatonin, the hormone that helps sleep, between 11 p.m. and 8 a.m. So it's better for us to sleep during these nine hours. But here comes the problem. We're asked to get to school by 7:30 a.m. That means we have to get up before 7, when our brains should still be sleeping. That's why we're always so tired and can't think clearly in early morning classes. No clear head, no good learning, right?

If we can't change how our body works, why can't school start later? In this way, we can not only sleep more but also learn better!

Want to help us make it happen?
Visit www.nvhsstudsup.org/later-better to learn more.

(brain 大腦　hormone 荷爾蒙)
questions.explain: 整封信都在說上課時間太早，害學生睡不夠、早上第一節課沒辦法專心，希望學校可以晚一點上課，所以答案選 Classes start too early in the morning。

questions.stem: What can we learn from the letter?

options.A: Too much melatonin can hurt teenagers' brain.
options.B: The brain stops making melatonin after 11 p.m.
options.C: Sleeping longer helps the body make more melatonin.
options.D: It is easier to fall asleep when the brain is making melatonin.

questions.explain: 文章說 melatonin 是「幫助睡眠」的荷爾蒙，而且是在晚上 11 點到早上 8 點之間分泌，所以身體在分泌這個荷爾蒙的時候比較容易睡著，所以答案選 It is easier to fall asleep when the brain is making melatonin。

questions.stem:
Below is some information from another study.
“We studied 48 high school students and found that the students who went to bed earlier did better on their school tests. This is not only true for those who slept nine hours, but also true for those who slept less. And some of them agreed that they learned better in early morning classes.”

Do the ideas in Selena Bieber's letter agree with the information?

options.A: Yes, because her letter says it is better to sleep from 11 p.m. to 8 a.m.
options.B: Yes, because her letter says getting up early is not helpful for learning.
options.C: No, because her letter says students are asked to get to school by 7:30 a.m.
options.D: No, because her letter says teenagers' brains do not work well in early morning classes.

questions.stem: What can we learn about Kivalina?

options.C: It is fighting for one last hope.
options.D: It has given up its chance to rise.

questions.passage:
Kivalina, an island village in Alaska, USA, is known by only a few people. It cannot be found on most maps of Alaska because it is only 10 km2. This small Arctic village is home to 400 Inuit people*. However, their home will become uninhabitable because the island could be covered by the Chukchi Sea by 2025. These Inuit people will have to leave their home.
But life now is already difficult. Over the past twenty years, winters have become warmer, the Arctic ice has kept melting, and the sea has been rising. These changes have made it harder to live by hunting and fishing than before. What's worse, there is no Arctic ice thick enough to keep them safe from terrible wind and rain.
These Inuit people think it is the oil and power companies in Alaska that have brought all these troubles, but they are the ones who are paying the price. It would cost hundreds of millions to move their village, and they have no idea where to get the money. Groups like ReLocate are working together with the Inuit people to save Kivalina, but nobody is sure if their hard work will come to anything. All the Inuit people can do now is to pray the rising sea will not cover their home too soon.

* The Inuit people are a group of people who live in the Arctic.

(melt 融化　hunt 打獵　company 公司)
questions.explain: 村子快要被海水淹沒，但族人和一些團體還在努力搶救、祈禱海水不要那麼快淹過來，這是在「為最後一絲希望努力」，所以答案選 It is fighting for one last hope。

questions.stem: What does the writer think about the Inuit people in Kivalina?

options.A: Their way of living has been hurting the earth.
options.B: They might not be able to get enough money to move their village.
options.C: They should make the oil and power companies fix their problems.
options.D: There are better ways than to move their village to somewhere else.

questions.stem: What does the reading say about the Arctic ice?

options.A: The Arctic ice covered 10 km2 of Kivalina.
options.B: The melting Arctic ice kills about 400 Inuit people each year.
options.C: The Arctic ice helped the Inuit people in Kivalina during bad weather.
options.D: The melting Arctic ice has given the Inuit people enough water to use.

questions.stem: But before you decide whether it's OK to have such a long commute, it's best to know ______ .

options.B: how you can enjoy it more
options.C: how it will change your life
options.D: what is the best way of commute for you

questions.passage:
After a lot of tests and interviews, you finally got into a good school or got a good job. But there's a problem: it's a long way from home. “Well, I can take the bus or drive,” you might think. But before you decide whether it's OK to have such a long commute, it's best to know 35 .
Having a long commute means you'll get fewer hours of sleep, and that'll make you feel tired easily. You'll also have less time for friends and family, for exercise, or for a nice meal.
A long commute not only eats up your time but also 36 . Studies show that people who have a long commute get fat easily and often have neck or back problems. It's easier for them to have heart problems too. 37 . Studies find that these people get angry more often, feel less happy with their lives, and do less well at work.
Now, does that good school or that good job still sound good to you?

(interview 面試　commute 通勤)
questions.explain: 後面的段落分別講長途通勤會怎麼影響睡眠、健康、心情，這些都是在說「它會怎麼改變你的生活」，所以答案選 how it will change your life。

questions.stem: To the cities that fight for this chance, ______ . The Olympics usually do a lot of good to the host city.

options.B: this sounds like a good idea
options.C: this is perhaps a smart answer
options.D: this is old news they have heard

questions.passage:
NOlympics in Munich
11/11/2013

People in the city of Munich have spoken their mind: they do not want to host the Winter Olympics in 2022. To the cities that fight for this chance, 38 . The Olympics usually do a lot of good to the host city. The Games bring in jobs, visitors, and, most important of all, money. So why did people in Munich say no?
The Olympics 39 for people in Munich. For them, the world's biggest sports festival could invite serious trouble. There would be a lot of building work before the Games, and during the Games, too much traffic and too many visitors. Life would become terrible and nature would be hurt. And 40 : Munich hosted the 1972 Summer Olympics, and its neighbor, the mountain town Garmisch-Partenkirchen, hosted the 1936 Winter Olympics.
Still, the heavy price Munich paid does not worry the cities that are in the race to get the 2022 Winter Olympics. 41 , they are happy that Munich said no to the Olympics—their chances of getting the Games have become higher.

(host 舉辦　Olympics 奧運　visitor 遊客)
questions.explain: 奧運通常會替主辦城市帶來工作機會、遊客和很多錢，好處這麼多，所以對那些正在爭取主辦權的城市來說，慕尼黑拒絕主辦「一定會讓他們很意外」，所以答案選 this is surely a surprise。
