questions.stem: Look at the picture. The mother duck is ______ the baby ducks across the street.
questions.explain: 鴨媽媽帶著小鴨過馬路。lead 是「帶領」的意思，像媽媽牽著小孩過馬路一樣，所以答案選 leading。carrying 是「抱著」、pushing 是「推」、watching 是「看著」，跟「帶著走」不一樣，所以不選。

questions.stem: Ms. Liu usually gives us a lot of homework, ______ she didn’t give us any today.
questions.explain: 前面說劉老師平常出很多功課，後面卻說今天沒出，前後意思相反，要用「可是」這個轉折的字，英文是 but，所以答案選 but。if 是「如果」、or 是「或者」、so 是「所以」，語氣都對不上。

questions.stem: Playing games on the cellphone ______ popular with high school students.
questions.explain: Playing games on the cellphone（玩手機遊戲）是一整件事情，當作一個東西來看，所以要用單數的 is，就像 Swimming is fun（游泳很好玩）一樣。are 是給很多東西用的，所以不對。

questions.stem: There ______ more than twenty clubs in our school. Which one would you like to join?
questions.explain: There are 用來說「有…」，後面接 more than twenty clubs（超過二十個社團），社團是很多個，所以用 are。has、have、is 都跟「很多個」對不起來。

questions.stem: Diane had a bad cold last week. She is still too ______ to go mountain climbing with you.
questions.explain: Diane 重感冒剛好還沒完全恢復，身體還很虛弱，沒辦法跟你去爬山，weak 就是「虛弱、沒力氣」的意思，所以答案選 weak。busy 是「忙」、short 是「矮」、young 是「年輕」，都跟生病沒力氣沒關係。

questions.stem: The book under Mary’s desk is ______ , but I don’t know why it is there.
questions.explain: 這句話是說「瑪莉桌下的書是我的」。用 be 動詞（is）後面要用「我的東西」這種說法，英文是 mine，例如 It is mine（那是我的）。my 後面要接名詞，像 my book，這裡沒有名詞跟著，所以不能用 my。

questions.stem: Last night a garbage truck hit a tree and turned over. Now the street is ______ with lots of garbage.
questions.explain: 垃圾車翻車，垃圾灑滿街道，街道變得很髒，dirty 就是「髒」的意思，所以答案選 dirty。dark 是「暗」、heavy 是「重」、poor 是「窮」，都不合適。

questions.stem: It almost killed Kevin to take care of his baby sister for one afternoon. He wondered how his mom could do ______ every day.
questions.explain: Kevin 照顧妹妹一個下午就快累死了，他很好奇媽媽怎麼能「每天做這件事」。it 可以用來代替前面講的「照顧妹妹」這整件事，所以答案選 it。her、one、them 都不能拿來代替一整件事情。

questions.stem: Joyce tells her brother everything. She never ______ anything from him.
questions.explain: Joyce 什麼事都告訴哥哥，從來不對他「隱瞞」什麼，hide 就是「藏起來、隱瞞」的意思，hide something from someone（對某人隱瞞某事）是固定的說法，所以答案選 hides。

questions.stem: Ben always uses his birthday as a(n) ______ to ask Dad to buy him something expensive.
questions.explain: Ben 每次生日都拿來當「藉口」，要爸爸買貴的東西給他，excuse 就是「藉口」的意思，所以答案選 excuse。action 是「動作」、prize 是「獎品」、rule 是「規則」，都不是藉口的意思。

questions.stem: ______ that last piece of pie? If not, can I have it? I didn’t eat much this morning.
questions.explain: 這句話在問對方「你打算要吃那最後一塊派嗎？」用 be going to 表示「打算要做的事」，像 Are you going to eat...?（你打算要吃…嗎？），所以答案選 Are you going to eat。

questions.stem: Everyone in the class enjoys playing chess ______ Bill; he thinks the game is boring.
questions.explain: 全班同學都喜歡下棋，「除了」Bill 之外，因為他覺得下棋很無聊。except 就是「除了…之外」的意思，所以答案選 except。

questions.stem: In my school days, I ______ to English radio programs every day. That was how I learned English at that time.
questions.explain: 這句話在說「以前上學的時候，我每天都聽英語廣播」，這是在講「以前常常做、現在已經不做了」的習慣，英文用 used to，例如 I used to listen（我以前常常聽），所以答案選 used to listen。

questions.stem: Tom’s voice ______ when he talks about his neighbors. He speaks faster, his face becomes redder and you can almost see fire in his eyes.
questions.explain: Tom 講到鄰居就愈說愈激動，講話速度變快、臉變紅，聲音也跟著「提高」，rise 就是「升高」的意思，所以答案選 rises。

questions.stem: ______ other waiters in the restaurant have worked here longer than Clark; only Lois and Lana started working here before him.
questions.explain: 這句話說只有 Lois 和 Lana 兩個人比 Clark 早來上班，代表比 Clark 資深的服務生「很少」，few 就是「很少」的意思，所以答案選 Few。all 是「全部」、most 是「大部分」、some 是「一些」，數量都太多了。

questions.stem: What are “Daddy and me” doing in the poem?
questions.explain: 這首詩在講爸爸和「我」一起煮 goulash（燉牛肉湯），詩裡寫到用刀切牛肉、馬鈴薯在水裡轉來轉去、湯在鍋子裡冒泡，這些都是在「做菜、煮東西」的畫面，所以答案選 Cooking。詩裡說馬鈴薯在「跳舞」、湯在「唱歌」只是用來形容做菜的樣子，不是真的在跳舞唱歌，所以不選 Singing 或 Dancing。
questions.passage:
Making goulash
we don’t talk much,
Daddy and me.

“The talking’s in the making, son,”
he smiles.
I see what he means.

It’s in the knife
meeting the beef,
in the dancing of potatoes
as they turn in the water,
in the singing
of boiling soup in the pot.

There are enough words
in the way
Daddy teaches me his famous dish
and the way he hugs me
when we finish.

Making goulash
we don’t talk much,
Daddy and me,

but everything is said.

(Ideas from Candace Pearson’s poem)

questions.stem: What can we most likely know about the speaker?

options.B: He enjoys his time with his father.
options.C: He often makes goulash for his father.
options.D: He wants to be as famous as his father.

questions.explain: 爸爸和說話的孩子一起做菜，雖然話不多，但最後會互相擁抱，詩的最後說 everything is said（什麼都不用說也懂了），代表他很享受跟爸爸在一起的這段時光，所以答案選 He enjoys his time with his father。

in the singing

questions.stem: Which is true about City Sight Bus tickets?

options.A: It costs less to buy tickets on the Internet.
options.B: People must pay the full ticket price for a pet.
options.C: Tickets are more expensive in the high season.
options.D: People save 10% if they buy two tickets for the same line.

questions.explain: 海報上寫「Save 10% if you buy tickets on the Internet.」（網路買票可以省 10%），代表網路買票比較便宜，所以答案選 It costs less to buy tickets on the Internet。寵物是付「半價」不是全票、同條路線買兩張沒有折扣、票價也沒有因為旺季變貴，這些都不對。
questions.passage:
City Sight Bus (www.citysightbus.org)
[路線圖：停靠站含 Central Station、Farmers’ Bridge、Queen Marguerite’s Park、East End Temple、Westside Church、Starlight Garden、Great Age Theater、Grand Castle of King Charles、King Henry’s Castle、King Henry’s Garden、Lovers’ Bridge、City Square、King James’ Castle 等]

OPENING HOURS
High Season (April to September): 9:00 – 18:00 every day
Low Season (October to March): 9:00 – 17:00 every day

TICKET PRICES
Line A: $20 each person
Line B: $18 each person

THINGS YOU SHOULD KNOW
Buses for both lines leave every hour from Central Station.
Line A takes one hour; Line B takes 50 minutes.
If you are taking your pet with you, please buy a seat for it at half price.
Save 10% if you buy tickets on the Internet.
Save 10% if you buy tickets for both lines.
Go to www.citysightbus.org for more information.

questions.stem: Which is true about the bus lines?

options.C: Both lines leave from Central Station.
options.D: There are more gardens to see on Line A.

questions.explain: 海報上寫「兩條路線的公車都從 Central Station（中央車站）出發」，所以答案選 Both lines leave from Central Station。Line B 只要 50 分鐘，比 Line A（60 分鐘）快，所以「B 比較久」是錯的。

questions.stem: Why were there seventeen calls asking for Mike?

options.A: His team was angry at him.
options.B: He is a popular soccer player.
options.C: He did not show up for the game on Friday.
options.D: His name did not appear on the list of players.

questions.explain: Mike 忘記去店裡拿球隊的球衣，結果隊友（Johnny、Ricky 等）打了 17 通電話，說他「死定了」，可以知道是全隊的人都在生他的氣，所以答案選 His team was angry at him。
questions.passage:
Sarah: You’re VERY popular today. I’ve had seventeen calls asking for you.
Mike: Finally! After all these years of kicking and running, I’ve got my own fans! So what did they want? My photos? Do I need to sign my name? Are they starting a fan club for me?
Sarah: Well, let me ask you this: What day was yesterday?
Mike: Friday… Why?
Sarah: Did you forget something?
Mike: Did I forget… OH, NO! I didn’t pick up my soccer team’s sport shirts from the shop! And we’re having this big game tomorrow!
Sarah: Yeah. And from what I’ve heard, it’s your team’s most important game this season.
Mike: OK. I have to go fix this now.
Sarah: Too late. They’ve tried. It’s not open on the weekend. And if you want to know, they are Johnny, Ricky, Archie, Freddie…
Mike: I know, I know, everyone on my team. Oh, I’m a dead man now.
Sarah: Yeah, that was what they said on the phone, seventeen times.

questions.stem: What does It mean in the dialogue?
questions.explain: 「It's not open on the weekend.」（它週末不營業）指的是前面說的那家店，Mike 要去拿球衣的那家店，所以 It 指的是 The shop。

questions.stem: What can we learn about Mike?

options.A: He is nice to people from his fan club.
options.B: He plays soccer with Johnny and Ricky.
options.C: He found a way to fix the trouble he made.
options.D: He decided not to go to the game on Sunday.

questions.explain: Sarah 說打電話來的人是 Johnny、Ricky 這些人，Mike 說「我知道，都是我隊上的人」，可以知道 Mike 和他們一起踢足球，所以答案選 He plays soccer with Johnny and Ricky。

questions.stem: Which is the best title for this reading?

options.A: Australia: The Best Place for Birds.
options.C: Larger Mother Birds Have Larger Eggs.
options.D: Family Love: Brothers & Sisters Work Together.

questions.explain: 這篇文章主要在講，媽媽鳥會根據天氣的好壞，下大小不同的蛋，來幫助小鳥對抗惡劣的天氣，所以最適合的標題是「母鳥對抗天氣變化」，答案選 Mother Bird Fights Weather Changes。
questions.passage:
Animals have their special ways to deal with hard times in nature. The superb fairy-wren, one kind of bright-blue bird in Australia, is one example. This year, a ten-year study showed that this small bird has eggs of different sizes in different kinds of weather. When the weather is hot and dry, there is less food for young superb fairy-wrens, and they die easily. So the mother bird will make larger eggs to help her babies grow stronger inside before they break out of the eggs into the “hungry” world.

However, not all mother birds are able to do this trick. Only those with at least one male child can. When the weather is “good,” the mother will make smaller eggs, and her sons will bring food back for their baby brothers and sisters from the eggs. With her sons’ help, the mother can save more energy to make larger eggs when the weather is bad. Isn’t this amazing?

questions.stem: What does this trick mean in the reading?

options.C: Saving food for weaker baby birds.
options.D: Helping baby birds break out of the eggs.

questions.explain: this trick（這個絕招）指的就是前面提到的，媽媽鳥會「依照天氣下不同大小的蛋」這件事，所以答案選 Having eggs of different sizes。

questions.stem: What can we learn about superb fairy-wrens from the reading?

options.A: Male birds can usually live for ten years.
options.B: Baby birds grow well in hot, dry weather.
options.C: Young male birds share the job of feeding the baby birds.
options.D: Mother birds make smaller eggs when there is less food outside.

questions.explain: 文章說天氣好的時候，媽媽鳥下比較小的蛋，兒子們會幫忙把食物帶回來餵弟弟妹妹，所以答案選 Young male birds share the job of feeding the baby birds。

questions.stem: What idea is talked about in both pieces of news?

options.A: More young people need to join in making art.
options.B: People’s ways of enjoying art change with time.
options.C: Bringing important art works to Taiwan from abroad.
options.D: Giving new life to old arts that were almost forgotten.

questions.explain: 紙紮（快要消失的傳統技藝）跟相聲（曾經式微）這兩則新聞，都是在講「幫快被遺忘的老藝術重新注入活力」，所以答案選 Giving new life to old arts that were almost forgotten。
questions.passage:
Here are two pieces of news that Annie collected for her report.

Papier-mâché is works of art that are made of paper. In Taiwan, it is known as zhizha and is usually burned as a gift for dead people. Taiwan’s papier-mâché is dying because it is not much needed now. However, this dying art won a Frenchman’s heart when he visited the island. He then invited one of the papier-mâché shops, Hsin Hsin Paper Offering Store, to show their beautiful works in France. The shop owner’s son also used papier-mâché in a way that no one ever thought of—making a movie with it.

Stan Lai is very important in Taiwan’s theater. He brought changes to Chinese theater art of the old days to make it closer to our life experiences and easier to understand. One of his most famous works is That Evening, We Performed Crosstalk (1985). It was such a smash hit that people became interested again in xiangsheng, a show of funny talk between two actors. This art used to be a hobby that only a few people shared, but Lai has opened a bigger market for it. Now it has fans across all ages.

questions.stem: What does it mean when something is a smash hit?
questions.explain: 後面說相聲因此「讓大家重新感興趣、市場變大」，可以猜出 a smash hit 是「非常成功、大受歡迎」的意思，所以答案選 It is successful。

questions.stem: What do we know from the news?

options.A: Lai has taught xiangsheng in many schools.
options.B: Lai often puts his own life stories into his art works.
options.C: The owner of Hsin Hsin Paper Offering Store is a Frenchman.
options.D: People do not burn papier-mâché for dead people as often as before.

questions.explain: 文章說紙紮現在「因為需求不多而漸漸沒落」，可以知道現在燒紙紮給往生者的人比以前少了，所以答案選 People do not burn papier-mâché for dead people as often as before。

questions.stem: Which idea is talked about in the news?

options.A: How often we should drink coffee.
options.B: What happens when we have too much coffee.
options.C: How to help the body make the cortisol we need.
options.D: What happens when we have coffee at the wrong time.

questions.explain: 這篇報導主要在講「起床後馬上喝咖啡其實是最糟的時機」，重點是講「喝錯時間會發生什麼事」，所以答案選 What happens when we have coffee at the wrong time。
questions.passage:
VOF News  16/01/2015

For many people, having a cup of coffee right out of bed is the best way to start a day. But a report from Asap Science says otherwise. It says the worst time for coffee is soon after we get up. When we wake up, our bodies start to make cortisol. Cortisol helps us to think clearly and be quick to understand and act. The cortisol levels rise after we wake up and climb to the highest in about an hour. So there are two problems when we drink coffee during this time: (1) our bodies will make less cortisol, and (2) the cortisol will make coffee less useful. And that’s why some of us drink more and more coffee.

Then, when’s the best time to have coffee? When the cortisol levels are going down, the report says. Cortisol levels are usually highest between 8 a.m. and 9 a.m., between noon and 1 p.m., and between 5:30 p.m. and 6:30 p.m. So if you want to have coffee, enjoy it after these times, and this popular drink will help you the most.

questions.stem: What does it mean when someone thinks otherwise about something?

options.B: They have no idea about it.
options.D: They do not think it is a problem.

questions.explain: think otherwise 是說這份報告跟一般人「起床先喝咖啡最好」的想法「不一樣」，otherwise 在這裡是「有不同的看法」的意思，所以答案選 They think differently about it。

questions.stem:
Four people read the news and wrote what they thought about it.
Mike (Jan 16 8:26 pm): The best time to drink coffee is when you want to drink coffee.
Sarah (Jan 16 8:43 pm): I usually wake up AFTER I drink coffee!
Charlie (Jan 16 8:59 pm): The worst time to drink coffee is when you're DEAD.
Lisa (Jan 16 9:18 pm): If you need someone to tell you when to drink coffee, well, that’s just sad.
What did they think about the news?

options.A: They wanted to try what it said.
options.B: They were sad about what it said.
options.C: They did not agree with what it said.
options.D: They were not surprised at what it said.

questions.explain: 四個人的留言都是用開玩笑的方式在反駁這篇報導（想喝就喝、需要別人告訴你才可悲），可以看出他們都不同意報導講的內容，所以答案選 They did not agree with what it said。

questions.stem: What does the report NOT suggest?

options.A: Having more free buses for shoppers.
options.B: Changing the opening hours on weekdays.
options.C: Making plans to help weekday morning business.
options.D: Changing the time of the special sale on weekends.

questions.explain: 報告裡建議了增加免費接駁車、延長平日營業時間、把週末特賣改到下午，但完全沒提到要怎麼改善「平日早上」的業績，所以答案選 Making plans to help weekday morning business（沒有提到的就是這一項）。
questions.passage:
Below is part of a new shopping center’s market report.

Our busiest time is weekend afternoons, between 1:30 p.m. and 3:30 p.m. About half the business of the week is done then. So it would be better if we move Weekend’s Best Buy from Saturday evening to Saturday afternoon when there may be more shoppers during the sale. We should also think about having more free buses during the weekend, as 75% of the shoppers who were interviewed said two buses an hour are just not enough.

Weekend evenings are second busiest, though it is not as good as last season. An interesting fact is that business is better on weekday evenings, usually one hour before closing, than on weekend mornings. Most of the shoppers then are people who live near or working parents who do their last-minute shopping. They together make up almost 60% of the business on weekday evenings. It might bring in more business if we open half an hour longer during weekdays, until 10:30 p.m.

questions.stem: What does the report say about the shopping center?

options.A: Its business on weekdays is not as good as last season.
options.B: It has a special sale one hour before closing every day.
options.C: Most of the shoppers take the free buses to the shopping center.
options.D: A large part of its shoppers on weekday evenings are working parents.

questions.explain: 報告說平日晚上的顧客大多是住附近的人，或是急著採買晚餐的上班族爸媽，這兩種人加起來占了將近六成，所以答案選 A large part of its shoppers on weekday evenings are working parents。

questions.stem: It hurts me to think that you’re going to ______ .
questions.explain: 後面的信提到 Maggie 一直想變得跟大眼睛、細腿的女演員一樣，還提到 this change（這個改變），可以猜出空格是在講她想要「改變原本的自己」，所以答案選 change the way you are。
questions.passage:
Dear Maggie,

I know you won’t be happy but I still have to say this again. It hurts me to think that you’re going to ______(36). I know you’ve always wanted to be like those actresses with big eyes and slim legs, but in fact you are already beautiful. What you are born with makes you special because nobody looks the same as you.

It worries me more that ______(37). But sadly, there’s no quick fix to this, not even with the doctor’s help. If you don’t try to like yourself first, no one else will. People like you not because of your face, but because of your heart. You need to understand this first. Until you understand this, nothing is going to be different even if you have this change.

I truly hope you think this through again.

Love,
Linda

questions.stem: It worries me more that ______ .

options.A: you’re too shy to talk with people
options.B: you just don’t want to be like others
options.C: you don’t think you’re good enough
options.D: you don’t know what you’ve missed

questions.explain: 後面寫著，要 Maggie 先學會喜歡自己，別人喜歡她是因為她的心，不是外表，可以推出讓 Linda 更擔心的是 Maggie「覺得自己不夠好」，所以答案選 you don't think you're good enough。

questions.stem: No one thought one day she ______ the queen of the UK.
questions.explain: 這句話是說「以當時（過去）的角度來看，沒有人想到她以後會成為女王」，要講「過去對未來的預測」，英文要用 would become，所以答案選 would become。
questions.passage:
Elizabeth Alexandra Mary Windsor was born in London on April 21, 1926. No one thought one day she ______(38) the queen of the UK. After her grandfather, King George V, died in 1936, Elizabeth’s uncle, Prince Edward, became King Edward VIII. Edward ______(39) in love with a married American woman long before, and he decided to marry her after he became the king. However, Edward was asked to choose between the throne and his love. Finally, he decided to give up the throne.

______(40). After Edward VIII gave up the throne, Elizabeth’s father became King George VI. As the oldest child without any brothers, Elizabeth became next in line to the throne. She needed to say goodbye to her good old life and prepare to be a queen. After her father died in 1952, she became Queen Elizabeth II.

Ever since she became head of her country, Queen Elizabeth II ______(41): She has held meetings with important people in the UK, and has visited many foreign countries. In the few hours she keeps to herself, however, she reads and enjoys horseback riding.

questions.stem: Edward ______ in love with a married American woman long before, and he decided to marry her after he became the king.
questions.explain: Edward「愛上」這位美國女士，是發生在他當上國王「之前」的更早以前（long before），要表示「比過去更早發生的事」，英文用 had fallen，所以答案選 had fallen。
questions.passage:
Elizabeth Alexandra Mary Windsor was born in London on April 21, 1926. No one thought one day she ______(38) the queen of the UK. After her grandfather, King George V, died in 1936, Elizabeth’s uncle, Prince Edward, became King Edward VIII. Edward ______(39) in love with a married American woman long before, and he decided to marry her after he became the king. However, Edward was asked to choose between the throne and his love. Finally, he decided to give up the throne.

______(40). After Edward VIII gave up the throne, Elizabeth’s father became King George VI. As the oldest child without any brothers, Elizabeth became next in line to the throne. She needed to say goodbye to her good old life and prepare to be a queen. After her father died in 1952, she became Queen Elizabeth II.

Ever since she became head of her country, Queen Elizabeth II ______(41): She has held meetings with important people in the UK, and has visited many foreign countries. In the few hours she keeps to herself, however, she reads and enjoys horseback riding.

questions.stem: ______ . After Edward VIII gave up the throne, Elizabeth’s father became King George VI.

options.B: It was not easy to give up the throne
options.C: It was good news to Elizabeth and her family
options.D: This part of history made the UK a strong country

questions.explain: 後面說伊莉莎白因此變成王位的下一個繼承人，必須跟以前的生活說再見、準備當女王，可以知道這件事「改變了她的人生」，所以答案選 This changed Elizabeth's life。

After her grandfather, King George V, died in 1936, Elizabeth’s uncle, Prince Edward, became King Edward VIII. Edward ______(39) in love with a married American woman long before, and he decided to marry her after he became the king. However, Edward was asked to choose between the throne and his love. Finally, he decided to give up the throne.

She needed to say goodbye to her good old life and prepare to be a queen. After her father died in 1952, she became Queen Elizabeth II.

Ever since she became head of her country, Queen Elizabeth II ______(41): She has held meetings with important people in the UK, and has visited many foreign countries. In the few hours she keeps to herself, however, she reads and enjoys horseback riding.

questions.stem: Ever since she became head of her country, Queen Elizabeth II ______ : She has held meetings with important people in the UK, and has visited many foreign countries.

options.A: has worked hard for the UK
options.B: has been popular with the people of the UK
options.C: has been the queen of the UK for a long time
options.D: has brought the people of the UK much closer

questions.explain: 冒號後面舉的例子（開會、出訪國外）都是在說明女王怎麼「為英國努力工作」，所以答案選 has worked hard for the UK。

Finally, he decided to give up the throne.

She needed to say goodbye to her good old life and prepare to be a queen. After her father died in 1952, she became Queen Elizabeth II.

Ever since she became head of her country, Queen Elizabeth II ______(41): She has held meetings with important people in the UK, and has visited many foreign countries. In the few hours she keeps to herself, however, she reads and enjoys horseback riding.

questions.stem: The ______ is so strong today that my hair keeps blowing in the air.
questions.explain: 頭髮一直被吹起來，代表「風」很大，wind 是風的意思，所以答案選 wind。

questions.stem: When the baby cried, Mr. Wu ______ in the kitchen and did not hear it.
questions.explain: 寶寶哭的那個時候，吳先生正好在廚房煮飯，所以沒聽到。「當時正在做的事」要用「be 動詞（過去式）+ ing」，像 was cooking（當時正在煮），所以答案選 was cooking。

questions.stem: I don't have any money with me because I ______ my wallet this morning.
questions.explain: 「今天早上」是很明確的過去時間點，講一件已經做完、只發生一次的事，要用最普通的過去式，像 lost（弄丟了），所以答案選 lost。

questions.stem: You need not only good luck but also hard work to become a ______ baseball player.
questions.explain: 要成功當一個棒球選手，需要運氣，也需要努力，成功的英文是 successful，所以答案選 successful。

questions.stem: The woman who lives next door to Ken is a ______ to him. They have never met and he doesn't even know her name.
questions.explain: 這句話在說「住在 Ken 隔壁的女生對他來說是個陌生人」，因為他們從沒見過面，連對方的名字都不知道。stranger 就是陌生人的意思，所以答案選 stranger。

questions.stem: With the new bus line, it is much more ______ for Fanny to go to school. It saves her a lot of time now.
questions.explain: 有了新的公車路線，Fanny 上學省下很多時間，代表變得更「方便」了，convenient 是方便的意思，所以答案選 convenient。

questions.stem:
Dad: Have you brushed your teeth yet?
Ann: No, but I ______ before I go to bed.
questions.explain: 爸爸問有沒有刷牙，Ann 說還沒刷，但是「睡前會刷」，講未來會做的事情、表示意願，用 will，所以答案選 will。

questions.stem:
Alice: Do you know ______ Grandma is going to visit us?
Willy: It's next week. She told me on the phone. And we'll take her to the national park.
questions.explain: Willy 回答「下星期」，代表 Alice 問的是「什麼時候」奶奶要來，when 是「什麼時候」的意思，所以答案選 when。

questions.stem: It took the police lots of time ______ who entered Liu's house and killed her one year ago.
questions.explain: 這句話在說「警察花了很多時間才查出來是誰闖進劉家、殺了她」。It takes 時間 to V 是固定句型，意思是「花多少時間去做某件事」，所以答案選 to find out。

questions.stem: Many famous people visit this restaurant. Popular singers like A-mei and Jay Chou ______ here with their friends.
questions.explain: 這句話在說「像阿妹、周杰倫這樣有名的歌手，常常會被人看到跟朋友一起出現在這家餐廳」。因為歌手是「被看到」的那一方，所以要用「are + 過去分詞」這種說法，像 are often seen（常常被看到），所以答案選 are often seen。

questions.stem: The factory has been throwing trash into the river for years. Now ______ must be taken to stop this.
questions.explain: 工廠好幾年來一直把垃圾丟進河裡，現在該「採取行動」阻止這件事了。take action 是固定的說法，意思是「採取行動」，所以答案選 action。

questions.stem: The beach party tonight ______ the end of our summer vacation. Tomorrow we are all going back home and getting ready for school.
questions.explain: 今晚的海灘派對「代表著」暑假結束了，明天大家都要回家準備開學。mark 這裡的意思是「象徵、代表」，所以答案選 marks。

questions.stem: Anyone who reads Loren's business plan will quickly see some serious problems in it, or ______ feel something is not right.
questions.explain: 就算看不出 Loren 的商業計畫有嚴重的問題，「至少」也會覺得哪裡怪怪的。at least 就是「至少」的意思，所以答案選 at least。

questions.stem: I've wanted to read The Diary of a Young Girl for months, ______ today I finally borrowed the book from the library.
questions.explain: 這句話在說「我想讀《安妮日記》好幾個月了，今天終於從圖書館借到了」，前後兩件事是順著接下去的，所以用 and 連起來，所以答案選 and。

questions.stem: 選出第 16 格最適合的答案。
questions.passage:
Robert likes to take a walk in the park after work. One day when he was walking in the park, he heard a woman calling his name. He stopped to look around, but didn't see anything __(16)__. Shortly after Robert saw it, it was gone.
      Though Robert felt a little strange, he did not think about it too much and sat down for a rest on a bench in the park. Then he noticed the snake he had just seen __(17)__. Robert was too afraid to move away from the bench. Right at this moment, a woman behind him shouted, "Come here, Robert. You can't stay on the bench like that. __(18)__."
      In surprise, Robert turned around and said to the woman, "Excuse me, but that's not a very nice thing to say, and in fact some people say I'm handsome."
      "I'm not talking to you," said the woman. "I'm telling Robert, my pet snake, to get down from the bench, not you."
questions.explain: 下一句提到「他剛剛看到的那條蛇」，可以知道 Robert 看到的東西是一條小蛇，所以答案選 C（除了一條小蛇之外，沒看到別的）。

questions.stem: 選出第 17 格最適合的答案。
questions.passage:

Robert was too afraid to move away from the bench. Right at this moment, a woman behind him shouted, "Come here, Robert. You can't stay on the bench like that. __(18)__."
      In surprise, Robert turned around and said to the woman, "Excuse me, but that's not a very nice thing to say, and in fact some people say I'm handsome."
      "I'm not talking to you," said the woman. "I'm telling Robert, my pet snake, to get down from the bench, not you."
questions.explain: 後面說寵物蛇在長椅上、女生要牠下來，可以知道蛇正在「爬上長椅」，所以答案選 A。

questions.stem: 選出第 18 格最適合的答案。

options.C: People will be scared by you
options.D: The paint of the bench is still wet

questions.passage:
Robert likes to take a walk in the park after work. One day when he was walking in the park, he heard a woman calling his name. He stopped to look around, but didn't see anything __(16)__. Shortly after Robert saw it, it was gone.
      Though Robert felt a little strange, he did not think about it too much and sat down for a rest on a bench in the park. Then he noticed the snake he had just seen __(17)__. Robert was too afraid to move away from the bench. Right at this moment, a woman behind him shouted, "Come here, Robert. You can't stay on the bench like that. __(18)__."
      In surprise, Robert turned around and said to the woman, "Excuse me, but that's not a very nice thing to say, and in fact some people say I'm handsome."
      "I'm not talking to you," said the woman. "I'm telling Robert, my pet snake, to get down from the bench, not you."
questions.explain: 女生其實是在對她的寵物蛇說「你這樣待在長椅上，人家會被你嚇到」，結果 Robert 誤以為在說自己，所以答案選 C。

questions.stem: 選出第 19 格最適合的答案。

options.C: to make itself a famous city

questions.passage:
If you cannot live without your car, Zurich might be the last city you would like to visit. In Zurich, people are welcome, but cars are not! Over the past 20 years, this city has used smart ways __(19)__. One is to keep the same total number of parking spaces. For example, if 50 new parking spaces are built in one part of the city, then 50 old spaces in other parts are taken away for other uses. So the total number does not change. Some are unhappy that there are never enough spaces. That is just what the city has in mind: If people find parking more difficult, they will drive less.
      __(20)__, the total number of cars in the city is counted. Over 3,500 little computers are put under Zurich roads to check the number of cars that enter the city. If the number is higher than the city can deal with, the traffic lights on the roads that enter the city will be kept red. So drivers who are traveling into Zurich have to stop and wait until there are fewer cars in the city. Now, you may wonder __(21)__. The answer is simple: The city wants to make more space for its people.
questions.explain: 整篇文章都在說蘇黎世這座城市用很多聰明的方法，讓車子變少、交通變得更順暢，所以答案選 A（讓交通變得比較不塞）。

questions.stem: 選出第 21 格最適合的答案。

options.B: what all this has cost Zurich
options.C: if Zurich should try other ways
options.D: if Zurich can deal with angry drivers

questions.stem: What is the reading mostly about?

options.A: Where to find a nice holiday apartment.
options.B: How to choose a nice holiday apartment.
options.C: Why one should choose a holiday apartment.
options.D: How one can change their house into a holiday apartment.

questions.passage:
Edward's Travel Tips
         What kind of place do you usually choose to stay for the night during a trip? Expensive hotels that may make you feel like you don't get what you pay for? Or cheap hostels that put you and five other strangers in the same bedroom? If both choices sound terrible to you, here's something new: holiday apartments.
         In a holiday apartment, you have more space than what a hotel room can give you. Everything in the apartment is at your disposal. The living room, the kitchen, the study, and, of course, the bedrooms are all for your own use. Some apartments even have a lovely garden or a game room. You'll feel at home in the holiday apartment. The best thing is, a holiday apartment is not expensive. The price for a holiday apartment is for two people, and the apartment is often cheaper than a hotel room for two. You only need to pay a little more for a third or fourth person. If you travel with friends or your family, a holiday apartment will be your best choice!
(hostel：提供廉價食宿的旅舍)
questions.explain: 整篇文章列出度假公寓的各種好處，想說服讀者「為什麼」要選度假公寓，所以答案選 C。

questions.stem: From the reading, what can we learn about holiday apartments?

options.A: They are good for people who like to take one-day trips.
options.B: They are good for people who travel in a group of three or more.
options.C: They are good for people who want to make new friends when traveling.
options.D: They are good for people who like to spend their holidays in the country.

You only need to pay a little more for a third or fourth person. If you travel with friends or your family, a holiday apartment will be your best choice!
(hostel：提供廉價食宿的旅舍)
questions.explain: 文章說度假公寓是「兩人的價錢」，多一兩個人只要多付一點點錢，而且很適合跟朋友、家人一起去玩，所以最適合三人以上的團體，答案選 B。

questions.stem: What does it mean to say that something is at your disposal?

options.A: You are free to use it.
options.D: You can buy it at a lower price.

(hostel：提供廉價食宿的旅舍)
questions.explain: 後面說「客廳、廚房、書房，當然還有臥室，全部都給你自己用」，可以推出 at your disposal 的意思是「你可以自由使用」，所以答案選 A。

questions.stem: What is the quick lesson of SHARP WEAR about?

options.A: How to get to one of SHARP WEAR's stores.
options.B: How to send back a T-shirt to SHARP WEAR.
options.C: How to shop for a T-shirt from SHARP WEAR.
options.D: How to take care of a T-shirt from SHARP WEAR.

questions.passage:
SHARP WEAR: A T-shirt like no other!
               First time here? Don't worry! Here is our quick lesson for you!
1. What kind of T-shirt do you like? (4 kinds of T-shirts to choose from!)
2. What color for the T-shirt? (We have as many as 16 colors!)
3. Want a picture on your T-shirt? No, thank you. / Oh yes! (Now choose from one of our 72 pictures or send us your own!)
4. Pocket? No pocket? I don't need one. / Of course! What kind of pocket then? (We've got 4 shapes for you!)
5. How about a different color for the pocket? No, thank you. Same color as the T-shirt. / Why not? (Choose from the 16 colors AGAIN!)
6. Your size? (7 sizes for you!)
7. How many T-shirts do you want?
8. Finally, you can pay!
questions.explain: 整份說明書一步一步教你怎麼「客製、訂購」一件 T 恤，所以答案選 C。

questions.stem: What can we learn about SHARP WEAR?

options.A: You have to order at least four T-shirts each time.
options.B: You can choose from sixteen colors for both the T-shirt and the pocket.
options.C: Some of the T-shirts have pictures on both the front side and the back side.
options.D: T-shirts in the wrong sizes cannot be sent back if they have pictures or pockets on them.

questions.passage:
SHARP WEAR: A T-shirt like no other!
               First time here? Don't worry! Here is our quick lesson for you!
1. What kind of T-shirt do you like? (4 kinds of T-shirts to choose from!)
2. What color for the T-shirt? (We have as many as 16 colors!)
3. Want a picture on your T-shirt? No, thank you. / Oh yes! (Now choose from one of our 72 pictures or send us your own!)
4. Pocket? No pocket? I don't need one. / Of course! What kind of pocket then? (We've got 4 shapes for you!)
5. How about a different color for the pocket? No, thank you. Same color as the T-shirt. / Why not? (Choose from the 16 colors AGAIN!)
6. Your size? (7 sizes for you!)
7. How many T-shirts do you want?
8. Finally, you can pay!
questions.explain: 第 2 步說 T 恤有 16 種顏色可選，第 5 步說口袋也可以再從同樣的 16 種顏色裡挑一次，所以 T 恤跟口袋都可以從 16 種顏色裡選，答案選 B。

questions.stem: Which season is this poem about?
questions.passage:
The sky is turning bright and clear.
The earth is wearing red and green.
Winter is gone; spring has come.
The air smells fresh and sweet.

The summer heat is not yet here;
A little fox comes out to play.
A bird is busy in a tree
Waking a daisy from her sleep.

Little daisy, little daisy,
People call the flower the "day's eye."
When she opens her eye,
The springtime brings us cheer.
(poem：詩)
questions.explain: 詩裡直接寫「冬天走了，春天來了」，還說「夏天的熱度還沒到」，最後也用 springtime（春天）作結尾，所以這首詩講的是春天，答案選 A。

questions.stem: What is a daisy?
questions.passage:
The sky is turning bright and clear.
The earth is wearing red and green.
Winter is gone; spring has come.
The air smells fresh and sweet.

The summer heat is not yet here;
A little fox comes out to play.
A bird is busy in a tree
Waking a daisy from her sleep.

Little daisy, little daisy,
People call the flower the "day's eye."
When she opens her eye,
The springtime brings us cheer.
(poem：詩)
questions.explain: 詩裡寫「人們把這種花叫做 day's eye（白天的眼睛）」，直接說明 daisy 是一種花，所以答案選 C。

questions.stem: What does the reading say about shaking?

options.A: Different animals' shaking begins with different body parts.
options.B: Shaking is a way that animals use to make other animals scared.
options.C: Animals that are not kept as pets do the shaking better than those that are.
options.D: Shaking themselves dry may help animals run faster in dangerous moments.

questions.passage:
When we get wet, we need a towel to get ourselves dry. When a dog gets wet, all it needs is to shake its body. A study in 2010 showed that a wet dog can throw off half the water on its body by shaking for less than a second. In fact, this common act of dogs works better than a washing machine.
      The study found that animal shaking begins with the head and ends with the tail. During a shake, the animal's head, body and skin all move. Smaller animals must shake faster than bigger animals to get water off. For example, in one second, a rat can shake 18 times, a dog 6 times, and a bear 4 times. Bigger animals can get their bodies dry with fewer shakes.
      For animals, shaking is not just about getting themselves dry. It is also about saving their lives. Being wet makes animals heavier, and that makes it harder to run. In the animal world, how fast an animal can run often decides whether it will live or not. Maybe that's why the "wet-dog shake" has become a common habit of many animals.
(skin：皮膚)
questions.explain: 文章最後說「濕答答會讓動物變重，跑起來更難」，可以推知動物把身體甩乾，是為了在危急的時候能跑得快一點、保住性命，所以答案選 D。

questions.stem: What can we learn from the reading?

options.A: The animal's tail can help it run more quickly and easily.
options.B: Some animals shake more than they need to get water off.
options.C: The idea of washing machines came from the shaking of dogs.
options.D: Larger animals get themselves drier at each shake than smaller animals.

questions.passage:

      The study found that animal shaking begins with the head and ends with the tail. During a shake, the animal's head, body and skin all move. Smaller animals must shake faster than bigger animals to get water off. For example, in one second, a rat can shake 18 times, a dog 6 times, and a bear 4 times. Bigger animals can get their bodies dry with fewer shakes.
      For animals, shaking is not just about getting themselves dry. It is also about saving their lives. Being wet makes animals heavier, and that makes it harder to run. In the animal world, how fast an animal can run often decides whether it will live or not. Maybe that's why the "wet-dog shake" has become a common habit of many animals.
(skin：皮膚)
questions.explain: 文章說體型比較大的動物用比較少的次數就能把身體甩乾，代表牠們每甩一次，乾的效果比小動物好，所以答案選 D。

questions.stem: What does things like that mean?

options.A: Problems about the free coffee plan.
options.B: Trouble for people who go to Flora Café.
options.C: Ways to make the free coffee plan successful.
options.D: Good things free coffee will bring to Flora Café.

questions.passage:
Josh: Do you know about "pay it forward coffee" at Flora Café?
Eric: You mean you pay for a cup of coffee for someone poor to have it later?
Josh: Yeah. A cup of coffee is not much, but on cold winter days like today, it might warm them up a little. And maybe their hearts too.
Eric: But will Flora Café really give this cup of coffee to someone later?
Josh: Come on. I've known the shopkeeper well. He's an honest man. He's got a blackboard in the shop that says how many cups are paid for and how many have been given out.
Eric: But how will they know who to give? Anyone can ask for it, even if they're not poor.
Josh: True, but then I guess they'll just have to believe in people.
Eric: But will poor people go and ask for a free coffee? Won't they worry about losing face?
Josh: Why do you always say things like that?
Eric: Well, it may happen.
Josh: Yeah, I know, but I still think it's a good thing to do, and it gets people to care about others.
questions.explain: Eric 一直提出這個計畫可能會有的各種問題（誰該拿到咖啡、窮人會不會不好意思去要），things like that 指的就是這些「問題」，所以答案選 A。

questions.stem: What can we learn about Josh and Eric?

options.A: Josh was not able to change Eric's mind about free coffee.
options.B: Eric has more hope for the free coffee plan than Josh does.
options.C: They found a way to make people feel OK to take free coffee.
options.D: They do not know whether Flora Café will do business honestly.

questions.passage:

questions.explain: Josh 一直支持這個活動，Eric 卻一直質疑，到最後 Eric 還是說「這件事可能會發生」，可以看出 Josh 沒有說服 Eric 改變想法，所以答案選 A。

questions.stem: What do we know about Flora Café from the dialogue?

options.A: It has a way to know who really is poor.
options.B: Free coffee has brought it more business.
options.C: People pay less for free coffee than other coffee.
options.D: Its blackboard shows if there are free coffees to be taken.

questions.explain: Josh 說店裡有一塊黑板，寫著「已經有多少杯被買走、已經送出多少杯」，可以知道這塊黑板是用來顯示「還有沒有免費咖啡可以領」，所以答案選 D。

questions.stem:
Below are the writer's points in the reading:
   a. Whether we should stop tribespeople whaling
   b. The problem of whaling
   c. Whaling as a way of life
In what order does the writer talk about his points?
questions.passage:
Over the years, the number of whales has dropped sharply. From 1946 to 1986, about 340,000 whales were killed. People worry that children in the future can only see whales in pictures. They believe all kinds of whaling (whale hunting) should be stopped before it's too late.
      Some people are trying to stop whaling all over the world. But they fail to notice one fact: Whaling was going on for a long time before the number of whales went down and became a problem.
      Whaling started as early as 1,500 years ago. This was how tribespeople fed their families. They hunted whales for meat because almost nothing could grow on their land. They also made whale fat into oil and used it to make candles or oil lamps. Over the years, whaling became their way of living, and even part of who they are.
      Tribe whaling is not the thing we should worry about. Of all the whales that were killed over the past forty years, only 10% were hunted by tribespeople. The other 90% died at the hands of the money-making whaling business. When we try to stop all kinds of whaling, we should think what we are asking tribespeople to give up and whether this is the best answer to the problem of whaling.
(tribe：部落 fat：脂肪)
questions.explain: 文章先講捕鯨造成鯨魚變少的問題(b)，接著講捕鯨其實是部落原本的生活方式(c)，最後才討論到底該不該阻止部落捕鯨(a)，順序是 b→c→a，所以答案選 B。

questions.stem: Which idea may the writer agree with?

options.A: We should stop all kinds of whale hunting.
options.B: We should not worry about the number of whales.
options.C: Tribespeople's way of living is as important as animal lives.
options.D: Working with the whaling business can make tribespeople's lives better.

questions.passage:

They also made whale fat into oil and used it to make candles or oil lamps. Over the years, whaling became their way of living, and even part of who they are.
      Tribe whaling is not the thing we should worry about. Of all the whales that were killed over the past forty years, only 10% were hunted by tribespeople. The other 90% died at the hands of the money-making whaling business. When we try to stop all kinds of whaling, we should think what we are asking tribespeople to give up and whether this is the best answer to the problem of whaling.
(tribe：部落 fat：脂肪)
questions.explain: 作者一直在幫部落捕鯨說話，提醒大家如果禁止的話，部落原本的生活方式也會跟著消失，暗示部落的生活方式跟動物的生命一樣重要，所以答案選 C。

questions.stem: What can we learn from the reading?

options.A: Tribespeople believe whales bring good luck.
options.B: Tribespeople become rich by selling whale oil.
options.C: Whaling was not a problem until 1,500 years ago.
options.D: Whaling helped tribespeople's lives in different ways.

When we try to stop all kinds of whaling, we should think what we are asking tribespeople to give up and whether this is the best answer to the problem of whaling.

questions.explain: 文章說部落捕鯨是為了取肉養家，鯨魚的油脂還可以拿來做蠟燭、點油燈，可以看出捕鯨在很多方面幫助了部落的生活，所以答案選 D。

questions.stem: From the news story, which fact is seen as a new turn?

options.A: Carolyn Cole is a first-time writer.
options.B: Carolyn Cole won the Inscriber Prize.
options.C: Carolyn Cole is not the winner's real name.
options.D: Carolyn Cole did not appear for the Inscriber Prize.

questions.passage:
NBN NEWS
         Ryan Kim 7/15/2013
         Guesses about Carolyn Cole have been flying around since last week, and now things have taken a new turn.
         Last week Carolyn Cole won the Inscriber Prize, the country's biggest book prize, with her book Paper Soldier. Cole herself did not show up for the prize. It was not only the first time in history that a first-time writer won the prize, but also the first time that the winner did not appear.
         Now here comes another "first time" for the Inscriber: Cole is not real. "She" is in fact Thomas Goode. Goode is known for his picture books. His best-known book, Blue Moon, won him two national book prizes and sold over one million copies.
         Goode had tried to write "serious" books before. His first try was Parent, which came out in 2006. Sadly, Goode's big name did not help, and there was little interest in Parent. Goode's friend told NBN that he wanted to write a book under a different name. Goode thought that people might treat his book seriously if they did not know it was from a picture book writer.
         For the moment, there has been no word from Goode yet.
questions.explain: 新聞裡最新的轉折是「發現 Carolyn Cole 根本不是真的人，其實是 Thomas Goode」，也就是得獎的名字不是真名，所以答案選 C。

questions.stem: Which is most likely true about the Inscriber Prize?

options.A: It was started by NBN News.
options.B: It is a new prize for picture books.
options.C: It is a prize only for first-time writers.
options.D: It is treated seriously by writers in the country.

questions.passage:
NBN NEWS

         Guesses about Carolyn Cole have been flying around since last week, and now things have taken a new turn.

It was not only the first time in history that a first-time writer won the prize, but also the first time that the winner did not appear.

Goode's friend told NBN that he wanted to write a book under a different name. Goode thought that people might treat his book seriously if they did not know it was from a picture book writer.
         For the moment, there has been no word from Goode yet.
questions.explain: 文章說 Inscriber Prize 是這個國家最大的圖書獎，Goode 也希望用這個獎讓自己的作品被認真看待，可以知道這個獎在作家心中很重要，所以答案選 D。

questions.stem: What can we learn about Thomas Goode?

options.A: He has won the Inscriber Prize twice.
options.B: He told NBN that Paper Soldier was his work.
options.C: He wants to be more than just a picture book writer.
options.D: His life as a picture book writer has not been successful.

questions.passage:
NBN NEWS
         Ryan Kim 7/15/2013
         Guesses about Carolyn Cole have been flying around since last week, and now things have taken a new turn.

Goode's friend told NBN that he wanted to write a book under a different name. Goode thought that people might treat his book seriously if they did not know it was from a picture book writer.
         For the moment, there has been no word from Goode yet.
questions.explain: Goode 以前就嘗試過寫「嚴肅」的作品，也希望作品能被認真對待，可以看出他不想只當一個繪本作家，所以答案選 C。

questions.stem: What do we know about Thomas Goode's books?

options.A: The sales of Parent were bad.
options.B: Paper Soldier is his best-selling book.
options.C: His first picture book came out in 2006.
options.D: Blue Moon was written under a different name.

questions.passage:
NBN NEWS
         Ryan Kim 7/15/2013
         Guesses about Carolyn Cole have been flying around since last week, and now things have taken a new turn.
         Last week Carolyn Cole won the Inscriber Prize, the country's biggest book prize, with her book Paper Soldier. Cole herself did not show up for the prize. It was not only the first time in history that a first-time writer won the prize, but also the first time that the winner did not appear.
         Now here comes another "first time" for the Inscriber: Cole is not real. "She" is in fact Thomas Goode. Goode is known for his picture books. His best-known book, Blue Moon, won him two national book prizes and sold over one million copies.
         Goode had tried to write "serious" books before. His first try was Parent, which came out in 2006. Sadly, Goode's big name did not help, and there was little interest in Parent. Goode's friend told NBN that he wanted to write a book under a different name. Goode thought that people might treat his book seriously if they did not know it was from a picture book writer.
         For the moment, there has been no word from Goode yet.
questions.explain: 文章說《Parent》這本書「乏人問津」，代表這本書賣得不好，所以答案選 A。

questions.stem: My sister wants to buy a car ______ she doesn't have enough money.
questions.explain: 姐姐想買車，可是她錢不夠，「但是」的英文是 but，所以答案選 but。（想買車 vs 錢不夠，這兩件事是相反的，中間要用 but 連起來）

questions.stem: Jim always forgets my birthday. He has asked me many times and still can't ______ it.
questions.explain: 這句話說 Jim 老是忘記我的生日，問了好多次還是「記不住」。remember 是「記得」的意思，所以答案選 remember。

questions.stem: The little girl was ______ by a truck on her way home and was badly hurt.
questions.explain: 這個小女孩回家的路上被卡車「撞到」，傷得很重。hit 是「撞」的意思，所以答案選 hit。

questions.stem: Excuse me. I really need to go now. My kids ______ for me at school.
questions.explain: 這句話說「我小孩現在在學校等我」，因為講的是現在正在發生的事，動詞要用「be 動詞 + ing」的形式，像 are waiting（正在等），所以答案選 are waiting。

questions.stem: Amy's father is very sick. The doctors can do nothing for him, so Amy went to the temple to ______ for him.
questions.explain: Amy 的爸爸病得很重，醫生也沒辦法，所以 Amy 去廟裡「祈禱」。pray 就是祈禱的意思，pray for someone 表示為某人祈禱，所以答案選 pray。

questions.stem: I'm glad that my school has students wear ______. I don't have to worry about what clothes to wear to school.
questions.explain: 後面說「不用煩惱要穿什麼衣服上學」，因為學校規定大家都穿一樣的「制服」，uniforms 就是制服，所以答案選 uniforms。

questions.stem: We are surprised that Sally and Ray decided to get married. No one knows ______. They often fight with each other and are unhappy together.
questions.explain: Sally 跟 Ray 常常吵架，感情不好，卻決定要結婚，大家都覺得奇怪，不知道「為什麼」。why 就是「為什麼」，所以答案選 why。

questions.stem: I don't think Katie will read any one of the books you've picked out for her: ______ look(s) very boring.
questions.explain: 這句話在說「你挑給 Katie 的書，她應該一本都不會看」，因為前面提到好幾本書（一個以上），後面動詞用的是沒有加 s 的 look，所以要用代表「他們（那些書）」的 they，所以答案選 they。

questions.stem: Jenny's bag is very heavy because ______ filled with toy cars.
questions.explain: Jenny 的書包很重，因為書包裡「裝滿」了玩具車。書包只有一個，所以要用 it is filled with（它裝滿了），所以答案選 it is。

questions.stem: ______ a map with you when you go to a place for the first time.
questions.explain: 這句話在教人「第一次去一個地方時，要帶一張地圖」。像這種叫別人做什麼事的句子，開頭要直接用動詞原形，像 Take（帶）、Go（去）這樣，所以答案選 Take。

questions.stem: The weather ______ rainy and cloudy in the last few days. I hope the sun will come out soon.
questions.explain: 這句話說「最近幾天天氣一直是又下雨又多雲」，從過去一直到現在的事情，英文會用「have/has + 過去式動詞（been、gone 這種）」來表示，像 has been rainy，所以答案選 has been。

questions.stem: All the excuses Nick made ______ a lot about how much he hates to do the job.
questions.explain: 這句話在說「尼克找的所有藉口，都透露出他有多討厭做這件事」。All the excuses（所有的藉口）是這句話真正的主詞，需要一個主要的動詞，因為藉口是很多個，所以動詞不加 s，用 say，所以答案選 say。

questions.stem: Sabine walks so ______ in the apartment that sometimes you don't even know she's just passed by. You wonder if her feet ever touch the floor.
questions.explain: Sabine 走路走得好「輕」，輕到你有時候根本不知道她剛剛走過去，好像腳沒有碰到地板一樣。lightly 就是「輕輕地」的意思，所以答案選 lightly。

questions.stem: In the past, people thought it was a ______ idea to send e-mails and read news online, but now it is part of our everyday life.
questions.explain: 以前的人覺得寄電子郵件、上網看新聞是很「新奇（新鮮）」的事，但現在已經變成生活的一部分了。fresh 這裡是「新鮮、新奇」的意思，所以答案選 fresh。

questions.stem: What is the book festival for?

options.B: Telling people how to choose books.
options.C: Inviting people to change books with each other.
options.D: Knowing what kinds of books are the most popular.

questions.passage:
Do you have some books you don't want? Your old love may be someone's new favorite. Bring one book to the Town Library & get another one home for free!
When & How:
March 2 - March 14   Bring one book for one book festival card.
March 16 - March 31   Bring one card for one book you like to read.
What books:
All kinds of books EXCEPT school books, comic books, and dictionaries.
Notice:
Your book must be in good shape, without any page missing.
Your book must be clean, and nothing is written on it.
Want to know more? Please call 1234-5678. (We are closed on Mondays.)
-Town Library-
questions.explain: 圖書館海報上寫著「帶一本書來，就能免費帶另一本書回家」，重點是讓大家互相「交換」書，所以答案選 C。

questions.stem: Sonja is busy packing some books for the book festival. Which is most likely one of them?

options.B: A book of short stories in English.
options.C: A picture book with her own notes.
options.D: A workbook she used in her math class.

questions.passage:
Do you have some books you don't want? Your old love may be someone's new favorite. Bring one book to the Town Library & get another one home for free!
When & How:
March 2 - March 14   Bring one book for one book festival card.

Notice:
Your book must be in good shape, without any page missing.
Your book must be clean, and nothing is written on it.
Want to know more? Please call 1234-5678. (We are closed on Mondays.)
-Town Library-
questions.explain: 活動規則說「學校用書、漫畫、字典」不能拿來換，而且書上不能有寫過的字跡。A（醫學字典）、C（有寫筆記的圖畫書）、D（數學習作）都違反規定，只有 B（英文短篇故事書）符合，所以答案選 B。

questions.stem: What happened in the story?

options.A: A killer was caught by the police.
options.B: The writer's family invited a stranger to dinner.
options.C: The writer's brother ran to their parents for help.
options.D: A man ran away after he heard the writer's mother's voice.

questions.passage:
I had a horrendous experience last Saturday. That day, my family went camping near a big lake. While my parents were preparing dinner, my brother and I were playing by the lake. A dirty man appeared from somewhere, and his face was half covered by his hair. He walked to us and asked for some water.
When I gave him water, the bag he carried dropped, and things inside fell out on the grass. I saw a rope, a knife, and a baseball bat. The strangest thing was that there were also a woman's shoe and a ring, and I'm sure they were not his. The man quickly put all his things back in the bag and looked at us angrily. At that moment, the picture of a man the police was looking for came to my mind. He was the crazy killer! I was so afraid that I could not move at all.
Luckily, before he could get any closer, my mom shouted from far away, and the man hurried off into the dark.
questions.explain: 故事最後說「媽媽在遠遠的地方大喊一聲，那個男人就匆匆忙忙跑進黑暗裡逃走了」，所以答案選 D。

questions.stem: What can we learn about the writer and the man with a bag?

options.B: The writer followed the man into the dark.
options.C: The writer did not find out who the man was in the end.
options.D: The man did not want the writer to see things in his bag.

questions.explain: 袋子裡的東西掉出來以後，這個男人很快把東西收回去，還生氣地瞪著他們，可以看出他不希望被別人看到袋子裡的東西，所以答案選 D。

questions.stem: How do people feel when they experience something horrendous?

questions.explain: 作者說自己嚇到完全動不了，可以知道 horrendous（很可怕的）經驗會讓人覺得「害怕」（scared），所以答案選 C。

questions.stem: What does the catch mean in the dialogue?

options.A: A plan that starts well but ends badly.
options.B: A fact that everyone knows but never talks about.
options.C: A problem that is hidden behind something good.
options.D: An action that is right in one way but wrong in another.

questions.passage:
Ruth: I've got two tickets for a football game. The Lions vs. the Sharks, front-row seats, next Saturday. Interested? You can take Jessica to the game.
Nate: How did you get the tickets?! They were all sold out months ago!
Ruth: And a table for two at Leeds.
Nate: Leeds?! But I called them yesterday and they said they were all booked for the year!
Ruth: Now it's yours. You and Jessica can have a lovely dinner there after the game.
Nate: Wait a second. This is just not like you. What's the catch?
Ruth: Well, you know this Friday I'm leaving for my vacation in London, and . . . .
Nate: And what?
Ruth: And I was thinking if Charlie could stay with you next week.
Nate: The whole week?!
Ruth: Please! I can't just leave him in my apartment.
Nate: You know I hate that stupid dog! And he hates me too.
Ruth: Please! You're my last hope.
Nate: I need to think about it.
Ruth: OK, but not too long.
questions.explain: Ruth 先給 Nate 好處（球票、餐廳位子），才提出要 Nate 幫忙顧狗的請求。catch 在這裡的意思是「藏在好事背後的問題」，所以答案選 C。

questions.stem: What do we know from the dialogue?

options.A: Ruth will go to London next Saturday.
options.B: Ruth had trouble booking a hotel room in London.
options.C: Nate is the first person that Ruth thought of for help.
options.D: Nate is surprised that Ruth was able to book a table at Leeds.

questions.stem: What is this reading for?

options.A: Telling people rules about hospital visits.
options.B: Getting people to stand up and speak for doctors.
options.C: Telling people how to find a good doctor for themselves.
options.D: Getting doctors to share their experiences in saving lives.

questions.passage:
When you need to see a doctor, surely you will want to see a good one. But there is one important thing you may never have thought about: Has your doctor rested enough?
Doctors in our country often have to work 34 hours or longer without sleep. Most of them work 104 hours a week, and some even 150. (There are only 168 hours a week!) One report said that a doctor who works such long hours cannot think more clearly than a drunk driver. Another found that doctors who sleep 5.8 fewer hours than the week before might make 22% more serious mistakes. Surely no one wants to get in a car that is driven by a drunk driver. So, how safe would you feel when you are under the care of a tired doctor?
Sadly this problem has been around for a long time. There are no laws about it yet and hospital bosses don't look like they care either. And that is why we doctors are asking you to join us on the street this Sunday. Fight together with us on the street so we can fight better for your life in the hospital.
Time: 1 p.m. May 2nd    Place: City Hospital Square
questions.explain: 文章最後呼籲大家星期天跟醫生一起上街，希望大家一起替醫生發聲，所以答案選 B。

questions.stem: What does this problem mean in the reading?

options.C: Doctors do not see enough people a day.
options.D: Doctors are not paid enough for their work.

questions.explain: 整篇文章都在說醫生常常要工作超過 34 小時、一星期工作上百小時，this problem（這個問題）指的就是「工時太長」，所以答案選 A。

questions.stem: What does Wonky Markt care about?

options.A: Supermarkets often sell "seconds" at a lower price.
options.B: Farmers don't know how to grow fewer "seconds."
options.C: "Seconds" are thrown away because of how they look.
options.D: People don't know much about picking fresh "seconds."

questions.passage:
Have you ever seen a pumpkin that looks like a nose or an apple that looks like a funny face? They may not look great, but they taste just as good as the pretty ones. These strange-looking fruit and vegetables are called "seconds." About 20-40% of what farmers grow is not so beautiful. Sadly, these "seconds" seldom arrive at your table, but often go straight into the trash can. Why? It's because supermarkets don't want to buy them from farmers-they believe their shoppers only love beautiful things. Because of these "seconds," some farmers lose a lot of money.
Wonky Markt wishes to change this. We're helping sell these "seconds." Farmers can save them from the trash can, and you can get them at a lower price.
1. NT$250 a box.
2. You can choose as many as six kinds of fruit and vegetables a box.
3. Service charge: NT$50 a box.
4. No service charge if order is over NT$1,000.
Send us photos of your delicious dishes that are made with our "seconds" to oneandonly@wonkymarkt.com, and you may win our cookbook.
questions.explain: 文章說長得醜的水果蔬菜常常因為「長相不好看」就被丟掉，農夫也因此賠錢，Wonky Markt 想要改變這件事，所以答案選 C。

questions.stem: What do we know about buying the "seconds" from Wonky Markt?

options.A: You can save 20-40% if you shop for a second time.
options.B: If you buy three boxes, you will need to pay NT$900.
options.C: You can choose not only their size but also their shape.
options.D: To win the cookbook, you need to buy at least six boxes.

questions.explain: 一箱要 250 元，三箱就是 750 元，因為還沒超過 1000 元，所以每箱要多加 50 元的服務費，三箱就是 150 元，加起來 750+150=900 元，所以答案選 B。

questions.stem: What can we learn from the reading?

options.A: No buses go from Apple City to Orange Island before the bus line changes.
options.B: Before July 1, those who want to visit Mango Island have to take a bus at Berry City.
options.C: Traveling from Apple City to Grape Town will take less time after the bus line changes.
options.D: Before July 1, Dashing Shuttle does not have buses from Apple City to Peach City.

questions.passage:
Below is the news about Dashing Shuttle's bus line changes.
1. Great news for people who travel between Apple City and Peach City! From July 1, we have three more buses to Peach City every day.
2. A trip from Apple City to Lemon City or Berry City will take less time. From July 1, you can go from Apple City to each of the two cities without changing buses.
3. Dashing Shuttle is the first in the country to open a bus line between Apple City and Orange Island. From July 1, our bus will take you to Orange Island every day!
4. Our bus line between Apple City and Grape Town will stop running on June 30. From July 1, changing buses at Guava Town or Banana Town will be the best way to travel.
5. From July 1, our bus runs from Apple City to Mango Island three times a day. You don't need to change buses at Melon City anymore.
questions.explain: 第 3 點說「從 7 月 1 日起，我們第一次開通蘋果市到橙島的路線」，可以知道改變之前根本沒有這條路線，所以答案選 A。

questions.stem: What does the report say about globalization's influence on the movie world?

options.A: It has changed the way of making movies.
options.B: It has changed a country's domestic market for movies.
options.C: It has brought together movie makers from around the world.
options.D: It has helped US movies make more money abroad than in the US.

questions.passage:
Cinema Times, June 5th, 2005
Thanks to globalization, we get to see movies from around the world. If anyone should feel most excited about globalization, it is US movie makers. A study in 2001 shows that, for every 100 people who go to the theater, 85 see US movies. This may be good news for US movie makers, but not for those in other countries.
Chart 1 shows the percentages (%) of US movies in five countries in 1984 and in 2001; Chart 2 shows the percentages (%) of domestic movies in the five countries in 1984 and in 2001. In Chart 1, we can see that US movies have been popular in foreign markets. In Chart 2, we can see that domestic movies in the five countries are not doing well in their domestic market. The falling market for domestic movies may be a serious problem, but there is something more important. When people welcome US movies with open arms, they in fact also say yes to the American way of thinking. Some people in these countries worry that what is lost may not just be their domestic market for domestic movies, but also their own way of thinking that has been passed down from parents to children.
questions.explain: 報導用圖表說明，全球化讓很多國家自己國內拍的電影，在自己國家反而賣得不好，也就是改變了一個國家電影的本地市場，所以答案選 B。

questions.stem: What can we learn from the report?

options.A: The American way of thinking may be changed when it is brought to other countries.
options.B: More and more people are asking their country to help their domestic movie business.
options.C: US movies are popular in foreign markets because English is spoken in many countries.
options.D: The fact that US movies are popular may mean the American way of thinking is popular.

questions.stem: He ______ a proud businessman of a successful shaved ice shop.
questions.passage:
Jerry Stevens has been unhappy these months. He   34   a proud businessman of a successful shaved ice shop. His highest sales numbers were 1,899 plates a week; people called him "Ice King." But all this changed when the cookie shop across the street started selling ice cream cookies. It took away half of his business.
And   35   when Dan, his dead sister's only son, came to work for him.
Dan was a nice young man with a simple mind, too simple, maybe. He always let people try some shaved ice for free, but he never learned to see when "some" became "too much." Many people came, but few bought. When the sales did not grow with the heat, Jerry started to worry. He wanted to send Dan home. But how could he? Dan had no family except him.
Then, one day,   36  . And it was from across the street. The cookie shop   37   a clerk. For Jerry, it was a great chance to get Dan out of his store without sending him home. After taking the shopkeeper to several nice dinners, Jerry got Dan the job.
Now Jerry could finally try and save his store. And maybe Dan would help by sending him a few people who are thirsty from eating too many cookies.
questions.explain: 後面提到對街的餅乾店賣冰淇淋餅乾以後，情況就變了，可以知道他「以前曾經是」風光的老闆，used to be 就是「以前曾經是」的意思，所以答案選 C。

options.B: he was ready to give up
options.C: the sales of shaved ice finally started to rise

questions.explain: 這句話在說「當時餅乾店正在找一個店員」，講的是過去正在進行的事情，動詞要用「was + ing」的形式，像 was looking for，所以答案選 C。

questions.stem: The first one made him a star; the second ______.
questions.passage:
Englishman Robert Scott is known for leading two trips to Antarctica. The first one made him a star; the second   38  .
After his first successful trip, Scott decided to be the first person to stand on the South Pole. However,   39  . He would be in a race with Roald Amundsen, from Norway. Both left their countries by ship in June of 1910 and arrived in Antarctica in January of 1911.
About ten months later, both teams started their trips down to the South Pole.   40  . Amundsen's team used dogs, and Scott's team used horses. Because horses weren't good at traveling on snow, it took Scott's team 77 days to arrive at the South Pole. They got there on January 17, 1912, and were surprised to find that Amundsen was ahead of them. The news hit Scott very hard, but what he didn't know was that   41  . His team began their long trip home with broken hearts. After days of terrible weather and little food, Scott lost his men one after another, and he himself was the last one to meet the end of his life. No one on his team lived to go back home and tell their story. It was only learned through Scott's diary.
questions.explain: 後面說 Scott 一行人在回程中一個接一個過世，他自己也走到了生命的盡頭，可以知道第二趟旅程讓他「賠上了性命」，所以答案選 C。

questions.stem: After his first successful trip, Scott decided to be the first person to stand on the South Pole. However, ______.

options.A: he didn't want to take this trip with others
options.B: he wasn't the only one who had this dream
options.C: he wouldn't let anyone else take his prize away
options.D: he couldn't miss the chance to make his country proud

questions.passage:

After his first successful trip, Scott decided to be the first person to stand on the South Pole. However,   39  . He would be in a race with Roald Amundsen, from Norway. Both left their countries by ship in June of 1910 and arrived in Antarctica in January of 1911.
About ten months later, both teams started their trips down to the South Pole.   40  . Amundsen's team used dogs, and Scott's team used horses. Because horses weren't good at traveling on snow, it took Scott's team 77 days to arrive at the South Pole. They got there on January 17, 1912, and were surprised to find that Amundsen was ahead of them. The news hit Scott very hard, but what he didn't know was that   41  . After days of terrible weather and little food, Scott lost his men one after another, and he himself was the last one to meet the end of his life. No one on his team lived to go back home and tell their story. It was only learned through Scott's diary.
questions.explain: 下一句說他要跟挪威的 Amundsen 一起競賽，可以知道不是只有他一個人想當第一個到南極的人，所以答案選 B。

questions.stem: About ten months later, both teams started their trips down to the South Pole. ______. Amundsen's team used dogs, and Scott's team used horses.

options.A: And Scott used a popular way for his men to travel fast
options.B: But there were problems to deal with before they traveled
options.C: But Amundsen didn't know better about animals than Scott
options.D: And their ways of traveling decided which team would win

questions.explain: 後面對比兩隊用的交通工具（狗 vs 馬），而且因為馬不擅長在雪地走路，讓 Scott 隊落後了，可以知道「用什麼方式移動」決定了誰會贏，所以答案選 D。

questions.stem: The news hit Scott very hard, but what he didn't know was that ______.

options.C: the worst had not come yet

questions.passage:

After his first successful trip, Scott decided to be the first person to stand on the South Pole. However,   39  . He would be in a race with Roald Amundsen, from Norway. Both left their countries by ship in June of 1910 and arrived in Antarctica in January of 1911.
About ten months later, both teams started their trips down to the South Pole.   40  . Amundsen's team used dogs, and Scott's team used horses. Because horses weren't good at traveling on snow, it took Scott's team 77 days to arrive at the South Pole. They got there on January 17, 1912, and were surprised to find that Amundsen was ahead of them. The news hit Scott very hard, but what he didn't know was that   41  . His team began their long trip home with broken hearts. After days of terrible weather and little food, Scott lost his men one after another, and he himself was the last one to meet the end of his life. No one on his team lived to go back home and tell their story. It was only learned through Scott's diary.
questions.explain: 後面說他們回程遇到很糟的天氣、糧食又不夠，隊員一個接一個過世，可以知道「最糟的事情其實還沒發生」，所以答案選 C。

questions.stem:
Chris: Which ______ looks better on me?
Penny: I think purple's better. You look great in purple.
questions.explain: 這句話問「哪一個______比較適合我？」，回答說「我覺得紫色比較好看，你穿紫色很好看。」既然回答講的是「紫色」，那問題問的一定是「顏色」，所以選 A（color 顏色）。grade 年級、shape 形狀、size 尺寸都跟顏色沒關係。

questions.stem: My cat got excited when it saw the boy ______ the birds.
questions.explain: 這句話說：我的貓看到那個男孩在抓鳥，就變得很興奮。用眼睛看到別人「正在做」某件事，英文句型是 see + 人 + 動詞加ing，像 see the boy catching（看到男孩正在抓），所以選 catching。catches、to catch、caught 用法都不對。

questions.stem: Mr. Jackson is a very ______ writer; people of all ages enjoy his stories.
questions.explain: 這句話說：Jackson 先生是個很______的作家，各年齡層的人都愛看他的故事。「各種年齡的人都喜歡」表示他很受歡迎，所以選 popular（受歡迎的）。polite 有禮貌、handsome 英俊、honest 誠實，都跟「大家愛看他的故事」對不上。

questions.stem: Carol sounded happy when we talked on the phone; I could feel joy in her ______ .
questions.explain: 這句話說：講電話時 Carol 聽起來很開心，我可以從她的______感覺到快樂。講電話只能聽到聲音，看不到表情，所以感覺到快樂的地方是「聲音（voice）」，選 D。

questions.stem: Mark studies very hard and never ______ classes. He goes to school even when he is sick.
questions.explain: 這句話說：Mark 很用功讀書，從不______，就算生病也去上學。「就算生病也去上學」就是「從不缺課」，英文「缺課」的固定說法是 miss classes，所以選 misses。

questions.stem: Have you got anything for Joe ______ ? He'd be happy to get your gift on his birthday.
questions.explain: 這句話問：你有沒有幫 Joe 準備禮物了______？他生日收到禮物一定很開心。「你有沒有做某件事」這種問句，常常會加 yet（到現在為止、還……嗎），像 Have you got anything yet（你到現在準備好了嗎），所以選 D。

questions.stem: Susan bought ______ bread in the supermarket, but she did not buy anything to drink.
questions.explain: 這句話說：Susan 在超市買了______麵包，但沒買任何飲料。麵包（bread）是像水一樣沒辦法「一個一個」數的東西，肯定句裡說「買了一些」用 some，所以選 B。many 要接看得到一個一個數的東西、any 通常用在否定句或問句、one 要接可以數的單數東西，都不合適。

questions.stem: Playing sports at least three times a week ______ good for your health.
questions.explain: 這句話說：每週至少運動三次______對健康有益。「每週運動三次」整件事被當成一件事來看，是單數的概念，配的動詞要用 is，所以選 A。

questions.stem: My parents have different hobbies. One enjoys baking; ______ enjoys taking pictures.
questions.explain: 這句話說：我爸媽有不同的興趣。一個喜歡烘焙；______喜歡拍照。爸媽總共兩個人，講完「一個」之後剩下的最後一個，英文用 the other（剩下的那一個），所以選 C。

questions.stem: My mom told me to take care of ______ in England. She worried that I couldn't eat or sleep well there.
questions.explain: 這句話說：媽媽叫我在英國要照顧好______。她擔心我在那裡吃不好睡不好。「叫我（me）照顧的人」其實就是「我自己」，主詞和受詞是同一個人時，要用「自己（myself）」，所以選 B。

questions.stem: The paint on the wall is not as ______ as it was ten years ago. It has changed from white to gray.
questions.explain: 這句話說：牆上的油漆已經不像十年前那麼______了，已經從白色變成灰色。顏色從白變灰，就是變得比較不「亮（bright）」了，所以選 A。

questions.stem: I told you this road went the wrong way, but you just wouldn't listen. Now we have to ______ and take another road.
questions.explain: 這句話說：我早就告訴你這條路走錯了，你就是不聽。現在我們得______，改走另一條路。走錯路要「掉頭回去」，英文是 turn back，所以選 D。

questions.stem: The last five years have not been ______ to Jennie. Her face is covered with lines and she looks much older than she is.
questions.explain: 這句話說：過去五年對 Jennie 並不______。她臉上滿是皺紋，看起來比實際年齡老很多。be kind to someone 是「對某人好」的意思。這裡是說「歲月沒有善待她」，所以老得比較快，選 A。

questions.stem: My dog Jimmy loves ______ with a comb. Every time I comb his hair, he will close his eyes and fall asleep.
questions.explain: 這句話說：我的狗 Jimmy 很喜歡______梳子梳毛。每次我幫牠梳毛，牠都會閉上眼睛睡著。狗狗是「被梳毛的那一方」，不是自己動手梳，所以要用「被……」的說法，love 後面接 being brushed（喜歡被梳理），選 D。

questions.stem: What is the reading for?

options.A: Finding out why the old street was on fire.
options.B: Inviting people to do business on the old street.
options.C: Getting people to find ways to save the old street.
options.D: Asking for help for people who got hurt in the fire.

questions.explain: 這篇公告說 Pinky 老街差點被大火燒毀，呼籲大家「一起想辦法救回老 Pinky」，還說週六要開會，歡迎大家一起幫忙。目的是號召大家一起想辦法挽救老街，所以選 C。
questions.passage:
Our Pinky Street, one of the oldest streets in the world, was almost lost to a big fire last night. Most of the old houses on Pinky were burned down. The living history we are proud of is dying. But we should never give up. Pinky is like a parent, always there caring for our town. Now we must do something together to get the old Pinky back. There will be a meeting at 2:00 this Saturday afternoon at Town Office. Anyone who wants to help is welcome. Let's see what we can do.
                                        Adam Bolton, Town Office
                                        March 9

questions.stem: Which is NOT used in the reading to talk about the old street?
questions.explain: 文章裡把老街比喻成「老 Pinky」「像父母一樣」「活的歷史」，但完全沒有用「好朋友」來形容，所以選 A。

                                        Adam Bolton, Town Office
                                        March 9

questions.stem: What is FunGana 2016 for?
questions.explain: 文章介紹 FunGana 活動多年來帶大家去 Gana 各地玩，今年要帶大家去 Piso 島，體驗看鯨魚、泥漿溫泉等等。這是在幫大家體驗 Gana 這個地方，所以選 D。
questions.passage:
No idea where to spend your vacation? If you are a big fan of sun, beach, and water sports, then you must join FunGana 2016!
Over the years, FunGana has taken people to many places in Gana. This year, we are taking you to Piso Island, one of Gana's beautiful islands. Piso Island is adjacent to Wako Island, the biggest island in Gana, so it is only 15 minutes away by boat from Wako Island. The best time to visit? All year around, the weather on Piso Island is just wonderful for a vacation. You can sail out to watch whales, or enjoy one of the best mud springs in the world. We will also show you around the island in a special car, the Cocoon Rider. It's our pleasure to help you enjoy your stay on Piso Island.
Find out more about FunGana 2016 at http://www.fungana2016.com.gn.

questions.stem: What does the reading say about Piso Island?

options.A: It makes the best cars in Gana.
options.B: It has nice weather in every season.
options.C: It is the most popular island in Gana.
options.D: It has the most mud springs in the world.

questions.explain: 文章說「一年四季，Piso 島的天氣都很棒，很適合渡假」，所以選 B。
questions.passage:

Find out more about FunGana 2016 at http://www.fungana2016.com.gn.

questions.stem: What does adjacent to mean?
questions.explain: 文章說 Piso 島「adjacent to」Wako 島，搭船只要 15 分鐘，可以猜出 adjacent to 的意思是「靠近」，所以選 B。

questions.stem: What do we know from the notice?

options.A: The summer school is three weeks long.
options.B: Students can order lunch from the school restaurant.
options.C: There are basketball classes in the gym every week except the third week.
options.D: Those who need to borrow dancing shoes must tell Mr. Shum the day before.

questions.explain: 公告說第三週體育館要辦高中球賽，所以那一週的籃球課要改到操場上。這代表除了第三週，其他週的籃球課都是在體育館上，所以選 C。餐廳暑期沒開、要自己帶午餐，跟 A、B、D 的說法不符。
questions.passage:
Here is the schedule with the notice of the summer school that Tina goes to.

SCHEDULE (課表)
Time / Day        Mon.      Tues.     Wed.      Thurs.      Fri.
09:30~10:30       English   Computer  English   English     Piano
10:40~11:40       Piano     English   Painting  Dancing     Dancing
13:30~14:30       Tennis    Painting  Tennis    Painting    Computer
14:40~15:40       Baseball  Baseball  Swimming  Basketball  Basketball

NOTICE
- The school restaurant is closed during the summer school (7/4~7/31). Please bring your own lunch. If you need to order lunch, please tell your class leader the day before.
- Dancing shoes can be borrowed with your summer school card. Please find Mr. Shum in Room 117.
- During the third week, the gym will be used for the High School Ball Games. Please go to the playground for the basketball classes of the week.

questions.stem:
Below is what Tina told her friends about the classes in her summer school schedule.

"Mr. Reed was really crazy to give us so many new words at one time. Who can possibly remember all of them in a night? In our art class, when Ms. Grant asked us to draw a picture, I just drew a mad me looking at Mr. Reed. But then I totally forgot about Mr. Reed after two hours of PE classes in the afternoon. I was just too tired to think! Luckily, there was no computer class today to make it a really bad day."

Which day was Tina talking about?
questions.explain: Tina 說那天英文課學了很多新單字、美術課要畫畫、下午上了兩堂體育性質的課、而且那天沒有電腦課。對照課表，只有星期三符合：有英文課、有畫畫課、下午是網球和游泳、而且沒有電腦課，所以選 C。

questions.stem: Which is most likely true about the speaker in the reading?

options.A: He thinks a black cat is hiding somewhere in the house.
options.B: He feels lonely because he is the only child in his family.
options.C: He is looking for something he left somewhere in the house.
options.D: He is afraid of being by himself when he feels ghosts around.

questions.explain: 這首詩裡，說話的人一直說「風一直吹、門一直開」，拜託 Lucy 陪他、用唱歌趕走「他們（鬼）」。這代表他很害怕自己一個人面對這些恐怖的聲音，所以選 D。
questions.passage:
The wind keeps blowing.
The door keeps opening.
Will you be with me, my dear Lucy,
When I look for candy
Down there in the dark, dark kitchen?

The wind keeps crying.
The door keeps shaking.
Will you be with me, my dear Lucy,
When I pick up my toy puppy
Out there on the high, high balcony?

Wish you could always be with me
And make them go away with your Do-Re-Me.
I remember last Halloween
They ran away when you started to sing,
"Dear Tommy, my little king,
Close your eyes and have a sweet dream."

So please stay with me and sing
To stop them kicking the door, riding the wind,
And pulling me down to their house under the ground.
They'll go away if you sing here for me,
In a voice that gets a black cat's hair standing,
The strongest legs shaking, and the window glass breaking.

(ideas from Matthew Sweeney's poem)

questions.stem: What can we learn about Lucy in the reading?

options.B: Her voice makes the speaker feel safe.
options.C: Her candy was hidden in the kitchen.
options.D: She was away from home last Halloween.

questions.explain: 詩裡說 Lucy 一唱歌，那些可怕的東西就會被趕走，說話的人也一直拜託 Lucy 陪他唱歌。這代表 Lucy 的聲音（歌聲）讓他覺得安心，所以選 B。

questions.stem: What does it in the first line mean?
questions.explain: 這句「你覺得它怎麼樣？」出現在對話一開始，後面 Lori 說「整個計畫都寫在紙上」「如果你照這個計畫做」，才知道原來大家在討論的是 Alec 寫好的約會計畫，所以 it 指的是 C。
questions.passage:
Alec: So, what do you think about it?
Ellie: It's…interesting.
Alec: Oh, no, don't say that.
Ellie: The first thing you should know about Olivia is that she's scared of anything with six legs. I don't think she'll be able to walk past the gate of Buzzing World.
Alec: But she loves butterflies! Well, she loved the photos of butterflies I took last time I was there.
Ellie: Only when they're not moving.
Alec: Fine. I'll just take her somewhere else, and that'd be OK. Right?
Ellie: Umm, you're taking her to Wavelength for dinner?
Alec: What's wrong with that?!
Ellie: I wouldn't say a dead fish is really her idea of a nice dinner, and this restaurant sells seafood…
Alec: Now you're wrong about this one. She loved my mom's fish balls. She had several last time!
Ellie: OK, let's ask someone else. Oh, there's Lori. Hey, Lori, could you look at Alec's plan? He's taking Olivia out.
Lori: Wow! Everything's written down on paper! Hmmm… I thought you wanted her to be your girlfriend.
Alec: I do!
Lori: If you follow this plan, I'm sure it'll be your only date with her.

questions.stem: What does this one mean in the dialogue?

options.B: Why Olivia does not like seafood.
options.D: What food Olivia does not like to eat.

questions.explain: Ellie 說 Olivia 不喜歡把死魚當晚餐，而且那間餐廳賣海鮮；Alec 回她「這件事你搞錯了」。「this one」指的就是「Olivia 不喜歡吃的食物（海鮮）」這件事，所以選 D。
